title: SQL-LS-001-001 List Learning spaces
author: RolandoVillavicencio013
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-001  **Name**: Learning spaces management  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow users to explore and manipulate data related to learning spaces. This includes the ability to list available spaces, view details of a specific space, update existing records, create new entries, and retrieve relevant information.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: List Learning spaces  As an administrator user I want to get a list of the Learning spaces in a building So that I can check the ones that are available to teach classes ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: There are registered learning spaces   Given there are available Learning spaces in a building   When I request a list of the Learning spaces   Then the system displays a list of available learning spaces with their respective identificators and types for each one ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: There aren't registered learning spaces   Given there aren't available learning spaces in a building   When I request a list of the learning spaces   Then the system displays a message that says "There aren't available learning spaces" ```  ```gherkin    ℹ️ It doesn't manage pagination. ```  ## Non-functional Requirements  | Category         | Description                                                                                                                                       | |------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | **Performance**   | The system must return the list of learning spaces in less than 0.2 seconds from request to full response.                                        | | **Reliability**   | The system must properly handle errors.       | | **Usability**     | The listing UI must clearly show identifier and type; and display a friendly message when no data is available.                |.                     | | **Maintainability** | Code must follow Clean Code principles, be organized according to DDD, fully documented, easy to maintain. | **Portability**   | The system should work correctly in different environments.                                                                   | | **Security**      | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0.                                                      | | **Auditability**  | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0.                                           |  ---  ## Technical Tasks  | Task                                                                                     | Minutes | |------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Validate listing requirements with PO (refinement)                                       | 20      | | Clone repository                                                                         | 3       | | Create feature branch                                                                    | 2       | | Review domain model for LearningSpace and Building relationship                          | 30      | | Define and code the listing service interface in the domain layer                        | 40      | | Implement list use case in the application layer                                         | 60      | | Implement repository method for listing learning spaces in the infrastructure layer      | 45      | | Update database context and entity mapping if necessary                                  | 25      | | Handle constraints related to building-learning space relationship                       | 45      | | Create GET /learning-spaces endpoint in controller                                       | 35      | | Configure routing and parameter binding                                                  | 20      | | Implement input validation and error handling for invalid building IDs or parameters     | 50      | | Define and implement response DTOs for success and error cases                           | 25      | | Build and compile project                                                                | 10      | | Troubleshoot and resolve build issues (if any)                                           | 30      | | Follow Clean Code and .NET 8 best practices                                              | 30      | | Align implementation with Clean Architecture and SOLID principles                        | 20      | | Document endpoint and response schema in Swagger (OpenAPI)                               | 20      | | Perform manual testing using Swagger UI                                                  | 25      | | Create pull request and address feedback from code reviewers                             | 75      | | Final acceptance criteria validation with PO                                             | 15      |  ---  ## ⏱️ Total Estimated Time  | Total estimate (minutes) | Total estimate (hours) | |--------------------------|------------------------| | 625                      | 10.41                   |  ## Performed Manual Tests  ### Test 1 – List with learning spaces in a building  * **Result:** **200 OK** with a JSON response containing multiple learning spaces. * **Validation:** The success scenario is fulfilled when there are learning spaces in a building; each record includes learning space ID, Floor ID and its type.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8cc8e207-36e1-4a41-936b-b88565c475b7)  ---  ### Test 2 – Empty list  * **Result:** **200 OK** with a empty list* * **Validation:** The scenario where there are no registered learning spaces is correctly handled; the response is clear and error-free.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b3b8b577-e0d1-4dc7-ac63-65f86ad42733)  ---  ### Test 3 – Try to list learning spaces in a nonexistent building  * **Result:** **404 Error** with the message: "The requested learning spaces list was not found". * **Validation:** The system successfully handled the error, displaying the expected behavior and the respective error message.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a1885b6c-b7ca-4aca-89f5-248439c4f5e9)  ---  ### Test 4 – Using a wrong format for search a learning space list  * **Result:** **409 Error** with the message: "Invalid building ID format. Expected Format: ´XX-Name. For example: IF-Anexo´". * **Validation:** The system successfully handled the error, displaying the expected behavior and the respective error message.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bd523add-a95c-4bcd-b9c3-a0ceeaaf818c)

title: CPD-LC-001-001 List components in a learning space
author: Fett-Ron
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-LC-001 **Name**: Learning components **Description**: This epic focuses on enhancing learning spaces within ThemePark@UCR, such as classrooms and laboratories, by adding interactive components like whiteboards and projectors. These components are designed to create an interactive and dynamic learning environment.   ---  ### User Story ```gherkin Feature: List of components of a learning space.  As a user I want to list the learning components So that I can view which components exist in a given learning space ``` ---  ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: Successfully listed the learning components in a learning space Given the learning space has one or more learning components When the user lists the learning components Then the system should display the list of all the components in that learning space And display them to the user ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Attempted to list the learning components of a learning space that has none Given the learning space has no learning components When the user lists the learning components Then the system should return an empty list And display an empty list ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Attempted to list the learning components of an invalid learning space  Given the learning space is invalid When the user lists the learning components Then the system should return an error  And display an error message ``` ---  ### Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs)  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | The system should retrieve and display list of components within 500ms under normal load conditions. | | **Scalability**  | The listing operation should maintain performance when multiple requests occur simultaneously. | | **Availability** | The listing functionality should maintain the same uptime as the rest of the API. | | **Security**     | Though authentication is not implemented yet, the API should be designed to allow future implementation of authorization checks | | **Usability**    | The API endpoint should follow RESTful conventions. Should return clear, actionable error messages and status codes should accurately reflect the result of operations | | **Maintainability** | Code should include appropriate comments and follow the project's documentation standards. Implementation should align with existing patterns in the codebase | | **Portability**  | The implementation should work consistently across all environments (development, testing, production) | | **Reliability**  | The operation should be transactional - either complete fully or not at all. Should properly handle edge cases like listing components of an invalid learning space |  ### Technical Tasks  | Task | Minutes | |------|---------| | Validate listing requirements with PO (refinement) | 20 | | Clone repo | 3 | | Create feature branch | 2 | | Review domain model for Whiteboard and Space relationship | 30 | | Model/code the listing service interface in domain layer | 40 | | Implement list use case in application layer | 60 | | Implement repository method for listing components in infrastructure layer | 45 | | Update database context configuration if needed | 25 | | Handle constraints with Learning Spaces relationship | 45 | | Create LIST endpoint | 35 | | Configure routing and parameter binding | 20 | | Implement input validation and error handling | 50 | | Create response DTOs for successful/failed listing | 25 | | Build/Compilation | 10 | | Troubleshoot build issues (if any) | 30 | | Follow Clean Code standards (.NET 8 best practices) | 30 | | Respect Clean Architecture and follow SOLID, DDD principles | 20 | | Swagger documentation for the new endpoint | 20 | | Automated unit tests | 75 | | Manual testing with Swagger UI | 25 | | Update documentation in backlog management tool | 20 | | Create pull request and handle code review feedback | 75 | | Meet acceptance criteria, validated with the PO | 15 |  Total estimate (minutes): 720 Total estimate (hours): 12  ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 – Successfully listed the learning components in a learning space  * **Endpoint tested:** GetLearningComponentByIdHandler * **Result:** **200 OK** with a JSON response containing multiple learning components * **Validation:** The success scenario is fulfilled when there are registered learning components in the specified building, floor and learning space; each record includes id, orientation, position, dimensions, and the buildingId, floorId and learningSpaceId in where the learning component is located  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/35aa87cd-7757-4296-984a-cd07e05d178c) ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/63b05680-08f5-4a62-92ec-358e8fc9e5c4)  ---  ### Test 2 – Attempted to list the learning components of a learning space that has none  * **Endpoint tested:** GetLearningComponentByIdHandler * **Result:** **200 OK** with a JSON response containing an empty list * **Validation:** As the acceptance criteria says, the system should return an empty list when a list of learning components from a learning space with no learning components is solicitated  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/910447e9-3b96-420d-9319-d889695c7d42) ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3db93a66-0258-4cca-a31f-48aac3289ce3)  ---  ### Test 3 – Attempted to list the learning components of an invalid learning space   * **Endpoint tested:** GetLearningComponentByIdHandler * **Result:** **Error: response status is 400** with a JSON response containing an error message * **Validation:** As the acceptance criteria specifies, the program should return an error when buildingId inputed is invalid.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/046acbb1-bf30-4a6b-b471-93f18e62d433) ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4ae4989f-24dd-4254-b99c-90ff0f5dd118)

title: SQL-LS-001-002 Read Learning Spaces
author: RolandoVillavicencio013

This includes the ability to list available spaces, view details of a specific space, update existing records, create new entries, and retrieve relevant information.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin title: SQL-LS-001-007 Refactor Create Learning Spaces
author: RolandoVillavicencio013
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-001  **Name**: Learning spaces management  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow users to explore and manipulate data related to learning spaces. This includes the ability to list available spaces, view details of a specific space, update existing records, create new entries, and retrieve relevant information.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Create learning spaces using a relational database and internal keys  As an administrator user   I want to be able to create learning spaces using defined specifications   So that the system accurately reflects the physical layout of the institution while maintaining a normalized and scalable data structure. ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: Create a new learning space with all specifications and internal ID Given I am on the administrative module of the system When I submit the required data to create a learning space (type, height, width, length) Then the system generates a unique internal ID and successfully registers the space ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: Missing required fields when creating a learning space Given I am on the administrative module and attempt to create a learning space with incomplete data (e.g., missing type or dimensions) When I submit the form Then the system displays an error message indicating which fields are required and does not register the space ```  ```gherkin  Supports internal ID management, along with type, height, width, and length for each learning space  ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements – View Registered People**  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | The system should create a learning space within 1 second under normal load. | | **Usability**    | The form for learning space creation must have clear labels and validations for each field. | | **Maintainability** | The logic for handling learning space data should be modular and easy to extend (e.g., add new attributes). | | **Reliability**  | Learning spaces should be saved and retrieved consistently with 99.9% accuracy. |  ---   ### **Technical Tasks – Refactor "Create Learning Spaces" for Maintainability and Extension**  | Task                                                               | Minutes | | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------- | | Clone the repository                                               | 5       | | Create or update the refactor branch                               | 5       | | Review and validate refined "Create Learning Spaces" flow with PO  | 15      | | Refactor domain entities to ensure consistency and reusability     | 30      | | Simplify and decouple the "Create" use case for future extension   | 25      | | Isolate logic into reusable services or helper methods             | 20      | | Review infrastructure repositories and apply necessary adjustments | 15      | | Refactor and optimize database schema for scalability              | 20      | | Apply migrations reflecting schema improvements                    | 15      | | Review and update entity configurations (Fluent API)               | 10      | | Validate mappings across all layers                                | 10      | | Align Application, Domain, and Infrastructure layers with DDD      | 60      | |     - Application layer (refactored create logic)                  | 30      | |     - Domain layer (ValueObjects, rules)                           | 20      | |     - WebAPI (refactor POST endpoint)                              | 10      | | Run unit tests and adapt them to reflect refactored create logic   | 45      | | Write new integration tests if any components were decoupled       | 30      | | Ensure build passes and code quality standards are met             | 20      | | Update CI workflow if structure was impacted                       | 15      | | Update technical documentation to reflect structural changes       | 15      | | Validate new flow and integrity with PO                            | 20      |  ---  ###  Total Estimated Time  | **Total estimate (minutes)** | **Total estimate (hours)** | | ---------------------------- | -------------------------- | | 495                          | 8 hours and 15 minutes     |  ---  ## Performed Manual Tests  ### Test 1 – Creating a learning space with all invalid parameters  * **Result:** **400 Bad Request** with multiple validation error messages. * **Validation:**   A request was sent to create a learning space with all parameters invalid:  - **Type:** invalid value (e.g., `"wrongType"`). - **Name:** more than 100 characters or empty. - **Max capacity:** negative number (`-3`). - **Height, width, length:** negative or zero values (`-4, `-3, `0`).  The system correctly rejected the request with status 400 and returned detailed validation errors for each field.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/daedbc8f-7dfe-4a7d-a438-b759776c2ec9)  ---  ### Test 2 – Creating a learning space with one invalid parameter at a time  * **Result:** **400 Bad Request** with a validation error message related to the specific invalid parameter. * **Validation:**   Separate requests were made with only one invalid field per request while all others were valid:  - Invalid **type** (`"random"`). - valid **name**  - valid **max capacity** - valid **height**  - valid **width**  - valid **length**  Each request failed with a 400 error and a message specifying the incorrect parameter, confirming proper field-level validation.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d090531e-8b4b-4e54-946f-4780cdf75aed)  ---  ### Test 3 – Creating a learning space linked to a non-existent building ID  * **Result:** **404 Not Found** with an error message indicating the building does not exist. * **Validation:**   The request was made with a `building_id` that does not exist in the system (e.g., `9999` or `"abc"`).   Although all other parameters were valid, the system responded with a 404 error, preventing the creation of a learning space linked to a non-existent building.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f5056eb5-2650-49f5-8b8c-63e2813c510f)  ---

title: SQL-LS-001-006 Refactor Update Learning Spaces
author: RolandoVillavicencio013
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-001  **Name**: Learning spaces management    **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow users to explore and manipulate data related to learning spaces. This includes the ability to list available spaces, view details of a specific space, update existing records, create new entries, and retrieve relevant information.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Refactor learning space update with internal key-based database integration  As an administrator   I want the system to handle learning spaces using a relational database with internal IDs   So that we ensure data consistency, normalization, and long-term scalability.  ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: Update a learning space using the internal ID as the reference Given I am on the administrative module and viewing the details of a learning space When I submit updated information for a space identified by its internal ID Then the system saves the changes correctly and confirms the update ```  ```gherkin  Scenario: Attempt to update with invalid or incomplete data Given I am on the administrative module and viewing the details of a learning space When I submit incomplete or invalid information Then the system prevents the update and shows error messages indicating what must be corrected ``` ##  Non-functional Requirements  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**   | The system must refresh data in less than 0.2 seconds. | | **Reliability**   | The system must properly handle errors. | | **Usability**     | The interface should be simple and user-friendly. | | **Maintainability** | The implementation must follow Clean Code principles, be modular, follow DDD architecture, be documented, and easy to maintain. | | **Portability**   | The system must run successfully when integrated with other program functionalities. | | **Security**      | Data update operations must be restricted to users with the Administrator role. | | **Auditability**  | All update operations must be logged and traceable for auditing purposes. |  ---  ##  Technical Tasks   | **Task**                                                                | **Minutes** | | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------- | | Group validation of the update logic                                    | 15          | | Creation of Git branch and logic refactor                               | 10          | | Refactor of the mapping endpoint                                        | 25          | | Refactoring of layer-to-layer and method-to-method flows                | 70          | | Data validation using Value Objects                                     | 20          | | Database connection and configuration                                   | 25          | | Design of the new database schema                                       | 40          | | Implementation of the new database structure and corresponding entities | 50          | | Modifications to handler methods                                        | 20          | | Refactor of the frontend, application, and domain layers                | 45          | | Modifications and validation with the Product Owner (PO)                | 15          | | Validation of Acceptance Criteria and Definition of Done                | 20          | | Review and refactor to align with DDD principles                        | 20          | | Documentation of the update logic and API endpoint                      | 20          | | Code review and feedback integration                                    | 20          |  ---  ##  Total Estimated Time  | **Total Estimate (Minutes)** | **Total Estimate (Hours)** | | ---------------------------- | -------------------------- | | 415                          | 6 hours and 55 minutes     |  ---  ## Performed Manual Tests  ### Test 1 – Updating a learning space with all invalid parameters (except valid ID)  * **Result:** **400 Bad Request** with multiple validation error messages. * **Validation:**   A request was made to update a learning space with a valid existing ID but with all other parameters invalid:  - **Type:** invalid value (e.g., `"invalidType", "string"`). - **Max capacity:** negative number (`0`). - **Height, width, length:** negative or zero values (`0`, `0`, `0`).  The system returned a 400 status with detailed validation errors explaining the incorrect inputs.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6fddc6ab-038e-41d1-b44c-a1ccc9c51f8c)  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/59feb922-067f-4636-b16c-a7c7f1118da1)   ---  ### Test 2 – Updating a learning space with one invalid parameter at a time  * **Result:** **400 Bad Request** with a validation error message specific to the invalid parameter. * **Validation:**   Multiple requests were sent where only one parameter was invalid per request, for example:  - valid **type**  - valid **max capacity** - Invalid **height** (non-numeric) with other valid fields. - valid **width**  - valid **length**  Each request returned a 400 error with a clear message identifying the single invalid parameter.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dfb76ef7-c125-480c-a7a9-bc9576ecaf78)  ---  ### Test 3 – Updating a learning space with a non-existent or invalid ID  * **Result:** **404 Not Found** with and error message indicating the learning space ID does not exist. * **Validation:**   Update requests were made using IDs that do not exist or are invalid format (e.g., negative ID, string instead of integer).   The system responded with a 404 status and a message stating the learning space was not found, blocking the update.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/27abb7dc-1a32-4c0d-b4c8-50b68094b111)  ---

title: PQL-AE-001-002 Edit building information
author: AleJimenezRJ
body: # EPIC ID: PQL-AE-001  **Name:** Add buildings  **Description:** As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow administrative users to register new buildings  to keep track of the university's infrastructure. This includes the ability to add a completetely new building, and updating already existing buildings by editing their information.  ## User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Edit information of buildings    As an administrative user   I want to edit the building information   So that I can keep the university's infrastructure up to date. ``` ## Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: Edit all the building information     Given I am on the administrative module of the system     When access a building information     Then an edit option should appear     And I should be able to edit any information and get a confirmation message that it was succesfully edited ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: Edit the information of a building with invalid or missing information     Given I am editing a building's information     When leaving an information empty or adding invalid information     Then an error message should appear     And the information should not change ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: Edit the information of a non-existing building     Given I am editing a building's information     When I try to edit the information of a building that doesn't exist     Then an error message should appear ```  ## Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs)  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Scalability**  | The infrastructure should follow the  CA guideliness | | **Availability** | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Security**     | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Usability**    | Ease of use in swagger | | **Maintainability** | Ease of changing upper layer implementations | | **Portability**  | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Compliance**   | Adherence to regulatory standards | | **Reliability**  | Should build for all development teams | | **Localization** | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 |  ## Task division  | Technical Task | Minimum Time (Minutes) | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Refining/Validate basic business needs with PO | 40 | | Clone git repository | 3 | | Fetch and pull previous changes to the repository | 3 | | Create branch | 2 |  ### Back-End Development with Clean Code & Clean Architecture  | Technical Task | Minimum Time (Minutes) | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Model/code entities to provide functionality | 40 | | Model/code repositories in infrastructure (I) | 40 | | - Design and scripts for DB, tables, and relationships | 40 | | - PDS for table data | 5 | | - Publish the Data Base | 5 | | Model/code repositories in infrastructure (C) | 30 | | - Review integration with EFF | 2 | | - dbContext | 5 | | - DTO and Mapping Layer | 60 |  ### PBI: Edit Building Information  | Technical Task | Minimum Time (Minutes) | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Implement editing building item service | 150 | | Dependency management | 10 | | Continuous integration to repo, into main + PR | 60 | | Meets acceptance criteria validated with PO | 60 |  ### Front-End Development: App, Presentation, WebAPI (EP class)  | Technical Task | Minimum Time (Minutes) | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | App | 60 | | Presentation | 150 | | WebAPI | 60 | | Cross-Team API Integration | 60 | | Compatibility | 30 | | Clean Code Compliance (duplicate code, code smells) | 10 | | Don’t break architecture (CA) and follow SOLID, DDD | 30 |  ### Tests & SQA  | Technical Task | Minimum Time (Minutes) | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Swagger tests | 60 | | Unit automated tests, 80% coverage | 150 | | Integration Testing with Shared Components | 150 | | Error Handling Strategy | 45 | | Documented and updated status in backlog tool | 10 | | Prototyping, design, validation | 120 |  --- ### Total  | Total Type | Total | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Total Minutes | 1425 | | Total Hours    | 23.75 |   ## Performed Manual Tests   ### Test 1 – Successful update of a building  * **Scenario:** `the user tries to update a building's properties with the correct format` * **Format needed:** The ID is made up of text with the format of two letters, a dash and more text after the dash. The color is a text that has to match with a color stored in a predetermined list. The university, campus and area are made up of any text. The height, width, and length have to be made up of positive numbers. The coordinates for the building have to be made up of numbers. * **Result:** **200 OK** with successful updating of the building's data in the data base. * **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: *"Edit all the building information"*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9b5577ac-4f72-4896-a06c-c4716f80de77)  ---  ### Test 2 – Missing information while updating a building * **Scenario:** `the user tries to edit the information of a building with invalid or missing information` * **Result:** **404 ERROR** without adding anything to the data base and letting the user know that the properties they entered might have the incorrect format. * **Validation:** Satisfies the error scenario from the acceptance criteria: *"Edit the information of a building with invalid or missing information"*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9aabef1f-6086-4fab-8963-06613fcbf7f8)  ---  ### Test 3 – Nonexistent building ID  * **Scenario:** `User tries to edit a building's information, but a building with the inputted ID doesn't exist in the system` * **Result:** **404 ERROR** without editing anything in the data base and letting the user know that the building with the inputted ID was not found. * **Validation:** Satisfies the error scenario from the acceptance criteria: *"Edit the information of a non-existing building"*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a06ae1fa-5ac8-4d87-a328-6e8e76367440)

title: SPT-UM-001-003 Create person
author: ebai03
body: ### EPIC ID: SPT-UM-001 **Name:** User Management **Description:** As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow the management of user accounts. This includes the ability to create, view, modify, delete, and retrieve user information. Proper user management is essential for controlling access and customizing the environment.  --- ### User Story ```Gherkin Feature: Create a person  As a user I want to create a new person in the system So that I can access the platform ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria ```Gherkin Scenario: Successful person creation Given valid person data (e.g., email) When the create person function is called Then the system should save a new person record in the database And should return a success response ```  ```Gherkin Scenario: Reject creation if the person already exists Given that a person with the same email or identity number already exists in the system When the create person function is called Then the system should not create a new person And should return an error indicating duplication ``` ```Gherkin  Scenario: Reject creation if required fields are missing Given the person data is missing required fields (e.g., no email) When the create person function is called Then the system should return a validation error And not save anything to the database ```  #### Non-funtional requirements  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | Person creation should not take longer than 2 seconds under normal system load. | | **Reliability** | The person creation feature should succeed in 99.9% of cases when provided with valid data. | | **Usability**     | The person creation form must display clear validation messages. | | **Maintainability**    | The person creation logic should be modular to support future updates, such as validation rule changes or user model adjustments. | | **Portability** | The person creation module must work across different deployment environments. |  ---  ### Technical Tasks  | Task | Minutes | |------|---------| | Validate basic business needs with the PO (refinement) | 15 | | Set up development environment | 20 | | Create dummy `create person` endpoint (mock logic) | 25 | | Implement basic response structure and Handler codes in dummy | 15 | | Implement real person creation logic in application layer (SQL version) | 60 | | Implement person repository in infrastructure layer with SQL | 40 | | Replace dummy logic with real implementation | 10 | | Add basic input validation (e.g., required fields, email format) | 30 | | Add integration tests for endpoint | 25 | | Add Swagger documentation for user creation endpoint | 25 | | Test manually in Swagger | 20 | | Conduct peer code review before merge | 20 | | Merge to main via pull request | 10 | | Confirm with PO that Acceptance Criteria are met | 20 |  **Total estimate (minutes):** 365   **Total estimate (hours):** 6.08    --- ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 - Successful creation  - **Result:** 200 OK with successful creation. - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “the system should save a new person record in the database and should return a success response”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8d1c3c27-16bc-449a-b4ba-f8b45348eb3c)  ---  ### Test 2 - Invalid Email  - **Result:** 400 Error    - Invalid email format. It must contain '@' and end with . domain.   - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “the system should return a validation error and not save anything to the database”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b590b1ae-e297-4f42-bc28-ca57d29998a3)  ---  ### Test 3 - Invalid Identity Number  - **Result:** 400 Error    - Invalid identity number format. Expected format: 1-1234-5678   - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “the system should return a validation error and not save anything to the database”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/07b4f920-0b6a-4500-8d86-d3083cc77186)  ---  ### Test 4 - Invalid Phone Number  - **Result:** 400 Error   - Invalid phone number format. Expected format: 12345678   - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “the system should return a validation error and not save anything to the database”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/699672f5-4262-4bc7-9529-ab0711aa3649)  ---  ### Test 5 -  Invalid BirthDate  - **Result:** 400 Error   - Malformed JSON or invalid request.  - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “the system should return a validation error and not save anything to the database”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0e69d028-4b14-4d97-8298-6239d6dc3e79)  ---  ### Test 6 - Existing person  - **Result:** 400 Error    - Attempted to create a person with an existing identity number or email.   - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “the system should not create a new person and should return an error indicating duplication”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9d325904-53a3-402f-9da7-687e1bbd2136)

title: SPT-UM-001-002 Read person
author: tatsparamo
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-UM-001**  - **Name:** Person Management   - **Description:** As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow the management of person records. This includes the ability to create, view, modify, delete, and retrieve person information. Proper person management is essential for controlling access and customizing the environment.  ---  ### **User Story** ```gherkin Feature: Read the person  As a user   I want to read a person’s info in the system   So that I can see their details when needed   ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Successful person reading Given the person exists in the system   When the user requests to read their data   Then the system will return the person’s info   ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Reject reading person data if missing or incorrect information Given no person associated with an ID is found   When I try to find them in the system   Then the system should notify me the person was not found   And shouldn’t show any information   But it may keep a record that I tried to look   ``` --- #### **Non-functional Requirements – Read the person**  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | Retrieving a person’s information should take no longer than 1 second under normal system load. | | **Reliability**  | The person retrieval feature should return correct results in 99.9% of cases when the person exists. | | **Usability**    | If the person is not found, the system must provide a clear, user-friendly message. | | **Maintainability** | The logic for retrieving person data should be isolated and easy to update if the data model evolves. | | **Portability**  | The person retrieval logic should function consistently across all supported deployment environments. |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks – Read the person**  | Task | Minutes | |------|---------| | Validate requirements and acceptance criteria with the PO | 10 | | Ensure test database has person records for testing | 10 | | Implement person retrieval logic in application layer | 35 | | Implement method in person repository in infrastructure layer | 25 | | Handle “person not found” scenario with proper messaging | 20 | | Add Swagger documentation for the read endpoint | 15 | | Write unit and integration tests for success and failure cases | 40 | | Create pull request and request code review | 10 | | Validate with PO that the functionality meets expectations | 15 |  **Total estimate (minutes):** 180   **Total estimate (hours):** 3  ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 – Successful person retrieval  * **ID used:** `2-2345-6789` * **Result:** **200 OK** with correct return of the person’s data. * **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: *“the system returns the information when the person exists.”*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3cfc48ac-07df-48be-ad58-c0af73fa2e03)  ---  ### Test 2 – Person not found  * **ID used:** `2-2345-6999` * **Result:** **404 Not Found** with a clear message: *“Person with identity number '2-2345-6999' was not found.”* * **Validation:** Satisfies the error scenario defined in the acceptance criteria for non-existent persons.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8887b35e-14a0-4e21-a4ca-fc16e9dc2f9e)  ---  ### Test 3 – Invalid identity number format  * **ID used:** `abc123` * **Result:** **400 Bad Request** with a clear message: *“Invalid identity number format. Expected format is 1-2345-6789.”* * **Validation:** Input validation is correctly enforced, preventing queries with invalid formats and maintaining system integrity.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1139dca0-83e2-49f0-99b4-8be5136db68e)

title: SPT-UM-001-001 View registered people
author: cyaos
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-UM-001**  - **Name:** Person Management   - **Description:** As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow the management of person records. This includes the ability to create, view, modify, delete, and retrieve person information. Proper person management is essential for controlling access and customizing the environment.  ---  ### **User Story** ```gherkin Feature: View the list of registered people  As a system administrator I want to view a list of all registered people So that I can manage access and ensure the correct people are in the system  ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Successful view list of registered users Given the system contains one or more registered users When the administrator requests the user list Then the system should display a list of all registered users And each entry should include basic information (e.g. name, email) ```  ```gherkin Scenario: No registered users Given there are no registered users in the system When the administrator requests the user list Then the system should display a message indicating there are no users And the list should be empty ```  ```gherkin Scenario: The user list includes newly added users Given a new user was recently created When the administrator refreshes or reopens the user list Then a new user should appear in the list And their information should be displayed correctly ```  --- #### **Non-functional Requirements – View the list of registered people**  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | Retrieving the list of registered people should take no longer than 1 second under normal system load. | | **Security**     | Only authenticated and authorized users (e.g., administrators) can access the list of registered people. | | **Reliability**  | The endpoint should return accurate and complete data in 99.9% of valid cases. | | **Maintainability** | The person retrieval logic should be modular and support future extensions such as filtering or pagination. | | **Portability**  | The endpoint should function consistently across all supported deployment environments. |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks – View the list of registered people**  | Task | Minutes | |------|---------| | Validate requirements and acceptance criteria with the PO | 10 | | Ensure test database has registered people for testing | 10 | | Implement retrieval logic in the application layer | 30 | | Implement method in the person repository (infrastructure layer) | 25 | | Handle empty list scenario with appropriate response | 20 | | Write unit and integration tests for success and empty cases | 35 | | Create pull request and request code review | 10 | | Validate with PO that the functionality meets expectations | 15 |  **Total estimate (minutes):** 155   **Total estimate (hours):** 2.6  ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 – List with registered people  * **Result:** **200 OK** with a JSON response containing multiple people. * **Validation:** The success scenario is fulfilled when there are registered people in the system; each record includes name, email, phone, birthdate, and identity number.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e034d9f8-c8f8-4826-9ea5-08a88ce507fa)  ---  ### Test 2 – Empty list  * **Result:** **200 OK** with message: *"There are no registered people."* * **Validation:** The scenario where there are no registered people is correctly handled; the response is clear and error-free.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3480ca5c-91b9-4f10-b55b-3b9d13608bb3)  ---  ### Test 3 – Recently registered person appears in the list  * **Result:** **200 OK** and the newly added record (`Juan Pérez`) appears in the list. * **Validation:** The system successfully updates the list after a new person is registered, showing their complete information immediately.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/58f85bd3-6052-4ff1-a82d-1270d0e6893f)  ---  ### Test 4 – Listing of multiple people  * **Result:** **200 OK** with a JSON response containing a list of several registered people. * **Validation:** The system correctly returns all existing records, displaying each person's name, last name, email, phone, birthdate, and identity number.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2536e4af-ca0b-4701-9141-4243d19fac34)

title: SPT-UM-001-004 Modify person
author: gaelalpizar
body: ### **EPIC ID**: SPT-UM-001   **Name**: Person Management   **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow the management of person records. This includes the ability to create, view, modify, delete, and retrieve person information. Proper person management is essential for controlling access and customizing the environment.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Modify a person  As an admin   I want to update a person's information in the system   So that I can keep their profile up to date and accurate ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Successful person modification   Given that valid updated data is provided for an existing person When the modify user function is called   Then the system should update the person record in the database   And should return a success response   But the system should not allow changes to protected fields like person ID ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Reject modification if the person does not exist   Given that the person ID provided does not match any existing person When the modify person function is called   Then the system should return an error indicating the person was not found   And should not make any changes in the database   But the system should log the failed attempt for auditing ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Reject modification if required fields are invalid Given that some required fields (e.g. number or email) are invalid in the request   When the modify person function is called   Then the system should return an error indicating the invalid fields   And should not update the person record   ``` #### Non-functional requirements  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Security**  | Only authorized users may modify person data. | | **Performance**  | Modifications must take less than 2 seconds. | | **Auditability** | All updates and failed attempts must be logged. | | **Reliability** | The modification should succeed in 99.9% of valid cases. | | **Maintainability** | Code should allow future updates to fields and validation rules. | | **Portability** | The logic must be independent of the database used. |  ---  ### Technical Tasks  | Task | Minutes | |------|---------| | Discuss update field constraints with PO | 10 | | Build update person logic | 40 | | Implement protection of immutable fields | 20 | | Add request validation logic | 30 | | Add error handling and feedback messages | 20 | | Add logging for failed attempts | 20 | | Write and run unit tests | 30 | | Confirm with PO that Acceptance Criteria are met | 20 |  **Total estimate (minutes):** 190   **Total estimate (hours):** 3.17 --- ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 – Non-existent identity number  * **ID used:** `1-1234-5677` * **Result:** **404 Error** – “Person with IdentityNumber 1-1234-5677 not found.” * **Validation:** Satisfies the rejection scenario from the acceptance criteria: *“Reject modification if the person does not exist.”*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/de8cc0af-b50f-4a43-96ae-8a0a524a1d4b)  ---  ### Test 2 – Invalid identity number  * **ID used:** `1-1234-567` * **Result:** **400 Error** – “Invalid identity number. Expected format: 1-1234-5678.” * **Validation:** Satisfies the rejection scenario from the acceptance criteria: *“Reject modification if required fields are invalid.”*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d03f24d0-405a-4270-a8dc-b1aa376ce7be)  ---  ### Test 3 – Invalid number  * **Number used:** `8888-1234` * **Result:** **400 Error** – “Invalid number. Expected format: 12345678.” * **Validation:** Satisfies the rejection scenario from the acceptance criteria: *“Reject modification if required fields are invalid.”*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/02702ce5-9e6d-4e1b-9b97-ea630828a89e)  ---  ### Test 4 – Invalid email  * **E-mail used:** `maria.lopezucr.ac.cr` * **Result:** **400 Error** – “Invalid email format. It must contain '@' and end with a valid domain.” * **Validation:** Satisfies the rejection scenario from the acceptance criteria: *“Reject modification if required fields are invalid.”*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a0ab187b-59f2-427b-bb41-f48f5b6e6d61)  ---  ### Test 5 – Successful person modification  * **ID used:** `1-1234-5678` * **Result:** **200 OK** – “Person with IdentityNumber 1-1234-5678 successfully updated.” * **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: *“Successful person modification.”*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8f885936-60b0-452f-a894-eecf45e01096)  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0833ab11-bcdf-4c37-9cd9-122819c217d5)

title: CPD-LC-001-003 Create a whiteboard
author: Earaya12
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-LC-001 **Name**: Learning components **Description**:  This epic focuses on enhancing learning spaces within ThemePark@UCR, such as classrooms and laboratories, by adding interactive components like whiteboards and projectors. These components are designed to create an interactive and dynamic learning environment.  --- ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Create whiteboard in a learning space  As an user   I want to create a whiteboard   So that I can display them in a learning space  ``` ---  ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: Successfully create a whiteboard in a learning space Given the user is logged in   And there are available whiteboards   And the selected whiteboard fits in the chosen learning space   When the user creates a whiteboard   Then the system adds the whiteboard to the chosen classroom or laboratory   And confirms the addition to the user ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Create a whiteboard with invalid input Given a user   When the user submits invalid or incomplete data   Then the system prevents creation   And displays an error message indicating the input issue ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Attempt to create a whiteboard that doesn’t fit in the learning space Given the user is logged in   And a whiteboard is selected that exceeds the available space dimensions   When the user tries to create the whiteboard   Then the system shows an error message indicating that the whiteboard doesn't fit in the space ```   #### Non-funtional requirements  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | The system must process the creation of a whiteboard in under 2 seconds. | | **Reliability** | The system must behave consistently under valid conditions and handle errors gracefully when unexpected input or failures occur, ensuring predictable and stable behavior. | | **Usability**     | The creation form must be clear and user-friendly, providing visible validation and understandable error messages. | | **Maintainability**    | The implementation must follow Clean Code principles, be modular, and documented to facilitate future maintenance. | | **Portability** | The functionality must run successfully in the current development and test environments used in this sprint. |  ---  ### Technical Tasks  | Task | Minutes | |------|---------| | Validate basic business needs with the PO (refinement) | 20 | | Create branch | 3 | | Model/code repositories in infrastructure layer (I) | 3 | | Design and scripts for database, tables and relationships | 40 | | Sample data (PDS) for those tables | 5 | | Model/code repositories in infrastructure layer (C) | 20 | | Configure `bdContext` | 1 | | Entity-to-table mappings | 15 | | Implement creation service | 10 | | Dependency management | 3 | | Continuous integration to repo, merged into main + PR (includes code review) | 60 | |    App | 60 | |    Presentation | 10 | |    WebAPI | 30 | | Apply validation logic and error messaging | 10 | | Handle edge cases: invalid input, oversized whiteboards | 20 | | Build/Compilation | 5 | | Follow Clean Code (no duplicated code, code smells, C# guidelines...) | 15 | | Respect architecture (CA) and follow SOLID, DDD principles | 5 | | Must be documented and status updated in backlog management tool | 15 | | Prototyping, design, validation | 120 | | Meet acceptance criteria, validated with the PO | 10 |  **Total estimate (minutes):** 480   **Total estimate (hours):** 8  ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 – Successfully create a whiteboard in a learning space  * **Endpoint tested:** PostWhiteboardHandler * **Result:** **201 Created** with a JSON response containing the characteristics of the new whiteboard * **Validation:** the new whiteboard is succesfully created and added to the database, just as it is said in the acceptance criteria  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/187c10f1-f388-4cbf-947b-4ff3f066da00) ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4d2d0991-ca79-4c98-a775-2e57e7819523)  ---  ### Test 2 – Create a whiteboard with invalid input  * **Endpoint tested:** PostWhiteboardHandler * **Result:** **400 Error: response status is 400** with a JSON response containing an error message * **Validation:** just as the acceptance criteria says, the whiteboard is not created and an error message is shown in it's place  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/81506eb9-33ea-4b3a-8bbc-b7283aca846b) ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/780bfe5a-d5ee-441d-8806-176b75cdd9a0)  ### Test 3 – Attempt to create a whiteboard that doesn’t fit in the learning space  * **Endpoint tested:** PostWhiteboardHandler * **Result:** **409 Error: response status is 409** with a JSON response containing an error message * **Validation:** just as the acceptance criteria says, the whiteboard is not created and an error message is shown in it's place  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/720fa515-2081-4539-ac9f-23310399befb) ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e6c751e1-078a-4c24-bd12-7846107d5ca7)

title: CPD-LC-001-002 Read characteristics of a learning component
author: Earaya12
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-LC-001 **Name**: Learning components **Description**: This epic focuses on enhancing learning spaces within ThemePark@UCR, such as classrooms and laboratories, by adding interactive components like whiteboards and projectors. These components are designed to create an interactive and dynamic learning environment.  ---  ### User Story ```gherkin Feature: Read a component on the list  As an user I want to read a component on the list So that I can view its specific characteristics ``` ---  ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: Successfully read a component from the list Given the learning space contains one or more components When the user selects a component from the list Then the system should display the component's details And the details should include basic information ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Attempt to read a non-existent component from the list Given the learning space contains one or more components And the selected component ID does not exist in the system When the user attempts to view the component's details Then the system should display an error message indicating the component was not found ```  ---  ### Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs)  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | The system should retrieve and display component details within 500ms under normal load conditions | | **Scalability**  | The read operation should maintain performance when multiple read requests occur simultaneously | | **Availability** | The read functionality should maintain the same uptime as the rest of the API | | **Security**     | Though authentication is not implemented yet, the API should be designed to allow future implementation of authorization checks | | **Usability**    | The API endpoint should follow RESTful conventions. Should return clear, actionable error messages and status codes should accurately reflect the result of operations | | **Maintainability** | Code should include appropriate comments and follow the project's documentation standards. title: SPT-UM-001-005 Delete person
author: isabellarodriguezs
body: **EPIC ID:** SPT-UM-001 **Name:** Person Management **Description:** As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow the management of user accounts. This includes the ability to create, view, modify, delete, and retrieve person information. Proper user management is essential for controlling access and customizing the environment.  --- **User Story**  ``` gherkin Feature: Delete person  As a person I want to delete all data related to a person So that the person no longer has access to the platform and their data is properly removed  ``` ---  **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Successful person deletion Given valid and existing person ID When the delete function is called Then the system should remove the person record from the database And revoke any active sessions or tokens ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Attempt to delete a non-existent person Given no person associated with an ID is found When the delete person function is called Then the system should not try to delete person And should return an error message ```   #### Non-functional requirements  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Security**  | Deletion operations must be restricted to authorized people. | | **Performance**  | Deletion must complete within 2 seconds under normal load. | | **Auditability** | All deletion attempts must be logged for audit purposes. | | **Reliability** | Deletion must succeed in 99.9% of valid requests. | | **Usability**     | Error and success messages must clearly communicate the outcome. | | **Maintainability** | The logic must support potential soft delete scenarios. |  ---  ### Technical Tasks  | Task | Minutes | |------|---------| | Validate deletion logic with PO | 15 | | Implement delete user endpoint | 45 | | Implement session/token revocation | 30 | | Add authorization guard logic | 25 | | Handle error for non-existent users | 20 | | Log unauthorized and failed deletion attempts | 20 | | Write unit tests | 30 | | Confirm with PO that Acceptance Criteria are met | 20 |  **Total estimate (minutes):** 205   **Total estimate (hours):** 3.42  ---  ## Performed Tests ### Test 1 – Successful person deletion  * *Endpoint tested:* DELETE /person/{identityNumber} * *Input:* Existing valid person ID * *Result:* *200 OK* with message: "Person has been deleted from the system successfully." * *Validation:* The person was removed from the database. Subsequent GET /person/{id} returned *404 Not Found*. This confirms correct behavior for valid deletion.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/234241b5-e952-463d-844a-39758b3e18b5)  ---  ### Test 2 – Person not found  * *Endpoint tested:* DELETE /person/1-1111-1111 * *Input:* Non-existent ID * *Result:* *404 Not Found* with message: "Person with identity number {identityNumber} was not found." * *Validation:* System handled the case gracefully without attempting deletion, as expected by the acceptance criteria.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a9f11a8f-ab5f-424f-b498-fee48f42fa81)

title: PQL-AE-001-001 Add building
author: AleJimenezRJ
body: ### EPIC ID: PQL-AE-001  **Name**: Add Building  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow administrators to register new buildings in the system to keep track of the university's infrastructure up to date, ensuring no duplicated and access restricted to admin users only.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Add and register a new building in the system.  As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want to be able to register new buildings in the system, so that I can maintain an up-to-date record of the university’s infrastructure. ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: Successful adding of the new building and registering of its information    Given the administrator added a new building   When the administrator submits the building's information   Then the system should store the new building as a new entry in the Buildings Data Base   And each entry should at least include basic information about the building(e.g. name, size) ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: Prevent addition of new buildings with incorrect property format   Given the administrator tries to add a new building with incorrect property format   When the administrator submits the building's information   Then the system should reject the registration and display an error message   And the building should not be added to the system. ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: Prevent duplicate building registration   Given a building with the same name or ID that already exists in the system   When the administrator attempts to register a new building with the same name or ID   Then the system should reject the registration and display an error message   And the building should not be added to the system. ```  ## Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs)  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Scalability**  | The infrastructure should follow the CA guideliness | | **Availability** | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Security**     | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Usability**    | Ease of use in swagger | | **Maintainability** | Ease of changing upper layer implementations | | **Portability**  | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Compliance**   | Adherence to regulatory standards | | **Reliability**  | Should build for all development teams | | **Localization** | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 |  ## Task division  | Technical Task | Minimum Time (Minutes) | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Refining/Validate basic business needs with PO | 20| | Clone git repository | 5| | Create branches | 3|  ### Back-End Development with Clean Code & Clean Architecture  | Technical Task | Minimum Time (Minutes) | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Model/code entities to provide functionality | 40 | | Model/code repositories in infrastructure (I) | 40 | | - Design and scripts for DB, tables, and relationships | 40 | | - PDS for table data | 5 | | - Publish the Data Base | 10| | Model/code repositories in infrastructure (C) | 30 | | - Review integration with EFF | 2 | | - dbContext | 1| | - Map entities to tables | 15|  ### PBI: Add building  | Technical Task | Minimum Time (Minutes) | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Implement adding building item service | 120| | Dependency management | 10 | | Continuous integration to repo, into main + PR | 60 | | Meets acceptance criteria validated with PO | 60 |  ### Front-End Development: App, Presentation, WebAPI (EP class)  | Technical Task | Minimum Time (Minutes) | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | App | 60 | | Presentation | 120| | WebAPI | 30| | Compatibility | 30 | | Clean Code Compliance (duplicate code, code smells) | 15| | Don’t break architecture (CA) and follow SOLID, DDD | 5|  ### Tests & SQA  | Technical Task | Minimum Time (Minutes) | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Swagger tests | 60 | | Unit automated tests, 80% coverage | 150 | | Integration Testing with Shared Components | 150 | | Documented and updated status in backlog tool | 10 | | Prototyping, design, validation | 120 |  | Total Type | Total | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Total Minutes | 1181| | Total Hours | 19.69|  ## Performed Manual Tests   ### Test 1 – Successful adding of a building  * **Scenario:** `the user tries to add a building with the correct format` * **Format needed:** The ID is made up of text with the format of two letters, a dash and more text after the dash. The color is a text that has to match with a color stored in a predetermined list. The university, campus and area are made up of any text. The height, width, and length have to be made up of positive numbers. The coordinates for the building have to be made up of numbers. * **Result:** **200 OK** with correct addition of the building's data to the data base. * **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: *"Successful adding of the new building and registering of its information"* Also satisfies *"Add building registration promptly"*, since the response occurs in less than 2 seconds.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ce4b51b3-9dd8-4ebd-bd87-3766f6bd1fc4)  ---  ### Test 2 – Prevent duplicate building registration  * **Scenario:** `the user tries to add a building that already exists, which is shown by using an ID that is already being used by an existing building` * **Result:** **409 ERROR** without adding anything to the data base and letting the user know that what they entered could be a duplicate, resulting in the error. * **Validation:** Satisfies the error scenario from the acceptance criteria: *"Prevent duplicate building registration"*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/621590af-d303-4c81-9c5a-e5f9c798bd63)  ---  ### Test 3 – Prevent adding a building with the incorrect format  * **Scenario:** `The user tries to add a building with the incorrect format for input` * **Result:** **400 ERROR** without adding anything to the data base and letting the user know that there was a format error (Correct format established in Test 1). * **Validation:** Satisfies the error scenario from the acceptance criteria: *"Prevent addition of new buildings with incorrect property format"*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/27de2a8b-c82a-48e3-820b-cd1e5c8434c6)  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f689bafc-0432-4e1d-acfb-38f5f5b09d91)

title: PQL-LB-002-002: Display Specific Building Information
author: camsko
body: # EPIC ID: PQL-LB-002  **Name:** Display Specific Building Information  **Description:** As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, it must be possible to access/read information for each building that exists in the system.  ## User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Access information of existing building    As a user wanting to learn about the buildings   I want to be able to see the details of a certain building   So that I can know more about the place I'm interested in. ```  ## Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: View information of existing building     Given there is a title: CPD-LC-001-009 Automatic ID generation for components
author: Fett-Ron
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-LC-001 **Name**: Learning components **Description**: This epic is about adding components to learning spaces in ThemePark@UCR, like classrooms and labs. These components can be things like whiteboards, chairs, or projectors that users can interact with, so that learning feels more active and engaging. It also focuses on building the functionality to create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) these objects in the system.  ---  ### User Story ```gherkin Feature: Automatic ID generation  As an administrator I want the component IDs to be generated automatically So I don’t need to worry about choosing or remembering unique IDs when creating components. ``` ---  ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: Component is assigned an auto-generated ID Given the administrator is creating a new component When the form is submitted without specifying an ID Then the system generates a unique ID and saves it with the component ```  ```gherkin Scenario: IDs are unique across all components Given multiple components are created When each one is saved Then each receives a different, unique ID ``` --- ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                                                                  | | ------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Security**        | Only when the component is created, the automatic ID is generated.      | | **Consistency**     | Generated IDs must be globally unique and consistent across the system.      | | **Usability**       | The UI should clearly indicate that the ID was auto-generated and show the value. | | **Maintainability** | The ID generation logic should be extensible to accommodate future rules (e.g., custom prefixes or suffixes). |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                              | Minutes | | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | Design and implement backend logic for auto-generating unique IDs | 45      | | Validate uniqueness of auto-generated IDs across all components   | 30      | | Update database schema if needed to support unique IDs            | 25      | | Update component creation UI to omit manual ID input              | 20      | | Display generated ID in the component creation confirmation       | 20      | | Write unit tests for ID generation logic                          | 30      | | Write integration tests for CRUD operations with auto-generated IDs | 30     | | Document functionality and provide usage examples                 | 15      | | Perform code review and open pull request                         | 10      |  ### **Total Time**  | **Total Minutes** | **Total Hours** | |-------------------|------------------| | 225 minutes       | 3 hours 45 minutes |  ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 – Component is assigned an auto-generated unique ID  * **Validation:** The web interface doesn't let the user to choose the ID of the component when is created, instead the ID is automatically generated and there's no way to change it in the future  (No ID field) ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/860a3a32-76cb-458e-8839-9a8c2dd1af0a)  (ID shown after creation) ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/638fd5df-3f06-4f5f-8448-89f3cb28ec27)

title: PQL-AE-001-004 Add Campus
author: disotocastro
body: ### EPIC ID: PQL-AE-001  **Name**: Add Campus  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow administrators to register new campuses in the system, this to keep track of which campuses of each university are available for view in the project.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Add and register a new campus in the system.  As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want to be able to register a new campus in the system, so that I can maintain an updated record of the campuses available in the system. ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: Successful adding of the new campus and registering of its information   Given the administrator selected a university and added a new campus   When the administrator submits the campus's information (including its university association)   Then the system should store the new campus as a new entry in the Campuses Data Base ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: Prevent addition of new campuses with incorrect property format   Given the administrator tries to add a new campus with incorrect property format   When the administrator submits the campus's information   Then the system should reject the registration and display an error message   And the campus should not be added to the system. ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: Prevent duplicate campus registration within the same university   Given a campus with a name that already exists within the selected university in the system   When the administrator attempts to register a new campus with the same name or ID under the same university   Then the system should reject the registration and display an error message   And the campus should not be added to the system. ```  ## As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want to be able to register a new university in the system, so that I can maintain an updated record of the universities available in the system. ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: Successful adding of the new university and registering of its information    Given the administrator added a new university   When the administrator submits the university's information   Then the system should store the new university as a new entry in the University Data Base   And each entry should at least include basic information about the university(e.g. name, country) ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: Prevent addition of new university with incorrect property format   Given the administrator tries to add a new university with incorrect property format   When the administrator submits the university's information   Then the system should reject the registration and display an error message   And the university should not be added to the system. ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: Prevent duplicate university registration   Given a university with the same name or ID that already exists in the system   When the administrator attempts to register a new university with the same name or ID   Then the system should reject the registration and display an error message   And the university should not be added to the system. ```  ## As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want to be able to register a new area in the system, so that I can maintain an updated record of the areas available in the system. ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario:  Successful addition and registration of a new area   Given the administrator has entered all required information for the new area (e.g. name)   When the administrator submits the area registration form   Then the system should store the new area as a new entry in the system   And the entry should include all required details such as name ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: Prevent area registration with invalid or empty name   Given the administrator enters an empty name or a name with invalid characters (e.g. symbols not allowed)   When the administrator submits the area registration   Then the system should reject the submission and display a validation error message   And the area should not be added to the system ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: Prevent duplicate area registration   Given an area with the same name already exists for the same campus or university   When the administrator tries to register a new area with that same name   Then the system should reject the registration and display an error message   And the new area should not be stored in the system ```  ##
