title: SPT-SR-007-001 Unified and reliable error handling
author: andresMurillo23
assignees: ebai03, tatsparamo, cyaos, isabellarodriguezs, andresMurillo23, gaelalpizar
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-SR-007**  **Name:** System Refactoring **Description:** To ensure stability, consistency, and performance, ThemePark@UCR must undergo internal improvements that result in a more reliable, predictable, and responsive experience for users. These improvements aim to reinforce permission handling, consistency across modules, and system scalability under high usage.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Unified and reliable error handling  As a user   I want the system to handle errors consistently across all sections   So that I can trust that the platform behaves predictably and provides clear feedback when something goes wrong  ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Understandable error messages guide the user Given a user encounters an error while using the platform   When the system displays the error   Then the message should explain the issue in simple language   And suggest what the user can do next ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Consistent error appearance across modules Given errors occur in different parts of the platform When the system presents these messages   Then the format, tone, and visual style of the errors should be consistent   So that users recognize and trust system feedback ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Avoid exposing technical or sensitive information Given a system-level error occurs   When the error is shown to the user   Then no technical details should be exposed   And the message should remain helpful and secure ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category                 | Description                                                                                        | | ------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Usability**            | Error messages must be easy to understand, written in clear and friendly language.                 | | **Security**             | Error messages must not reveal technical details or sensitive system information to end users.     | | **Consistency**          | All system error messages must follow a unified structure, tone, and visual style.                 | | **Accessibility**        | Error messages must be screen-reader friendly and compatible with assistive technologies.          | | **Internationalization** | Error messages must support at least Spanish and English, based on the users language preference. | | **Reliability**          | Error message handling must be robust and functional even during critical system failures.         |   ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                                   | Minutes | | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | Design a standardized error message template                           | 15      | | Create a centralized structure to define error codes and messages      | 20      | | Implement multilingual error message support                           | 25      | | Refactor frontend components to display friendly, user-facing errors   | 20      | | Update backend services to return errors using the standardized format | 25      | | Prevent exposure of technical/internal errors to end users             | 15      | | Integrate error messages with localization files                       | 20      | | Write automated tests to validate frontend and backend error handling  | 15      | | Document error handling conventions and include message examples       | 10      | | Code review and pull request                                           | 10      |  **Total estimate:** 175 minutes (~2.9 hours)   ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 - Understandable error messages guide the user  - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “the message should explain the issue in simple language”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9d9abb45-437a-41b7-9aa6-4b954ba215a5)  ---  ### Test 2 - Consistent error appearance across modules  - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “ the format, tone, and visual style of the errors should be consistent”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a2c9d6c7-d58d-4b72-b890-daaed5f85d1d)   ---  ### Test 3 - Avoid exposing technical or sensitive information  - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “no technical details should be exposed”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d07dfd5c-9162-4774-8332-5062d7d619c2)

email   Then both emails should be saved and linked to the user profile ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Validation for email formats Given the administrator enters invalid email formats  When the form is title: SPT-AU-002-003 Integrate backend with Microsoft B2C logout flow
author: andresMurillo23
assignees: ebai03, tatsparamo, cyaos, isabellarodriguezs, andresMurillo23, gaelalpizar
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-AU-002**  * **Name:** Backend Logout with Microsoft B2C   * **Description:** As part of the security infrastructure of ThemePark@UCR, the backend must implement logout support integrated with Microsoft Auth B2C. This ensures that user sessions are terminated correctly, and tokens are invalidated or redirected through the appropriate B2C logout flow.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Log out via backend  As a backend system   I want to support Microsoft Auth B2C logout flows   So that user sessions are properly terminated and unauthorized access is prevented ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Backend logout initiation Given the user is authenticated When a logout request is received Then the backend should redirect to the Microsoft B2C logout endpoint And clear any backend-managed session data ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Token reuse prevention Given a user logs out When the same token is reused for a protected endpoint Then the backend should respond with a 401 Unauthorized ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements – Backend Logout**  | Category        | Description                                                                 | | --------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Security**    | The backend must not allow reuse of tokens post-logout.                     | | **Maintainability** | The B2C logout URL should be externally configured.                     | | **Reliability** | Logout redirection must work reliably across client platforms.              |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                            | Minutes | | ----------------------------------------------------------------| ------- | | Implement logout endpoint         | 30      | | Redirect to Microsoft B2C logout URL with post-logout redirect  | 30      | | Clear any backend-managed session or auth state if applicable   | 20     | | Prevent access with stale tokens via standard validation checks | 30      | | Add tests for logout behavior and token invalidation            | 30      | | Externalize logout URL in configuration          | 30      | | Code review and pull request            | 10      |  **Total estimate:** 180 minutes (3 hours)  ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 – Successful login via Swagger UI (B2C)  * **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criterion *“Backend logout initiation,”* as the backend correctly recognizes the authenticated session prior to logout. * **System Behavior:** Available scopes are displayed, and after authorization, the `Logout` button is enabled, indicating the session is active.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/91981e56-7705-442d-8ee3-c41f83e45154)  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f573002c-97b3-491f-86e2-c2e9aac7998e)  ---  ### Test 2 – Logout from Swagger  * **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criterion: *“The backend should redirect to the Microsoft B2C logout endpoint and clear any backend-managed session.”* * **System Behavior:** The `Authorize` button becomes available again, indicating that the session was cleared and the flow reset.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/72fce662-d895-4e0b-bcdf-b93037d765a6)  ---  ### Test 3 – Token reuse or unauthorized access after logout  * **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criterion “Token reuse prevention.” * **Current Observation:** If the token is missing, expired, or not re-authorized after logout, the request is rejected, and the system ensures protected routes remain inaccessible.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/74c6027d-6b4c-4bf1-b90f-7b54c6211892)

title: SPT-AU-002-002 Display error on login failure
author: andresMurillo23
assignees: ebai03, tatsparamo, cyaos, isabellarodriguezs, andresMurillo23, gaelalpizar
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-AU-002**  * **Name:** Authentication * **Description:** As part of the security infrastructure of ThemePark\@UCR, the system must implement user authentication using Microsoft Auth B2C. This ensures that only authorized individuals can access the system, enabling secure personalization, protection of data, and controlled access to features.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Display error on login failure  As a system user   I want to receive clear feedback if I fail to log in   So that I can understand what went wrong and retry accordingly ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Invalid credentials Given I enter incorrect credentials When I attempt to log in Then the system should reject the attempt And display a clear error message like "Invalid credentials. Please try again." ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Network or configuration error Given the authentication service is unavailable When I try to log in Then the system should display "Service temporarily unavailable" or a similar generic message ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements – Login Failure**  | Category          | Description                                                      | | ----------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Usability**     | Error messages must be clear and avoid exposing technical terms. | | **Security**      | Do not specify which part (email or password) was incorrect.     | | **Accessibility** | Messages must be screen-reader friendly and follow WCAG 2.1.     |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                             | Minutes | | ------------------------------------------------ | ------- | | Implement error message UI in frontend           | 20      | | Handle failed authentication callbacks           | 20      | | Write UX copy for common error messages          | 10      | | Simulate error cases in local/test environments  | 15      | | Write unit and integration tests for error cases | 25      | | Validate experience with PO                      | 10      | | PR and code review                               | 10      |  **Total estimate:** 110 minutes (\~1.8 hours)   ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 - Invalid credentials  - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “ the system should reject the attempt”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f8d5ff43-9870-477f-9f97-4d97ae1226a0)  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/efafcfb0-0bd7-45cb-939e-73a4698fd44e)  ---  ### Test 2 - Network or configuration error  - **Validation:** Satisfies the second acceptance criterion: “the system should display "Service temporarily unavailable" or a similar generic message”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c02dcf9b-4d6d-4310-8da9-f9ac1380a8c7)

title: SPT-AU-002-001 Validate Microsoft Auth B2C tokens in backend
author: andresMurillo23
assignees: ebai03, tatsparamo, cyaos, isabellarodriguezs, andresMurillo23, gaelalpizar
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-AU-002** * **Name:** Authentication * **Description:** As part of the security infrastructure of ThemePark\@UCR, the system must implement user authentication using Microsoft Auth B2C. This ensures that only authorized individuals can access the system, enabling secure personalization, protection of data, and controlled access to features. --- ### **User Story** ```gherkin Feature: Secure and seamless access to the system As a system user I want to sign in securely to the platform So that I can access the system’s protected features ``` --- ### **Acceptance Criteria** ```gherkin Scenario: Successful login Given I have valid Microsoft Auth B2C credentials When I attempt to log in Then the system should authenticate me And redirect me to the main dashboard ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Backend token validation Given I send a request with a valid token When the backend receives the request Then it should validate the token using B2C’s public signing keys And allow access to protected endpoints ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Invalid token request Given I send a request with an expired or invalid token When the backend processes the request Then it should return a 401 Unauthorized response ``` --- ### **Non-functional Requirements – Login** | Category | Description | | --------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Performance | The login process should complete in less than 2 seconds under normal load. | | Security | Tokens must be securely stored in the frontend and transmitted over HTTPS only. | | Portability | The login flow must work across web and mobile browsers. | | Maintainability | B2C tenant settings (client ID, issuer, redirect URI) should be configurable in both frontend and backend. | --- ### **Technical Tasks** | Task | Minutes | | -------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | Add JWT for B2C token validation | 20 | | Configure OpenID Connect metadata and issuer | 15 | | Implement role/claims extraction from JWT | 15 | | Secure API endpoints using `[Authorize]` attribute | 15 | | Add unit tests for protected endpoints | 15 | | Document token validation logic and config | 10 | | Code review and pull request | 10 | **Total estimate:** 100 minutes (~1.7 hours) Gracias, Elisa. Aquí tienes los tests documentados para esos **criterios de aceptación de autenticación con Azure AD B2C**, siguiendo el mismo formato que venías usando: --- ## Performed Tests ### Test 1 – Successful login via Microsoft B2C * **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criterion: *“Given I have valid Microsoft Auth B2C credentials, when I attempt to log in, then I should be authenticated and redirected to the dashboard.”* * **System Behavior:** The login flow is initiated via Azure AD B2C. Upon successful authentication, a valid ID token is issued and the user gains access to the endpoints depending on its role. --- ### Test 2 – Backend token validation * **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criterion: *“Given a valid token, when the backend receives a request, it should validate it using B2C’s signing keys and allow access.”* * **System Behavior:** The backend middleware uses the B2C OpenID metadata and signing keys to validate JWTs. Requests with valid tokens gain access to secured APIs or controllers.

title: SQL-FL-001-003 Delete Floor

title: SPT-UM-005-001 Create New Users
author: isabellarodriguezs
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-UM-005**  * **Name:** User Management * **Description:** ThemePark\@UCR requires a comprehensive user management system to allow administrators to create, update, and delete user accounts. This is essential for maintaining system integrity, ensuring that only authorized individuals have access, and keeping user data current and accurate.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Create new users  As an administrator   I want to create new users in the system from the admin module   So that I can grant them access and assign roles as needed ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Successfully create a user Given the administrator provides all required user information (e.g. name, email, role) When the creation form is submitted Then the system should create a new user profile And confirm that the user was successfully added ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Missing required fields Given the administrator leaves required fields empty When the creation attempt is made Then the system should prevent the operation And show validation error messages ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Username already in use Given another user already exists with the same username  When the administrator attempts to create a user with that username  Then the system should reject the creation And show a message like "Username already registered" ``` ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                                                                | | ------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Usability**       | The form must clearly indicate which fields are required.                  | | **Security**        | Only authorized administrators can create users.                           | | **Validation**      | The system must validate required fields both client-side and server-side. | | **Maintainability** | The logic must support adding new user fields in the future if needed.     |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                      | Minutes | | --------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | Design and implement user creation form in the UI         | 25      | | Implement backend logic to store user data                | 25      | | Add validations for required fields                       | 20      | | Integrate role assignment during user creation            | 15      | | Display confirmation message and handle errors gracefully | 15      | | Write unit and integration tests                          | 25      | | Document the user creation process                        | 10      | | Code review and PR                                        | 10      |  **Total estimate:** 145 minutes (\~2.4 hours) ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 – Successfully create a user  * **Result:** **200 OK** with a JSON response . * **Validation:** Satisfies the successful scenario from the acceptance criteria: *“Successfully create a user.”*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cc67c47a-5662-4c0b-9e2c-91e9920d99fc)  ### Test 2 – Missing required fields  * **Result:** **Error 400** with a JSON response  "Invalid UserName format. It must not contain spaces or uppercase letters and be less than 50 characters.". * **Validation:** Satisfies the rejection scenario from the acceptance criteria: *“Missing required fields.”*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/20b1635c-b4e9-44db-9ad8-0554c53c9956)  ### Test 3 – Username already in use  * **Result:** Logger warning with message "Attempt to create User with existing user name" * **Validation:** Satisfies the rejection scenario from the acceptance criteria: *“Username already in use.”*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ec27d614-127b-4787-b1f5-d4a0e8dbc77b)

title: SPT-UM-005-002 Modify User Information
author: isabellarodriguezs
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-UM-005**  **Name:** User Management **Description:** ThemePark\@UCR requires a comprehensive user management system to allow administrators to create, update, and delete user accounts. This is essential for maintaining system integrity, ensuring that only authorized individuals have access, and keeping user data current and accurate.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Modify user information  As an administrator   I want to modify the details of an existing user   So that I can keep their information accurate and up to date ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Successfully update user information Given a user exists in the system When the administrator edits their data and saves changes Then the updated information should be saved and reflected in the system ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Attempt to edit a non-existent user Given the administrator enters an invalid user ID When the edit is attempted Then the system should show an error indicating the user does not exist ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Attempt to update with invalid data Given the administrator provides data that fails validation (e.g. bad email format) When the form is submitted Then the system should reject the changes And show appropriate validation error messages ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                                                                | | ------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Usability**       | Editing fields must be intuitive and clearly indicate what can be changed. | | **Security**        | Only administrators can modify user data.                                  | | **Validation**      | Updated fields must pass validation rules before saving.                   | | **Auditability**    | Modifications must be logged with admin ID and timestamp.                  | | **Maintainability** | The update logic must support partial and full edits.                      |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                    | Minutes | | ------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | Implement UI form for editing user information          | 25      | | Create backend method to handle user updates            | 25      | | Validate data before saving changes                     | 15      | | Handle "user not found" case with proper error messages | 15      | | Ensure updated data is correctly stored and retrieved   | 20      | | Write unit and integration tests                        | 25      | | Document the update process and edge cases              | 10      | | Code review and PR                                      | 10      |  **Total estimate:** 145 minutes (\~2.4 hours)   ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 - Successful update user information.  - **Result:** 200 OK with successful modify. - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “the system should save a new person record in the database and should return a success response”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fe5d781c-9f22-4304-ad45-1ef0ba7dba9f)  ---  ### Test 2 - Attempt to edit a non-existent user.  - **Result:** 404 Not Found with appropriate error message. - **Validation:** Satisfies the second acceptance criterion: “the system should show an error indicating the user does not exist”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/60cc4dbf-6fff-4e42-bf92-eb586881f374)  ---  ### Test 3 - Attempt to update with invalid data (invalid username).  - **Result:** 400 Bad Request with detailed validation errors:   - `username`: Must be lowercase, alphanumeric, and may include underscores only. - **Validation:** Satisfies the third acceptance criterion: “the system should reject the changes and show appropriate validation error messages”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f19d49df-2983-458d-88e4-f405272b8369)  ---  ### Test 4 - Attempt to update username with uppercase letters.  - **Result:** 400 Bad Request with message `"Username must be lowercase only"`. - **Validation:** Validates lowercase-only requirement for usernames as per new constraints.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5ab630f2-a802-4081-925e-dbc64589a851)

title: SPT-RM-003-001 Assign Roles to Users
author: isabellarodriguezs
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-RM-003**  **Name:** Role Management **Description:** To support granular access control within ThemePark\@UCR, the system must allow the management of roles. This includes creating, assigning, and removing roles, enabling administrators to define permissions and ensure that users only have access to the functionalities relevant to their responsibilities.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Assign roles to users   As an administrator   I want to assign existing roles to users   So that I can control their access to specific system functionalities ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Assign a valid role to a user Given the system contains predefined roles and a list of registered users When the administrator assigns a valid role to a user Then the role should be successfully linked to the user And the user should gain the corresponding permissions ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Attempt to assign a non-existent role Given the administrator tries to assign a role that doesn't exist When the assignment is attempted Then the system should display an error And prevent the operation ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Role assignment is updated Given a user already has a role assigned When the administrator changes the user's role Then the new role should replace the previous one And the user's permissions should be updated accordingly ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Assign the same role that the user already has Given a user already has a role assigned When the administrator attempts to reassign the same role Then the system should either ignore the operation Or confirm that the user already has that role ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                                                               | | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Security**        | Only administrators can assign or change roles.                           | | **Reliability**     | The system must accurately reflect role changes in real-time.             | | **Auditability**    | All role changes should be logged with user, timestamp, and action taken. | | **Usability**       | Role assignment interface must clearly indicate current role and changes. | | **Maintainability** | The role assignment logic should be easily extendable for future roles.   |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                  | Minutes | | ----------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | Define role-permission mappings in database or config | 20      | | Implement role assignment logic in backend            | 30      | | Validate role existence before assignment             | 15      | | Implement UI for assigning/changing roles             | 30      | | Ensure updated permissions are reflected immediately  | 15      | | Write tests for valid/invalid/updated assignments     | 30      | | Document the assignment flow and access changes       | 10      | | Code review and PR                                    | 10      |  **Total estimate:** 160 minutes (\~2.7 hours)  ---  #### **Test 1 – Assign a valid role to a user**  * **Input:** Valid `userId`, valid `roleId` not yet assigned * **Expected:**    * Returns `200 OK` or `204 No Content`   * Role is added to user’s list of roles   * Permissions from the new role are added to user’s permission set  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c4685e60-90b4-48b7-b084-32029340bf92)  ---  #### **Test 2 – Assign a role the user already has**  * **Input:** Valid `userId`, `roleId` already assigned * **Expected:**    * Returns `200 OK` or `204 No Content`   * No duplicate in user’s role list   * Response may include message like `"Role already assigned"`  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/85b4caa4-16f7-4a48-a90a-1debd77b3107)  ---  #### **Test 3 – Attempt to assign a non-existent role**  * **Input:** Valid `userId`, invalid `roleId` * **Expected:**    * Returns `404 Not Found`   * No changes made to user’s roles  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/77675ce3-2e46-477f-a575-27cb50d4e3fe)  ---  #### **Test 4 – Assign a role to a non-existent user**  * **Input:** Invalid `userId`, valid `roleId` * **Expected:**    * Returns `404 Not Found`   * No role assignment performed  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/97bbf8de-bdb5-4c50-994f-469a2596d178)

title: SPT-RM-003-002 Delete Role
author: isabellarodriguezs
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-RM-003**  * **Name:** Role Management * **Description:** To support granular access control within ThemePark\@UCR, the system must allow the management of roles. This includes creating, assigning, and removing roles, enabling administrators to define permissions and ensure that users only have access to the functionalities relevant to their responsibilities.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Delete Role  As an administrator   I want to delete roles that are no longer needed   So that I can keep the role structure clean and manageable ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Successfully delete an unused role Given the role is not currently assigned to any user When the administrator deletes the role Then the role should be removed from the system And no longer be available for assignment ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Delete a role in use Given the role is currently assigned to one or more users When the administrator tries to delete it Then the system should allow the action ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Attempt to delete a non-existent role Given the role ID does not exist in the system When the administrator attempts to delete it Then the system should return a not found error And inform the administrator that the role does not exist ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements – Delete Role**  | Category validation rules.                  |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                    | Minutes | | ------------------------------------------------------- | blocked deletions           | 30      | | Document deletion process and edge cases                | 10      | | Code review and PR                                      | 10      |  **Total estimate:** title: SPT-PA-004-003 Modify Permissions of a Role
author: isabellarodriguezs
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-PA-004**  * **Name:** Permission Assignment * **Description:** In order to ensure flexible and fine-grained access control, ThemePark\@UCR must provide functionality for assigning, modifying, and visualizing permissions associated with roles. This enables administrators to define exactly what actions each role can perform, maintaining both security and efficiency.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Add or remove permissions from a role  As an administrator   I want to add or remove permissions to/from a role   So that I can adjust user access based on updated requirements ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Successfully add a permission to a role Given a valid permission and a valid role When the administrator adds the permission to the role Then the permission should be added successfully And users with the role should gain access to the corresponding functionality ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Successfully remove a permission from a role Given a role with existing permissions When the administrator removes a permission Then the permission should no longer be associated with the role And users with the role should lose access to the corresponding functionality ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Attempt to modify a non-existent role or permission Given the administrator tries to modify a role or permission that doesn’t exist When the operation is attempted Then the system should reject the action And display an appropriate error message ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                                                                  | | ------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Security**        | Only administrators can modify role permissions.                             | | **Consistency**     | Changes should propagate immediately to affected users.                      | | **Auditability**    | All permission changes must be logged with timestamp and admin ID.           | | **Usability**       | The system must clearly show what permissions were added or removed.         | | **Maintainability** | The logic must allow for scalable changes as new permissions are introduced. |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                         | Minutes | | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ------- | | Implement backend logic for adding/removing permissions      | 25      | | Validate role and permission existence before modification   | 15      | | Update UI to allow selection and deselection of permissions  | 25      | | Apply changes to permission-role associations in database    | 15      | | Ensure user access reflects updated permissions in real-time | 15      | | Write unit and integration tests                             | 30      | | Document functionality and usage examples                    | 10      | | Code review and PR                                           | 10      |  **Total estimate:** 145 minutes (\~2.4 hours)  --- ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 – Successfully add a permission to a role  * **Input:** Valid role ID and permission ID (not yet assigned) * **Expected:** `200 OK` and permission appears assigned to the role  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7c30817c-97f5-4c7a-8f79-86a2d7bc74e7)  ---  ### Test 2 – Successfully remove a permission from a role  * **Input:** Valid role ID and permission ID currently assigned * **Expected:** `200 OK` and permission no longer assigned to the role  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9e757b4a-f395-458d-8a4f-8ab923ecb6d3)  ---  ### Test 3 – Add permission to a non-existent role  * **Input:** Non-existent role ID, valid permission ID * **Expected:** `404 Not Found` with `"Role not found"` error  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/566c83ad-12eb-469e-8418-8f2db71a4710)  ---  ### Test 4 – Add non-existent permission to a role  * **Input:** Valid role ID, non-existent permission ID * **Expected:** `404 Not Found` with `"Permission not found"` error  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/848e7c3c-0077-471e-9efc-05f3e50e4f63)  ---  ### Test 5 – Remove permission not assigned to a role  * **Input:** Valid role ID, permission ID not assigned * **Expected:** `200 OK` (or `409 Conflict`) and no change  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bcc7cbd0-b97f-4da5-86f8-f02958b330e2)

title: SPT-PA-004-001 View Permissions Assigned to Role
author: isabellarodriguezs
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-PA-004**  * **Name:** Permission Assignment * **Description:** In order to ensure flexible and fine-grained access control, ThemePark\@UCR must provide functionality for assigning, modifying, and visualizing permissions associated with roles. This enables administrators to define exactly what actions each role can perform, maintaining both security and efficiency.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: View permissions assigned to a role  As an administrator   I want to retrieve the list of permissions assigned to a specific role   So that I can review and manage them easily ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Role has assigned permissions Given a role with one or more permissions assigned When the administrator requests to view its permissions Then the system should display all permissions assigned to that role ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Role has no permissions Given a role without any assigned permissions When the administrator requests to view its permissions Then the system should show a message indicating no permissions are assigned ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Invalid role ID provided Given the administrator provides a role ID that is zero or negative When the system attempts to retrieve its permissions Then the system should return a "RoleId must be a positive number." error message And no permissions should be displayed ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                                                                  | | ------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Performance**     | The system should return the list of permissions in under 1 second.          | | **Usability**       | The interface must present permissions in a readable and organized manner.   | | **Security**        | Only users with permission to manage roles may access this information.      | | **Maintainability** | The logic must allow for easy extension when new permission types are added. |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                  | Minutes | | ----------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | Implement backend method to retrieve role permissions | 25      | | Validate role existence and access rights             | 15      | | Design UI layout to display permissions clearly       | 20      | | Handle empty result set with appropriate messaging    | 10      | | Write unit and integration tests                      | 30      | | Document endpoint and usage scenarios                 | 10      | | Code review and PR                                    | 10      |  **Total estimate:** 120 minutes (\~2 hours)  ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 – Role has assigned permissions  * **Result:** `200 OK` with a JSON response containing a list of permission objects (e.g., `["Manage Permissions", "Edit Events", "Delete User"]`) * **Validation:** Satisfies the successful scenario from the acceptance criteria: *“Role has assigned permissions.”*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a1e55d9a-191e-4701-8b67-ba9b8807767e)  ---  ### Test 2 – Role has no permissions  * **Result:** `200 OK` with a JSON message: `"No permissions found for role with ID {roleId}."` * **Validation:** Satisfies the scenario from the acceptance criteria: *“Role has no permissions.”*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ed7c3d4d-4a05-409f-b631-091aa45d9bb3)  ---  ### Test 3 – Invalid role ID provided  * **Result:** `400 Bad Request` with a JSON message: `"RoleId must be a positive number."` * **Validation:** Satisfies the validation scenario where the role ID is either `0` or negative. The system correctly identifies the input as invalid and returns a `400` error without attempting to retrieve permissions.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1b9084f9-580a-49e7-a9d4-5b51576088b2)

title: SPT-UI-006-004 User maintenance window
author: isabellarodriguezs
assignees: ebai03, tatsparamo, cyaos, isabellarodriguezs, andresMurillo23, gaelalpizar
body: ### EPIC ID: SPT-UI-006 - **Name:** User Interface - **Description:** ThemePark@UCR must provide comprehensive tools for user management. This includes an interface to view, create, modify, and delete users, with role and permission control from a single window. --- ### User Story ```gherkin Feature: User maintenance window As an administrator I want to view a list of all users and take actions like adding or inspecting them So that I can manage user access efficiently from a central interface ``` --- ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: View user list Given the admin is logged in When they access the user maintenance window Then they should see a scrollable list of users with: Username, Role, Email, FirstName, LastName, Phone, BirthDate, IdentityNumber ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Open user details Given the user table is visible When the admin clicks a Username Then the user details window for that user should open ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Add new user Given the admin is logged in When the user management view is displayed Then there should be a clearly labeled “Add user” button And clicking it should open the create user window ``` ```gherkin Scenario: View breadcrumbs for easy navigation Given the admin is logged in When the admin is on the user management view Then a breadcrumbs element should clearly reflect where the admin is ``` --- ### Non-functional Requirements | Category | Description | | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Usability** | Table must be readable and scrollable if too many users. | | **Security** | Only admins can access this window. | | **Responsiveness** | Must be usable on desktops and tablets; scrolling for long lists. | | **Performance** | The user list must load in under 1 second for up to 100 users. | --- ### Technical Tasks Task | Minutes -- | -- Build user management view with table | 60 Bind backend data to table | 30 Add "Add user" button and modal trigger | 20 Implement username click to open details | 25 Implement User with Person for joint information | 60 Add breadcrumbs and styling | 20 Write tests and documentation | 20 Review and merge PR | 5 **Total estimate:** 240 minutes (4 hours) Here are the test cases following your requested format: --- ## **Performed Tests (UI Validation)** ### **Test 1 – Admin access to user list** * **Result:** User list displaying columns: UserId, Username, Full Name, PersonId, Email, Phone Number, Identity Number, Birthdate and Roles * **Validation:** Satisfies AC: *"admin should see a scrollable list of users with specified fields"* ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f6424c6c-370f-4c4f-bba4-918689725c53) ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2c49eb25-80fe-4f29-b8fd-3665d5df66f4) --- ### **Test 2 – User details view** * **Result:** Details modal opens showing full user profile when clicking any Username * **Validation:** Meets AC: *"user details window should open on click"* ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/551e51ea-d0f5-427b-accd-bda966e8f694) --- ### **Test 3 – Add user button visibility** * **Result:** "Add user" button present and clickable in top-right corner * **Validation:** Verifies AC: *"clearly labeled button should open create window"* --- ### **Test 4 – Breadcrumbs navigation** * **Result:** Displays path: "HOME / USER MANAGEMENT" * **Validation:** Satisfies AC: *"breadcrumbs should reflect current location"* --- ### **Test 5 – Empty state handling** * **Result:** Displays "No users found" message when list is empty * **Validation:** Validates edge case handling ---

title: SPT-UI-006-005 Edit user via page window
author: isabellarodriguezs
assignees: ebai03, tatsparamo, cyaos, isabellarodriguezs, andresMurillo23, gaelalpizar
body: ### EPIC ID: SPT-UI-006  - **Name:** User Interface   - **Description:** To facilitate user management, ThemePark@UCR must allow user data to be modified through an intuitive pop-up window that is triggered from the main user maintenance window.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Edit user via page window  As an administrator   I want to edit the details of a user in a display page window.   So that I can update user data. ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: Open page with prefilled user data Given the admin clicks the “Edit” button for a user Then a display page should open And it should show the following pre-filled fields: Email, FirstName, LastName, Phone, BirthDate, IdentityNumber, Role assignment ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Modify and save user data Given the open page and the admin changes one or more fields When the admin clicks “Save” Then the user data should be validated and updated in the system And a success message should be shown ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Cancel editing Given the page is open When the admin clicks “Cancel” Then the page should close and go back to the previous page without making changes ```  ---  ### Non-functional Requirements   | Category           | Description                                                              | | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | **Usability**      | Page information must be centered and clearly show all editable fields.             | | **Security**       | Only authenticated admins can trigger the page and modify data.         | | **Validation**     | All fields must be validated (e.g., email format, phone number).         | | **Responsiveness** | Display page must be usable on tablets and adapt to screen size.                | | **Feedback**       | Must show clear messages for success, errors, and invalid inputs.        | | **Accessibility**  | Should support keyboard navigation and screen readers.                   |  ---  ### Technical Tasks  | Task                                                              | Minutes | | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | Design and implement modal page UI                               | 15      | | Prefill data from backend or parent context                       | 15      | | Implement editable form with validation (frontend)                | 20      | | Connect form with backend PUT/PATCH API for updates               | 20      | | Handle success and error responses with user feedback             | 10      | | Add loading indicators and disable buttons while saving           | 5       | | Write unit tests and integration tests for page interaction      | 15      | | Document expected API payload and behavior                        | 10      | | Code review and PR                                                | 10      |  **Total estimate:** 120 minutes (~2 hours)  ## **Performed Tests (User Edit Functionality)**  ### **Test 1 – Edit window opens with prefilled data**   * **Result:** Modal displays with all user fields pre-populated: Email, FirstName, LastName, Phone, BirthDate, IdentityNumber, Role   * **Validation:** Satisfies AC: *"display page should show pre-filled fields"*    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/241da696-b02b-4572-87a9-bb21332beedb)  ---  ### **Test 2 – Successful user data update**   * **Result:**     - `200 OK` response     - Toast message: "User jdoe updated successfully"     - Parent list refreshes automatically   * **Validation:** Confirms AC: *"user data should be validated and updated"*    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4ba5b705-c29d-46b6-b765-d2c52a5c4564)  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8c59090c-c949-4280-ac42-8b113be53720)  ---  ### **Test 3 – Cancel editing flow**   * **Result:** Modal closes without API calls, original data remains unchanged   * **Validation:** Verifies AC: *"no changes when canceled"*    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e9109d23-06f5-4b7b-a7e3-d397365d52f4)  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8a46b02b-9192-46c1-a528-816fd96aa6b7)  ---  ### **Test 4 – Field validation enforcement**   * **Result:**     - Invalid email: "Enter a valid email address" (blocks submission)     - Invalid username   - Invalid phone number * **Validation:** Meets validation requirement    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5110031b-00f9-4b35-bcec-962ab47c0ae1)  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f0c45390-1996-4179-b5cd-036404b53141)  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4bf36391-3c7a-45c3-adf0-7e329af3bcfc)

title: SQL-LS-001-005 Delete Learning Spaces
author: andervar
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-001  **Name**: Learning spaces management  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow users to explore and manipulate data related to learning spaces. This includes the ability to list available spaces, view details of a specific space, update existing records, create new entries, and retrieve relevant information.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Delete learning spaces no longer in use  As an administrator user   I want to be able to delete a learning space   So that I can keep the system data clean and up to date ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: Successfully delete an existing learning space Given I am on the administrative module of the system And the learning space exists in the database When I confirm the deletion of the learning space Then the system deletes the record and confirms the deletion ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Attempt to delete a non-existing learning space Given I am on the administrative module of the system And the learning space does not exist When I try to delete it Then the system shows an error indicating the space was not found ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements – Delete Learning Spaces**  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | The system should delete a learning space within 1 second under normal load. | | **Usability**    | The deletion action should require user confirmation and display clear success or failure messages. | | **Maintainability** | The deletion logic should be modular and include validations to prevent cascading data issues. | | **Reliability**  | Deletion operations must be transaction-safe and leave the system in a consistent state. |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks – Delete Learning Spaces**  | Task                                                                 | Minutes | |----------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Clone the repository                                                 | 5       | | Create a new branch for the feature                                  | 3       | | Refine and validate business requirements with the Product Owner     | 15      | | Ensure data model supports logical/physical deletion                 | 10      | | Implement deletion in repository layer (interface and implementation)| 15      | | Write database method to delete learning spaces                | 20      | | Review dependency with related tables (e.g., learning components )              | 15      | | Implement validation rules for deletion   | 30      | | **Implement logic in Application, Presentation, and WebAPI layers** |         | |     - Application layer (business logic)         | 45      | |     - Presentation layer (API gateway logic)     | 60      | |     - WebAPI controller class (delete endpoint)  | 10      | | Ensure successful compilation across all layers                      | 20      | | Apply Clean Code principles throughout the implementation            | 10      | | Preserve architectural integrity of the solution                     | 10      | | Set up CI integration to main branch and handle PR                   | 30      | | Update documentation and ensure backlog is current                   | 10      | | Ensure all Acceptance Criteria are met and validated by the PO       | 45      |  **Total estimate (minutes):** 418   **Total estimate (hours):** 6.96  --- ## Performed Manual Tests   ### Test Cases  **Test 1 – Successful deletion of a floor**   - **Result**:  204 Status Code (No Content) - **Validation**: A valid learning space is selected; user confirms the deletion; the space is removed from the database; system returns a 204 No Content response and displays a success message.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cfdb8cb3-de62-4b45-887e-610a3279db90)

title: SPT-UM-005-004 List All Users
author: tatsparamo
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-UM-005**  * **Name:** User Management * **Description:** ThemePark\@UCR requires a comprehensive user management system to allow administrators to create, update, and delete user accounts. This is essential for maintaining system integrity, ensuring that only authorized individuals have access, and keeping user data current and accurate.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: List all users  As an administrator   I want to view a list of all registered users   So that I can manage accounts efficiently ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Display all users Given that the administrator accesses the user management module   When the "List Users" view is opened   Then the system should display a list of all users with relevant information (e.g. name, email, role)  ```  ```gherkin Scenario: No users in the system Given that there are no registered users   When the "List Users" view is opened   Then the system should display a message like "No users found"  ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | Retrieving the list of registered users should take no longer than 1 second under normal system load. | | **Security**     | Only authenticated and authorized users (e.g., administrators) can access the list of registered users. | | **Reliability**  | The endpoint should return accurate and complete data in 99.9% of valid cases. | | **Maintainability** | The user retrieval logic should be modular and support future extensions such as filtering or pagination. | | **Portability**  | The endpoint should function consistently across all supported deployment environments. |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task | Minutes | |------|---------| | Validate requirements and acceptance criteria with the PO | 10 | | Ensure test database has registered people for testing | 10 | | Implement retrieval logic in the application layer | 30 | | Implement method in the user repository (infrastructure layer) | 25 | | Handle empty list scenario with appropriate response | 20 | | Write unit and integration tests for success and empty cases | 35 | | Create pull request and request code review | 10 | | Validate with PO that the functionality meets expectations | 15 |   **Total estimate:** 140 minutes (\~2.3 hours) --- ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 –  Display all users  * **Result:** 200 OK with a JSON response containing multiple users * **Validation:** Satisfies the successful scenario from the acceptance criteria: *“Display all users.”*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7e328786-5466-4f5d-ab26-68d809a93ce8)  ### Test 2 –  No users in the system  * **Result:** 200 OK with a JSON response "There are no registered users." * **Validation:** Satisfies the successful scenario from the acceptance criteria: *“No users in the system.”*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/631bc407-92d4-4c94-9b49-d3ae187e7ff6)

title: PQL-AE-001-007 Display specific university
author: camsko

```gherkin   Scenario: View information of an university that does not exist     Given an university that does not exist in the system     When a user enters a nonexistent name     Then the system will display an error ```  ## Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs)  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | university in the system through a GUI   As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want to be able to use a friendly GUI to manage universities, so that I can more easily maintain an updated record of the universities available in the system. ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator clicks a button and the add screen is displayed   Given the administrator is on the university management window   When the administrator clicks the "Add University" button   Then the screen changes to add mode    And the fields needed to add a new university are displayed and administrator adds the university information   And clicks the "Save" button   Then the information is saved and the university is addded to the system   And a confirmation message and the university title: SPT-RM-003-003 List All Roles
author: tatsparamo
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-RM-003**  * **Name:** Role Management * **Description:** To support granular access control within ThemePark\@UCR, the system must allow the management of roles. This includes creating, assigning, and removing roles, enabling administrators to define permissions and ensure that users only have access to the functionalities relevant to their responsibilities.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: List Roles  As an administrator   I want to view all the existing roles in the system   So that I can manage and assign them appropriately ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: List all roles Given roles exist in the system   When the administrator accesses the role management  Then the system should display a list of all roles ```  ```gherkin Scenario: No roles exist Given no roles are registered in the system   When the administrator accesses the role management  Then the system should indicate that no roles are currently available ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | Retrieving the list of roles should take no longer than 1 second under normal system load. | | **Security**        | Only users with admin privileges may access the role listing.  | | **Reliability**  | The endpoint should return accurate and complete data in 99.9% of valid cases. | | **Maintainability** | The role retrieval logic should be modular and support future extensions such as filtering or pagination. | | **Portability**  | The endpoint should function consistently across all supported deployment environments. |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task | Minutes | |------|---------| | Validate requirements and acceptance criteria with the PO | 10 | | Ensure test database has registered people for testing | 10 | | Implement retrieval logic in the application layer | 30 | | Implement method in the user repository (infrastructure layer) | 25 | | Handle empty list scenario with appropriate response | 20 | | Write unit and integration tests for success and empty cases | 35 | | Create pull request and request code review | 10 | | Validate with PO that the functionality meets expectations | 15 |   **Total estimate:** 140 minutes (\~2.3 hours)  --- ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 –  List all roles  * **Result:** 200 OK with a JSON response containing multiple roles * **Validation:** Satisfies the successful scenario from the acceptance criteria: *“List all roles.”*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ecb56237-f23f-4535-90f7-c11053ce2bb8)  ### Test 2 –  No roles exist  * **Result:** 200 OK with a JSON response "There are no registered roles." * **Validation:** Satisfies the rejection scenario from the acceptance criteria: *“No roles exist.”*  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f9d74dbd-adba-4418-9c02-de008a3d6a1f)

title: CPD-LC-001-008 Delete any learning component
author: luis-fch
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-LC-001 Name: Learning components Description: This epic is about adding components to learning spaces in ThemePark@UCR, like classrooms and labs. These components can be things like whiteboards, chairs, or projectors that users can interact with, so that learning feels more active and engaging. It also focuses on building the functionality to create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) these objects in the system.   ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Delete a learning component. As an administrator of ThemePark@UCR I want to delete a learning component So that I can have an up to date representation of the components in a learning space. ```  ---   ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: Delete a given learning component Given a valid learning component identifier When the administrator wants to delete the component Then the learning component is deleted from their corresponding learning space  ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Delete a learning component that does not exist Given an invalid identifier for the learning component When the administrator wants to delete a learning component that does not exist Then an error message is shown ```  ### Technical Tasks  Task | Minutes -- | -- Validation with the Product Owner | 20 Update domain model to support generic component deletion | 15 Modify repository interface to accept any component type | 10 Refactor repository implementation for generic deletion | 25 Update application service/use case to handle any component type | 20 Modify API endpoint to accept generic component identifier | 15 Update request/response DTOs to support generic component | 10 Code review and refactoring cleanup | 15 Documentation updates for API endpoints | 10  **Total estimate (minutes):** 135 **Total estimate (hours):** 2.25  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 – Delete a component that exists  * **Validation:** The learning component is eliminated.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f74ae206-51ff-45c3-a789-6bc3cb533ead)  ### Test 2 – Delete a component that not exists  * **Validation:** A response message is shown indicating that there is no component with that id.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/70807c5c-63ff-4456-b6ea-d3cd2bbcad6a)

title: SQL-LS-002-001 List Learning Spaces via web interface
author: RolandoVillavicencio013
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-002  **Name**: Learning Spaces Web Interface.  **Description:** As part of the development of the learning spaces module, the system must provide a user-friendly web interface with essential functionalities, including listing, viewin, upgdating, creating, and deleting records. It must also support data registration and import through forms. In parallel, initial permission control and security mechanisms will be implemented to support scalability.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: List Learning Spaces via web interface  As an administrator user, I want to view a list of learning spaces in a specific building through a web interface, So that I can easily identify which spaces are available. ```  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: Learning spaces are available   Given there are learning spaces registered for a building   When I access the GUI and request the list   Then the system displays a list of available learning spaces, each showing its name, the floo where is located, its type and a link to see its components. ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: No learning spaces available   Given there are no learning spaces registered for a building   When I access the GUI and request the list   Then the system displays a message: *"There aren't available learning spaces"* in the listing area ```  ### Initial Wireframes:  Learning spaces are available  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0a0be2ee-5707-4d91-a771-dc295054c566)  No learning spaces available  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9c28e679-36d1-4fb8-befb-ba2ee290409f)  ### Non functional Requirements   | **Category**      | **Description**                                                                                     | |--------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | **Reliability**    | Error handling must properly log errors and provide consistent error responses for debugging purposes.                    | | **Maintainability**| The backend and Frontend codebase must adhere to Clean Code principles and follow SOLID design principles. | | **Documentation**  | OpenAPI documentation must be automatically generated for all endpoints.                           | | **Usability**      | API endpoints must follow RESTful conventions for easy integration. |  ### Technical Tasks  | **Task**                             | **Time (Minutes)** | |--------------------------------------|--------------------| | **Validate Requirements with the Product Owner (PO)** | 20                 | | **Setup Development Environment**    | 10                 | | **Define Domain Entities**           | 10                | | **Create or Update Interfaces**      | 15                 | | **Implement Use Case for "Read Learning Spaces"** | 45                 | | **Map Domain Models to DTOs**        | 5                 | | **Implement dummy implementation**        | 10                 | | **Implement Repository Logic**       | 60                 | | **Setup Fluent API entity configurations*           | 30                 | | **Create API Handler and Endpoint**   | 60                 | | **Handle Input Parameters and Validation** | 20                 | | **Return Proper HTTP Responses**     | 5                 | | **Update OpenAPI Documentation**     | 20                 | | **Update Backlog**                   | 15                 | | **Follow Clean Code Principles**     | 20                 | | **Perform Code Reviews**             | 30                 | | **Deploy to Staging Environment**    | 30                 |  **Total estimate (minutes):** 625 **Total estimate (hours):** 10  ## Performed Manual Tests   Test 1 – List Learning Spaces when available      Precondition: There are learning spaces registered for a specific building.      Action: Access the GUI and request the list of learning spaces for the building.      Validation: The interface displays a list of available learning spaces. Each entry shows the name, floor, type, and includes a link to view components.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d6498810-3577-4c8b-9622-98a5aac4b375)  ---  Test 2 – List Learning Spaces when none are available      Precondition: There are no learning spaces registered for the selected floor.      Action: Access the GUI and request the list of learning spaces for the floor.      Expected Result: The interface displays the message: "No hay espacios de aprendizaje registrados en este piso." in the listing area.      Validation: The message appears correctly in the UI, with no broken layout or loading issues.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/884d9a21-e145-4f04-83af-307196f411d8)  ---  Test 3 – Try to list learning spaces in a nonexistent building      Precondition: The user enters or selects a building/floor identifier that doesn't exist in the system.      Action: Use the GUI to attempt to list learning spaces for a nonexistent building.      Expected Result: The GUI displays an error message: "No se encontró el edificio solicitado.".      Validation: The error message is displayed in the UI without breaking the layout or other interactive elements.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2643718c-fbf5-4be9-ae37-606ddb8a4301)  ---  Test 4 – Invalid building ID format      Precondition: The user enters a building ID with an incorrect value (negative number, etc)      Action: Try to list learning spaces through the GUI using an invalid building ID.      Expected Result: The GUI displays an error message: "No se encontró el edificio solicitado.".  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/87b71cac-1841-4502-a798-c1115d2da17a)

author: andervar
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-002  **Name**: Learning Spaces Interface  **Description:** As part of the development of the learning spaces module, the system must provide a user-friendly web interface with essential functionalities, including listing, viewing, updating, creating, and deleting records. It must also support data registration and import through forms. In parallel, initial permission control and security mechanisms will be implemented to support scalability.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Create Learning Spaces via web interface  As an administrator user,   I want to be able to create a learning space with defined dimensional specifications, maximum capacity and type through a web interface,   So that it accurately reflects the actual physical infrastructure of the institution. ```  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9d55ab6c-20a4-4a88-b679-5c2758e25b65)  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: Successfully create a new learning space Given that I provide valid and complete learning space information When I submit the required data to create a learning space (max capacity, type, height, width, length) Then the system responds with the message "¡El espacio de aprendizaje se ha creado exitosamente!" ``` Scenario: Successfully delete an existing floor from the web interface Given I am on the web interface of the administrative module   And the floor exists in the system   When I confirm the deletion of the floor   Then the system deletes the floor along with its associated learning spaces and components, and shows a success confirmation ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Attempt to delete a non-existing floor from the web interface Given I am on the web interface of the administrative module   And the floor does not exist   When I attempt to delete the floor   Then the system shows an error message indicating the floor was not found ```  ### Wireframes:  Successfully delete an existing floor from the web interface  1.
