title: SQL-LS-002-004 Update Learning Spaces via web interface
author: andervar
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-002  **Name**: Learning Spaces Web Interface  **Description:** As part of the development of the learning spaces module, the system must provide a user-friendly web interface with essential functionalities, including listing, viewing, updating, creating, and deleting records. It must also support data registration and import through forms. In parallel, initial permission control and security mechanisms will be implemented to support scalability.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Update Learning Spaces via web interface  As an administrator user,   I want to be able to update the details of an existing learning space through a web interface,   So that the system reflects any physical changes or corrections in the institution's layout. ``` ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Given that the administrator user accesses the edit interface for an existing learning space,   When they update the space's details with valid input values and click the update button,   Then the updated information must be correctly reflected in the database,   And a confirmation message must be displayed saying "The learning space was successfully updated",   And the interface must remain user-friendly and easy to understand. ``` #### Example: ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f861ba91-0530-4803-a716-20a0d05de07e)  ```gherkin Given that the administrator user attempts to update a learning space,   When one or more fields contain invalid input such as:   - missing required fields,   - incorrect data types,   - unselected options,   Then no changes must be made to the database,   And the system must display an error message clearly indicating the issue (e.g., "Invalid quantity", "Invalid name", "Type not selected"),   And the interface must allow the user to easily correct the errors. ``` #### Example: ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a150b37a-29a0-4713-b266-03c97996c2a8) ### Non-functional Requirements | Category            | Description                                                                                                        | | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | **Performance**     | The interface should complete updates within 3 seconds under typical usage scenarios.                              | | **Reliability**     | The system must accurately indicate whether the update was successful or not at least 99.9% of the time.           | | **Usability**       | The update form should be easy to use, with clear feedback and validation messages for the user.                   | | **Maintainability** | The codebase should follow clean design principles, allowing future updates to be implemented with minimal effort. | | **Portability**     | The update functionality should behave consistently across all major browsers and devices.                         |  ---  ###  **Technical Tasks** | **Task**                                                                 | **Minutes** | | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------- | | Clarify requirements and acceptance criteria with the Product Owner (PO) | 30          | | Clone the repository to the local development environment                | 10          | | Design the database changes and domain entities                          | 50          | | Create PBIs and organize the remote repository structure                 | 30          | | Update and migrate the database schema                                   | 50          | | Implement the Kiota repository update method                             | 40          | | Research the functionality and integration of Kiota                      | 20          | | Translate key system terms from English to Spanish                       | 10          | | Implement the frontend update form UI                                    | 90          | | Implement error validation and display in the UI                         | 50          | | Configure API endpoints or Blazor service for the update functionality   | 10          | | Set up continuous integration and review pull requests                   | 20          | | Apply coding standards and clean architecture principles                 | 10          | | Document the update functionality and update the project backlog         | 5           | | Validate that acceptance criteria are met with the PO                    | 20          |  ---  ###  **Total Time**  | Total Minutes | Total Hours     | | ------------- | --------------- | | 505 minutes   | 8 hours 25 mins |  ## Performed Manual Tests  ### Test 1 – Updating a learning space with multiple invalid inputs  * **Result:** **400 Bad Request** with one or multiple error messages indicating invalid or missing input data. * **Validation:**   Multiple invalid inputs were entered simultaneously to verify the update form validation:  - **Name:** empty, and with more than 100 characters. - **Type:** don't have a wrong input. - **Max capacity:** negative value (`-50`). - **Dimensions (height, width, length):** with a negative numbers (`-4`), and text instead of numbers.  The system responded correctly with validation errors on all invalid fields and did not allow the update submission.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/63f7e722-9e72-40a4-a1eb-9c0fa6c98760) --- ### Test 2 – Updating a learning space with non-existent IDs  * **Result:** **404 Not Found** with an error message indicating that the learning space ID   not exist. * **Validation:**   The update request was made using learning space IDs that are not present in the database (e.g., IDs `9999`, `-1`, or `abc123`).   The system correctly responded with a 404 error and a message stating that the specified learning space was not found, preventing any update operation. ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7fcaf8d5-bfba-4546-a6c5-26f99589ff92)  ---

title: PQL-WV-004-003 Add Building Window
author: AleJimenezRJ
body: ### EPIC ID: PQL-WV-004  **Name**: Add Building Window  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow administrators to add buildings in the system through a window to have an accessible way of adding new buildings to the university infrastucture.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Add  a new building in the system through a window.  As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want to be able to use a friendly GUI to add buildings, so that I can more easily maintain an updated record of the buildings available in the system. ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator clicks a button to add a building   Given the administrator has displayed a building they have permission to add   When the administrator clicks the "Add" button   Then the screen changes to the add building screen   And fields appear to add the information of the buillding   And there are "Save" and "Cancel" buttons on the screen.  ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator adds the building information and saves changes   Given the administrator has added all the building information   When clicks the "Save" button   Then the building is saved   And the screen returns to the list of buildings with the newly added building. ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator cancels an add operation   Given the administrator is in add mode   When the administrator clicks the "Cancel" button   Then the changes are discarded, the building is not added   And then the system returns to the list of buildings screen ```  ## Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs)  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Scalability**  | The infrastructure should follow the CA guideliness | | **Availability** | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Security**     | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Usability**    | Ease of use in swagger | | **Maintainability** | Ease of changing upper layer implementations | | **Portability**  | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Compliance**   | Adherence to regulatory standards | | **Reliability**  | Should build for all development teams | | **Localization** | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 |  ## Task division  | Technical Task | Minimum Time (Minutes) | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Refining/Validate basic business needs with PO | 20| | Clone git repository | 5| | Create branches | 3|  ### Back-End Development with Clean Code & Clean Architecture  | Technical Task | Minimum Time (Minutes) | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Model/code entities to provide functionality | 40 | | Model/code repositories in infrastructure (I) | 40 | | - Design and scripts for DB, tables, and relationships | 40 | | - PDS for table data | 5 | | - Publish the Data Base | 10| | Model/code repositories in infrastructure (C) | 30 | | - Review integration with EFF | 2 | | - dbContext | 1| | - Map entities to tables | 15|  ### PBI: Add building  | Technical Task | Minimum Time (Minutes) | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Implement adding building item service | 120| | Dependency management | 10 | | Continuous integration to repo, into main + PR | 60 | | Meets acceptance criteria validated with PO | 60 |  ### Front-End Development: App, Presentation, WebAPI (EP class)  | Technical Task | Minimum Time (Minutes) | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | App | 60 | | Presentation | 120| | WebAPI | 30| | Compatibility | 30 | | Clean Code Compliance (duplicate code, code smells) | 15| | Don’t break architecture (CA) and follow SOLID, DDD | 5|  ### Tests & SQA  | Technical Task | Minimum Time (Minutes) | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Swagger tests | 60 | | Unit automated tests, 80% coverage | 150 | | Integration Testing with Shared Components | 150 | | Documented and updated status in backlog tool | 10 | | Prototyping, design, validation | 120 |  | Total Type | Total | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Total Minutes | 1181| | Total Hours | 19.69|  ## Performed Manual Tests   ### Test 1 – Successful adding of a building  * **Scenario:** `the user tries to add a building with the correct format` * **Format needed:** A name The color is a text that has to match with a color stored in a predetermined list. The area has to exist The height, width, and length have to be made up of positive numbers. The coordinates for the building are selected in the map * **Result:** **200 OK** with correct addition of the building's data to the data base. * **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: *"The administrator clicks a button to add a building"* Also satisfies *"The administrator adds the building information and saves changes"*,  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9468e10a-1536-41c2-a237-1f652ee1f4f5)  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b1659683-9a27-4ac6-8f67-3dd65a02797c)

title: PQL-WV-004-005 List Specific Building Information Window
author: AleJimenezRJ
body: ### EPIC ID: PQL-WV-004  **Name**: List Specific Building Information Window  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow administrators to manage buildings in the system through a window to have an accessible way of managing which buildings are available for view in the project.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: View a specific building  in the system through a window.  As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want to be able to use a friendly GUI to view a specific building, so that I can more easily edit or delete it, and keep an updated record of the buildings available in the system. ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator clicks a building   Given the administrator has displayed the complete list of buildings   When the administrator selects a building by clicking it's name   Then the screen shows all the available information for that building   And a button to update and delete the building. ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator updates building details and saves changes   Given the administrator is in edit mode   When the administrator modifies the building information   And clicks the "Save" button   Then the updated information is saved   And a confirmation message is displayed   And the screen returns to view mode with the updated details ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator cancels an edit operation   Given the administrator is in edit mode   When the administrator clicks the "Cancel" button   Then the changes are discarded   And the screen returns to view mode with original data intact ```  ```gherkin    Scenario: The administrator deletes a building    Given the administrator is viewing a building they have permission to delete    When the administrator clicks title: CPD-LC-002-003 Display components in web interface
author: Earaya12
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-LC-002  **Name**: Web interface for managing learning components **Description**:  This epic focuses on enabling administrators to manage components (e.g., projectors, whiteboards) through a web interface. Administrators users should be able to create, view, update and delete components in a user-friendly interface.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Display learning components via web interface  As a administrator user I want to see a list of all components in the system So I can understand which components are available in each learning space.  ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: View a list of components in the interface Given an administrator user is on the management component page When the admin user loads the listing page Then the system displays all components available in the learning space ```  ```gherkin Scenario: No components available Given there are no components in the learning space When the page loads Then the system shows a message like “No components found” and the learning space empty ```  ```gherkin Scenario: List updates after creating a component Given the admin user has created a new component When returning to the listing component page Then the new component should appear in the list of that learning space  ``` ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                                                                  | | ------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Security**        | Only administrators should be able to access and modify learning components. | | **Consistency**     | Changes to components (e.g., additions, updates, deletions) should reflect immediately in the UI. | | **Usability**       | The interface should provide clear feedback on successful/unsuccessful actions and show loading indicators. | | **Maintainability** | The component logic should be modular and allow for easy future enhancements (e.g., adding new component types). |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                              | Minutes | | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | Implement backend API to retrieve a list of learning components   | 25      | | Implement backend API to create a new component                   | 25      | | Implement backend API to update existing components               | 20      | | Implement backend API to delete components                        | 20      | | Update UI to display list of components, including empty state    | 30      | | Add UI feedback for successful or failed component actions        | 20      | | Integrate real-time updates for component list (e.g., after create/delete) | 25      | | Validate data inputs when creating or updating components         | 20      | | Write unit and integration tests for backend and frontend         | 35      | | Document functionality and provide usage examples                 | 10      | | Perform code review and open pull request                         | 10      |  ### **Total Time**  | **Total Minutes** | **Total Hours** | |-------------------|------------------| | 245 minutes       | 4 hours 5 minutes |  ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 – View a list of components in the interface  * **Result:**  the list of existing components is shown * **Validation:** the management component page shows a list of every existing learning component in the actual learning space  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2deb5130-054f-4e3c-a182-d571df6d79ba)  ### Test 2 – No components available  * **Result:**  "No se encontraron componentes" * **Validation:** the management component page shows does not show a list and instead shows a message indicating that there are no learning components in the actual learning space   ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/997f595f-2c77-4d42-87c6-56bebef4d090)  ### Test 3 – List updates after creating a component  * **Result:**  list including the recently created components * **Validation:** after creating a new learning component the user is redirected to the management component page wich now shows the list of components including the one that was just created, as said in the acceptance criteria  (in this case, the list was empty but was updated with the recently created component) ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3a3117fe-5e5c-4e47-bedc-ceb13ae3d23e)

title: CPD-LC-001-010 Refactor CRUD methods to use LearningSpace internal ID
author: Earaya12
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-LC-001  Name: Learning components Description: This epic is about adding components to learning spaces in ThemePark@UCR, like classrooms and labs. These components can be things like whiteboards, chairs, or projectors that users can interact with, so that learning feels more active and engaging. It also focuses on building the functionality to create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) these objects in the system.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Standardize component CRUD operations to use LearningSpace internal ID  As an administrator user   I want the CRUD operations for learning components to consistently use the internal ID of their associated learning space  So that data relationships remain normalized and easier to maintain across the system. ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: Create a component with internal LearningSpace ID Given a valid LearningSpace internal ID is available When I create a component with this ID Then the system stores the component and associates it correctly with the referenced learning space ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Update a component using LearningSpace internal ID Given a component already exists in the database When I send request with updated data and the learning space internal ID Then the system updates the component and keeps the association to the given learning space ```  ```gherkin Scenario: List components by LearningSpace internal ID Given I have a valid internal ID of a learning space When I retrieve its components Then the system returns only those components associated with that internal ID ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                                                                  | | ------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Security**        | Only administrators can perform CRUD operations with LearningSpace IDs.      | | **Consistency**     | LearningSpace internal IDs must be used uniformly across all CRUD operations.| | **Usability**       | The system must provide clear feedback on CRUD operations, including successful associations. | | **Maintainability** | The CRUD logic should support future changes to LearningSpace ID structure and relationships. |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                              | Minutes | | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | Update backend to accept LearningSpace internal ID for component creation | 25      | | Implement update logic to maintain LearningSpace associations     | 20      | | Modify retrieval logic to filter components by LearningSpace ID   | 20      | | Add validation for LearningSpace internal ID existence and format | 20      | | Update UI to display and use LearningSpace IDs during CRUD actions| 25      | | Write unit tests for LearningSpace-based CRUD logic               | 30      | | Write integration tests for end-to-end CRUD operations            | 30      | | Document functionality and provide usage examples                 | 15      | | Perform code review and open pull request                         | 10      |  ### **Total Time**  | **Total Minutes** | **Total Hours** | |-------------------|------------------| | 195 minutes       | 3 hours 15 minutes |  ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 - Create a component with internal LearningSpace ID * **Validation**: The system request a valid learning space ID before creating the learning component  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/56a1057f-3408-47b2-bc62-4f992278b8e0) learning space ID before updating the learning component  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1f30ba55-60e9-484f-a909-95be0e0e762f) title: SPT-UI-006-006 User details window
author: isabellarodriguezs
body: ### EPIC ID: SPT-UI-006 - **Name:** User Interface - **Description:** ThemePark@UCR must provide comprehensive tools for user management. This includes an interface to view, create, modify, and delete users, with role and permission control from a single window. --- ### User Story ```gherkin Feature: View user details As an administrator I want to access a window to view detailed information about a user So that I can confirm and verify user data accurately ``` --- ### **Acceptance Criteria** ```gherkin Scenario: View full user details Given the admin selects a user from the list When the user details window opens Then the window should display: Username, Email, Role, Permissions, FirstName, LastName, Phone, BirthDate, IdentityNumber ``` ```gherkin Scenario: View options for user Given the admin selects a user from the list When the user details window opens Then there should be a clear "Edit" button And there should be a clean "Delete" button ``` ```gherkin Scenario: View breadcrumbs for easy navegation Given the admin selects a user from the list When the user details window opens Then there should be a clear breadcrumbs element that reflects where you are on the app ``` --- ### **Non-functional Requirements** Category | Description -- | -- **Usability** | Data must be clearly grouped and labeled for easy reading **Security** | Only admins may access this view **Responsiveness** | Must scale cleanly between desktop and tablet displays **Performance** | Details window should open in under 500ms --- ### **Technical Tasks** Task | Minutes -- | -- Create modal or window to display details | 60 Bind selected user data to view fields | 25 UI styling and layout | 25 Add edit/delete behavior | 25 Add User with Person implementation and dto | 30 Add delete User with Person implementation | 30 Write tests and documentation | 30 Write unit tests for UserWithPerson entity constructor and properties | 35 Validate default and custom constructor behavior for domain entity | 30 Write and validate unit tests for UserWithPersonService | 25 Review and merge PR | 5 **Total estimate:** 320 minutes (~5.3 hours) # Performed Tests ## Test 1 – Full user details are displayed **Result:** A detail window opens showing the following fields: - `FirstName` - `LastName` - `Username` - `Role` - `Phone` - `IdentityNumber` - `BirthDate` - `Email` **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criteria: *“View full user details.”* All fields are present, correctly labeled, and bound to the user data. ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6ca16804-0773-44ec-a926-99da603e4fc9) --- ## Test 2 – Edit and Delete buttons are visible **Expected Result:** - A clearly labeled `Edit` button is visible - A clean and distinct `Delete` button is visible **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criteria: *“View options for user.”* Both action buttons are accessible and functional. --- ## Test 3 – Breadcrumbs are shown **Expected Result:** A `breadcrumbs` component appears at the top of the window, e.g.: `Home > Users > Juan Perez` **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criteria: *“View breadcrumbs for easy navigation.”* Breadcrumbs match the location and help the admin navigate back.

title: SPT-UI-006-007 Create user window
author: isabellarodriguezs
assignees: ebai03, tatsparamo, cyaos, isabellarodriguezs, andresMurillo23, gaelalpizar
body: ### EPIC ID: SPT-UI-006 - **Name:** User Interface - **Description:** ThemePark@UCR must provide comprehensive tools for user management. This includes an interface to view, create, modify, and delete users, with role and permission control from a single window. ### User Story ```gherkin Feature: Create user window As an administrator I want to open a window with input fields to create a user So that I can onboard new users efficiently ``` --- ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: Fill in new user data Given the admin clicks the "Add user" button When the create user window opens Then the form should display fields for: Username, Email, Role, FirstName, LastName, Phone, BirthDate, IdentityNumber ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Submit valid user data Given the admin completes the form correctly When they click “Save” Then the user should be created and redirected to the user management view ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Cancel creation Given the admin opens the create user window When they click “Cancel” Then the window should close and no user is created ``` ```gherkin Scenario: View breadcrumbs for easy navigation Given the admin opens the create user window When the window opens Then a breadcrumbs element should clearly reflect the navigation context ``` --- ### Non-functional Requirements Category | Description -- | -- Usability | All input fields must be labeled and validated Security | Only admins may access this view Responsiveness | Must scale cleanly between desktop and tablet displays Feedback | System must confirm user creation or display validation errors --- ### Technical Tasks Task | Minutes -- | -- Create window for user creation | 40 Add and validate input fields | 40 Connect with backend user creation endpoint | 60 Implement Save and Cancel behavior | 35 Implement Create user with person | 60 Add breadcrumbs and styling | 20 Write tests and documentation | 20 Review and merge PR | 5 **Total estimate:** 280 minutes (~4.6 hours) ## **Performed Tests (User Creation UI)** ### **Test 1 – Create user form fields display** * **Result:** Form displays all required fields: Username, Email, Role dropdown, FirstName, LastName, Phone, BirthDate, IdentityNumber * **Validation:** Satisfies AC: *"form should display all specified fields"* ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ea75a186-4599-42ba-886a-dc8dfadb0c4e) --- ### **Test 2 – Successful user creation** * **Result:** 201 Created response, redirects to user management view with confirmation toast: "User jdoe created successfully" * **Validation:** Verifies AC: *"user should be created and redirected"* ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2b3f399b-8e0c-45a8-bb18-6b71e624f8b5) ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d36b1bd2-af59-4676-a013-cffa359d308d) --- ### **Test 3 – Cancel creation flow** * **Result:** Window closes without API calls, returns to user list view * **Validation:** Confirms AC: *"no user is created when canceled"* ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1ab68521-48ed-43d7-bff7-469741b10084) ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/43bc58e8-c0f7-400e-badd-0bc5b2e473df) --- ### **Test 4 – title: CPD-UI-003-001 Add a map to choose learning component position
author: elenig10
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-UI-003 - **Name**:  Improve learning component interface usability - **Description**: Enhance the user administrator experience when managing learning components by improving the visual layout and interaction patterns. This includes making information easier to find, reducing user effort, and ensuring that the design is intuitive for administrators.   --- User Story ```gherkin Feature: Learning component position map   As an administrator, I want to be able to select the position and size of a learning component in the learning space using a map interface, So that I can visually place components accurately within the layout of the learning space. ``` --- ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: Drag a component to a position on the map   Given the map is open   When the user clicks on a component within the map   Then the selected component can be dragged by the user ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Cancel component creation or update Given the user is using the map When the user clicks the "Cancel" button   Then the map should close and no position or size should be saved ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Resize a component on the map   Given the user is selecting a position on the map   When the user drags the edges of the component   Then the component size should update visually on the map ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Prevent overlapping components   Given the user is editing a component and there are other components in the space   When the user tries to overlap a component   Then the action is not allowed and an error message is displayed ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Position a component during creation   Given the user is creating a new learning component   When the map interface is displayed   Then the user can immediately position the component within the learning space before saving it ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                                                                 | | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Consistency**     | The map interface must follow the global layout and component structure used across the application.|       | **Usability**          |The map interactions must be intuitive| | **Performance**     | The map must load and respond to interactions (drag/resize) within 1 second.     |  --- Design and implement reusable map component with drag/resize logic  | 60      | | Implement overlap detection logic and error messaging                          | 120      | | Integrate the map with component creation/edit workflows                    | 30      | | Ensure accessibility support for map controls  | 60     | ---  ### **Total time**  | Total minutes                                                               | Total hourse| |-----------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | 270 | 4.5     |  ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 –  Component can be resized and relocated via the map  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6003cdf4-684e-45d5-b38c-3d4ee85f6b41)  ### Test 2 –  Component can't overlap another component in the same title: CPD-RP-004-001 Add permission access to add a learning component
author: elenig10
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-RP-004 - **Name**: Add role-based permissions to core functionalities - **Description**: Implement role-based access control across the system to ensure that only authorized users can access specific functionalities. This includes assigning permissions to actions like creating, updating, listing, and deleting learning components, and enforcing these permissions throughout the application for improved security.  ---  ## User Story   ```gherkin Feature: Add permission access to add a learning component  As a user with the "add learning component" permission,   I want to create new learning components,   So that I can register them into the system when needed. ``` ---  ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: Allow user with add permission to create a learning component Given a user has the "add learning component" permission When the user attempts to create a new component Then the system should allow the creation of the component ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Hide "Add Component" button in UI for unauthorized users Given a user does not have the "add learning component" permission When the component page is loaded Then the "Add Component" button should be hidden or disabled ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                                                                 | | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Security**        | Only users with the specific "add learning component" permission can add components. | | **Consistency**     | Permission should be consistently applied across all UI and backend endpoints. | | **Usability**       | Users without permissions should not see UI options they cannot access. | | **Maintainability** | Permission checks should be centralized and easily extendable for other component actions. |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                                  | Minutes | |-----------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Add the permission tags in the frontend page   | 10      | | Update documentation           | 3    |  ---  ### **Total Time**  | **Total Minutes** | **Total Hours**     | |-------------------|----------------------| | 13 minutes       | 0 hours 13 minutes   |  ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 – Administrator can add component Validation: User with "add learning component" permission can create components successfully.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c9df3c8a-34ab-4ce7-9936-a40536921347)  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/63b84370-2504-4809-8562-590227cfd141)  This user has the permission to add, so the button is shown and then the component is added successfully.  ### Test 2 –  User without permission can't add a component Validation: "Add" button is only shown to users with the proper permission.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0b314769-db17-494e-ab27-3edaf0d367c0)  This user doesn't have the add permission, so the button is not displayed.

body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-002  **Name**: Learning Spaces Web Interface  **Description:** As part of the development of the learning spaces module, the system must provide a user-friendly web interface with essential functionalities, including listing, viewing, updating, creating, and deleting records. It must also support data registration and import through forms. In parallel, initial permission control and security mechanisms will be implemented to support scalability.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: View Learning Space Details via web interface  As an administrator user,   I want to view the features of a specific learning space—such as ID, type, maximum capacity and dimensions—through a web interface,   So that I can understand what kinds of spaces are available in a building. ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: Viewing learning space details successfully   Given I am logged in as an administrator user   And a learning space with name "LS-001" exists in the system   When I navigate to the details page for learning space "Laboratorio programacion I"   Then I should see the learning space name, type, maximum capacity, and dimensions displayed  ``` Example:  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/19576e33-5796-4f60-b0a8-b7592ebb6f04)  ```gherkin Scenario: Viewing details of a non-existent learning space title: PQL-WV-004-005 Refactor edit window
author: AleJimenezRJ
body: ### EPIC ID: PQL-WV-004  **Name**: Refactor edit window  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow administrators to update buildings in the system through a window, this window should have the previous values of the building so the edition is easier for the user  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Update a building in the system through a window.  As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want to be able to use a friendly GUI to update buildings, so that I can more easily maintain an updated record of the building's information for the buildings available in the system. ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator clicks the button to update a building   Given the administrator has displayed a building information   When the administrator clicks the update button   Then the screen changes to update the building's information   And fields appear to add the information of the building   And there are "Save" and "Cancel" buttons on the screen.  ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator updates the building information and saves changes   Given the administrator has updated the building information   When clicking the "Save" button   Then the building is updated   And the screen returns to the list of buildings with the newly updated building. ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator cancels an update operation   Given the administrator is in update mode   When the administrator clicks the "Cancel" button   Then the changes are discarded, the building information is not changed   And then the system returns to the list of buildings screen ``` ## title: PQL-FL-005-001 Filter campus by university
author: AleJimenezRJ
body: **Name**: Filter campus by university  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow administrators to filter cambus by university in the system through a dedicated option to have an accessible way of managing which campus are available by university  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Filter campus  As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want to be able to use a friendly GUI to filter campus by universities, so that I can more easily maintain an updated record of the campus available for each university ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator clicks a button and the add screen is displayed   Given the administrator is on the university management window   When the administrator clicks the "Add University" button   Then the screen changes to add mode    And the fields needed to add a new university are displayed and editable   And there is save and cancel buttons on the screen.  ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator adds university details and saves   Given the administrator is in add mode   When the administrator adds the university information   And clicks the "Save" button   Then the information is saved and the university is addded to the system   And a confirmation message and the university information are displayed   And the screen returns to the homepage ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator cancels an add operation   Given the administrator is in add mode   When the administrator clicks the "Cancel" button   Then the data is discarded   And the screen returns to the homepage ```  ```gherkin    Scenario: The administrator deletes a university    Given the administrator is viewing a university they have permission to delete    When the administrator clicks the "Delete" button    And confirms the deletion in the prompt    Then the university is removed from the system    And it no longer appears in the list of available universities ```   ## title: CPD-PG-005-001 Add pagination to learning components list
author: luis-fch
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-PG-005 - **Name**: Implement pagination for learning components list - **Description**: Implement end-to-end pagination functionality for the learning components feature, including database-level pagination in the backend API and corresponding UI components in the frontend. This ensures efficient data retrieval and improved user experience by displaying information in manageable chunks instead of loading all data at once, preventing performance issues and long wait times. --- ## User Story ```gherkin Feature: Add pagination for learning components list As an administrator, I want to view learning components in paginated chunks through the user interface, So that I can efficiently browse through large datasets without performance degradation and have a responsive user experience. ``` --- ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: Display learning components table with default settings Given the learning components endpoints are available When the administrator accesses the learning components list via web interface Then the interface should display the first 10 learning components And show pagination controls (page numbers, next/previous buttons) Scenario: Navigate through the learning components table using pagination controls Given the learning components are displayed with pagination When the administrator clicks on a next/previous button Then the web interface should request and display the corresponding page of learning components Scenario: Retrieve learning components with custom page size Given learning components are displayed with pagination When the administrator selects a different page size from the dropdown Then the web interface should request and display the first page with the new page size ``` --- ### Non-functional Requirements | Category | Requirement | Acceptance Criteria | |----------|-------------|-------------------| | **Performance** | Database efficiency | Pagination queries must use LIMIT/OFFSET or equivalent with proper indexing | | **Reliability** | Error handling | System must gracefully handle invalid page parameters and display appropriate error messages | | **Reliability** | Data consistency | Pagination metadata must accurately reflect the current dataset state | | **Usability** | Intuitive navigation | Pagination controls must be clearly visible and follow standard UI patterns | | **Usability** | Loading feedback | Users must receive visual feedback during data loading operations | --- ### Technical Tasks | Task | Minutes | | ------ | ------- | | Validate with product owner | 25 minutes | | Update domain repositories to support page size and page number (index) | 25 minutes | | Update application interfaces and services to support page size and page index | 25 minutes | | Implement pagination using Entity Framework in the infrastructure layer | 25 minutes | | Update handlers and responses in the presentation layer | 30 minutes | | Add proper error handling for the page size and page number parameters | 30 minutes | | Update learning components table to support pagination with mudblazor components | 60 minutes | | Add any missing documentation to the new features added | 15 minutes | Total minutes: 235 minutes Hours: 3.9 hours --- ## Performed Tests Manual testing in the user interface were conducted. ### Test 1: Pagination works in table with data https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2c9de3f0-e431-4cb7-a1c8-6f1dc61389d9 ### Test 2: Pagination is disabled in table with no data

title: SQL-LS-003-001 Colors in the Creation of Learning Spaces
author: EJW1001001
body: ###  EPIC ID:  SQL-LS-003  **Name**: Add color attributes to Learning Spaces Backend  **Description**: As part of the customizable environment features of ThemePark\@UCR, the system must support the definition and persistence of visual attributes such as wall color, floor color, and ceiling color within learning spaces. These attributes should be integrated into the core backend logic to ensure consistent handling during creation, reading, and updating of learning space data.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Add color attributes to Learning Spaces via backend interfaces  As an administrator user   I want to be able to define visual attributes such as wall, floor, and ceiling colors when creating learning spaces   So that the system can store and reflect aesthetic properties that enhance the customization and visualization of learning environments. ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Create a learning space with valid color attributes Given I am accessing the backend via Swagger or API tools   When I submit a request to create a learning space with valid values for wall color, floor color, and ceiling color   Then the system should register the learning space successfully and store the color attributes in the database ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Submit a learning space with invalid or unsupported color values Given I am submitting a creation request for a learning space   When the color attributes include an invalid format, unsupported color, or empty string   Then the system should reject the request and return an error message clearly indicating the issue ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements – Add Color Attributes**  | Category            | Description                                                                                                | | ------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Performance**     | The system should process and store color attributes without adding significant latency to space creation. | | **Usability**       | Color fields must be clearly documented in Swagger with valid input formats and constraints.               | | **Maintainability** | The color attribute logic should be modular and allow future extension (e.g., color palettes, themes).     | | **Reliability**     | Color data must be stored and retrieved with 99.9% consistency across API calls.                           |  ---

title: SQL-LS-001-010 - Colors in the Updating of Learning Spaces
author: EJW1001001
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-001  **Name**: Learning spaces management  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow users to explore and manipulate data related to learning spaces. This includes the ability to list available spaces, view details of a specific space, update existing records, create new entries, and retrieve relevant information.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Update color attributes in Learning Spaces via backend interfaces  As an administrator user   I want to be able to modify wall, floor, and ceiling colors when updating a learning space   So that the system can reflect changes in the data base table of learning space over time. ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Update a learning space with valid new color attributes   Given I am accessing the backend via Swagger  When I submit an update request for an existing learning space with valid wall, floor, and ceiling color values   Then the system should update the space and persist the new color attributes in the database ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Attempt to update a learning space with invalid or unsupported color values   Given I am submitting an update request for a learning space   When the request includes invalid color values (e.g., unsupported format, empty string, or null)   Then the system should reject the request and return a descriptive error message indicating the issue ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements – Update Color Attributes**  | Category            | Description                                                                                                           | | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Performance**     | The system should process color attribute updates in under 1 second under normal load.                                | | **Usability**       | Swagger documentation must clearly describe valid color input formats for update operations.                          | | **Maintainability** | Color update logic should be reusable and consistent with the creation flow to avoid code duplication.                | | **Reliability**     | Updates to color attributes should be reflected accurately and consistently across the system with 99.9% reliability. |  ---  | Task                                                                                      | Minutes | |-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Validate update requirements and constraints with the PO                                  | 10      | | Verify existing database schema supports color updates for learning spaces                | 10      | | Implement update logic in the backend controller or service layer                         | 30      | | Add update logic to the learning space repository or equivalent infrastructure component  | 25      | | Add validation logic to reject null, empty, or improperly formatted color values          | 20      | | Write unit and integration tests for valid and invalid update cases                       | 35      | | Create pull request and request code review                                               | 10      | | Validate implementation with PO and ensure Swagger documentation reflects input rules     | 15      |  Total time: 155 minutes  ---  ## Performed Manual Tests  ### Test Cases  ### Test 1 – Updating a learning space  * **Result:** **200 OK** with a JSON response containing the attributes of a learning space. * **Validation:** The success scenario shows all attributes of a learning space in a floor(learning space ID, type, max capacity, height, width, length, colorFloor, colorWalls and colorCeiling) and success message "The learning space was updated succesfully.".  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/18f1f3d8-810a-49ad-8295-f3e8ced13028)  ### Test 2 – Try to update a nonexistent learning space  * **Result:** **404 Error** with the messages: "Learning space with the Id '4005' not found.". * **Validation:** The system successfully handled the error, displaying the expected behavior and the respective error message.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ed1833d8-ef8f-419f-911d-677c754fd5b6)  ### Test 3 – Try to update with invalid data  * **Result:** **400 Error**. * **Validation:** The system successfully handled the error, displaying the expected behavior and the respective error message.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/65ca1a1d-b340-4ab0-b7b8-8742b45b8abc)

title: SQL-LS-004-001 Colors in the Creation of Learning Spaces Via Web Interface
author: EJW1001001
body: ## EPIC ID:  SQL-LS-004  **Name:** Add color selectors and visual previews Web Interface  **Description:** As part of the development of the learning spaces module, the system must provide intuitive user interfaces to visualize, select, and modify visual attributes—such as wall, floor, and ceiling colors—associated with learning spaces. These elements must be seamlessly integrated into the workflows for creating, updating, and viewing space information to enhance user experience and promote personalized environments.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Create learning spaces with visual color selection on the web interface  As an administrator user   I want to be able to select wall, floor, and ceiling colors when creating a learning space via a friendly web form   So that I can visually configure environments in a personalized and intuitive way, ensuring an engaging creation process. ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Successfully create a learning space with selected color attributes via the web form   Given I am on the learning space creation page   When I select wall, floor, and ceiling colors using the provided color pickers and submit the form   Then the system should validate the inputs and register the space with the selected colors, displaying a success confirmation ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Attempt to submit a creation form with missing or invalid color selections   Given I am on the learning space creation page   When I leave one or more color fields empty or submit invalid values (e.g., wrong format)   Then the system should highlight the issue, display a clear error message, and prevent form submission until fixed ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements – Web Color Selection in Creation Flow**  | Category            | Description                                                                                             | | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Performance**     | The color picker interface must load in under 300 ms and respond instantly to user input.               | | **Usability**       | The form must include accessible, labeled color pickers with live visual previews and input validation. | | **Maintainability** | Color input components must be reusable across creation, update, and preview modules.                   | | **Reliability**     | The form must send valid color data consistently to the backend in 99.9% of form submissions.           | ---
