title: SQL-LS-002-009 Colors in the Reading of Learning Spaces Via Web Interface
author: EJW1001001
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-002  **Name**: Learning Spaces Web Interface  **Description:** As part of the development of the learning spaces module, the system must provide a user-friendly web interface with essential functionalities, including listing, viewing, updating, creating, and deleting records. It must also support data registration and import through forms. In parallel, initial permission control and security mechanisms will be implemented to support scalability.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: View color attributes of learning spaces in the web interface  As an administrator user   I want to be able to see the wall, floor, and ceiling colors associated with each learning space  So that I can confirm the visual forms and ensure accurate representation in the interface. ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Successfully view existing color attributes of a learning space   Given the administrator is viewing the list of learning spaces  When they select a specific learning space from the list   Then the system should display the wall, floor, and ceiling colors   And each color should be shown using accurate visual indicators (e.g., color blocks or previews) matching the stored values ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Error retrieving color attributes of a learning space   Given the administrator is viewing the list of learning spaces  When they select a specific learning space from the list   Then an error message must be displayed indicating that the color data could not be loaded   ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements – Web Visualization of Colors**  | Category            | Description                                                                                                                             | | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Performance**     | Color attributes must be displayed within 500 ms of page load under standard conditions.                                                | | **Usability**       | Color previews must use clear labels and visual indicators (e.g., swatches or color tags) for quick recognition.                        | | **Maintainability** | The color display components should be modular and reusable across list, detail, and update views.                                      | | **Reliability**     | The correct color data must be shown for each learning space with 99.9% accuracy and no mismatches between stored and displayed values. |  ---  | Task                                                                                   | Minutes | |----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Validate requirements and acceptance criteria with the PO                              | 10      | | Ensure test database has learning spaces with registered color attributes              | 10      | | Implement retrieval and display logic in the application layer                         | 30      | | Implement data access method in the repository layer                                   | 25      | | Handle missing or null color attributes with appropriate UI messaging                  | 20      | | Write unit and integration tests for successful and error scenarios                    | 35      | | Create pull request and request code review                                            | 10      | | Validate with PO that the displayed colors match expectations and visual design goals  | 15      |  Total time: 155 minutes  ---  ## Performed Manual Tests  ### Test Cases   **Test 1 – Successful read of a learning space**    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b6e0f5cf-c06d-4ad7-b67b-7983e2350a2f)

title: SPT-AU-002-004 Log in to the system
author: andresMurillo23
assignees: ebai03, tatsparamo, cyaos, isabellarodriguezs, andresMurillo23, gaelalpizar
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-AU-002**  * **Name:** Authentication * **Description:** As part of the security infrastructure of ThemePark\@UCR, the system must implement user authentication using Microsoft Auth B2C. This ensures that only authorized individuals can access the system, enabling secure personalization, protection of data, and controlled access to features.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Log in to the system  As a system user   I want to authenticate using Microsoft Auth B2C   So that I can securely access the system and its protected features ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Successful login Given I have valid Microsoft Auth B2C credentials When I attempt to log in Then the system should authenticate me And redirect me to the main dashboard ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements – Login**  | Category            | Description                                                                 | | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Performance**     | The login process should complete in less than 2 seconds under normal load. | | **Security**        | Tokens must be securely stored and transmitted over HTTPS only.             | | **Portability**     | The login feature should work across web and mobile browsers.               | | **Maintainability** | B2C tenant settings (client ID, redirect URI) should be configurable.       |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                                                                       | Minutes | | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | Set up Microsoft B2C tenant and register the application                                                   | 25      | | Configure redirect URIs and scopes in Azure                                                                | 15      | | Implement login redirection and callback handling in the frontend                                          | 25      | | Configure and integrate API Connector in B2C to enrich tokens with custom claims (e.g., role, internal ID) | 20      | | Handle enriched token response and store session securely                                                  | 15      | | Test login and token enrichment flow end-to-end                                                            | 25      | | Document login functionality and token enrichment logic                                                    | 10      | | Code review and pull request                                                                               | 10      |  **Total estimate:** 145 minutes (\~2.4 hours)  ---  ## Performed Test  ### Test – Successful login and redirection to dashboard  * **Validation:** Fully satisfies the acceptance criterion: *“Successful login.”* * **System Behavior:**    * The login window is triggered via the `Authorize` button.   * The user completes authentication via Microsoft B2C.   * A valid token is issued and stored.   * The system redirects the user to the dashboard or authenticated home page.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3b1ce081-fb1a-4007-9f9c-819148922d08)  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7035daf5-f0b7-44b5-ab91-9246d45a4b5b)

title: SPT-AU-002-005 Log out of the system
author: andresMurillo23
assignees: ebai03, tatsparamo, cyaos, isabellarodriguezs, andresMurillo23, gaelalpizar
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-AU-002**  * **Name:** Authentication * **Description:** As part of the security infrastructure of ThemePark\@UCR, the system must implement user authentication using Microsoft Auth B2C. This ensures that only authorized individuals can access the system, enabling secure personalization, protection of data, and controlled access to features.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Log out of the system  As a system user   I want to log out securely   So that my session is terminated and no one else can access my data ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Successful logout Given I am logged in When I click the logout button Then the system should clear my session And redirect me to the login screen ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements – Logout**  | Category        | Description                                                        | | --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | | **Security**    | All session data must be cleared on logout.                        | | **Usability**   | Logout button should be visible in the UI and confirm redirection. | | **Reliability** | Logout must work consistently across devices and browsers.         |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                  | Minutes | | ------------------------------------- | ------- | | Add logout button and UI component    | 15      | | Clear session and token from storage  | 20      | | Redirect to login screen after logout | 10      | | Test logout on different platforms    | 15      | | Validate behavior with PO             | 10      | | PR and code review                    | 10      |  **Total estimate:** 80 minutes (\~1.3 hours)  ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 – Successful logout and redirection to login screen  * **Result:** When the user clicks the logout button, the session is cleared and the application redirects to the login screen. * **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criterion: *“Successful logout.”* * **System Behavior:** Tokens and session data are removed, and the user sees the login interface again.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f892429c-a7d4-44c0-a095-1c6967b9c46a)  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/33e4f105-1302-4b43-a6e3-b18f3d465dba)  ---  ### Test 2 – Logout button is always visible and functional  * **Result:** The logout button is consistently shown in the navigation bar or header while the user is authenticated. * **Validation:** Confirms the non-functional requirement: *“Logout button should be visible in the UI and confirm redirection.”* * **System Behavior:** Clicking the button reliably triggers the logout logic.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d9519980-fd3e-4591-83bf-80f2265f7937)  ---  ### Test 3 – Logout works across Windows browsers  * **Result:** Logout was successfully tested on multiple browsers (e.g., Chrome, Edge, Firefox) in Windows OS. * **Validation:** Confirms the non-functional requirement: *“Logout must work consistently across devices and browsers.”* * **System Behavior:** The same logout behavior is observed on all Windows platforms tested.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2399cb20-a20c-4292-ab81-d1c804075272)  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ddc2ce77-4e3d-47bb-b0ed-e9e077f83bf7)  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/22d9b6ba-54a6-4974-b9af-b72df6dbea89)

title: SPT-AU-002-006 – Enforce role based access control
author: andresMurillo23
assignees: ebai03, tatsparamo, cyaos, isabellarodriguezs, andresMurillo23, gaelalpizar
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-AU-002**  * **Name:** Authentication   * **Description:** As part of the security infrastructure of ThemePark@UCR, the system must implement user authentication using Microsoft Auth B2C. This ensures that only authorized individuals can access the system, enabling secure personalization, protection of data, and controlled access to features.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Enforce role-based access control  As a system administrator   I want the system to restrict access to functionalities based on user roles   So that only authorized users can perform specific actions ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Unauthorized user tries to access a restricted feature Given I am logged in as a regular user When I try to access an admin-only section Then the system should deny access And display a message indicating lack of permissions ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Authorized user accesses permitted features Given I am logged in as an administrator When I access the admin panel Then the system should allow me to proceed ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Role information is validated on each request Given a request to access a protected endpoint When the system receives the request Then it should validate the user’s role before granting access ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements – Authorization**  | Category           | Description                                                                 | | ------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Security**        | Role-based access must be enforced on both frontend and backend.            | | **Maintainability** | Role rules should be defined in a centralized configuration.                | | **Scalability**     | New roles and permissions should be easy to add without major refactoring. |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                                                                 | Minutes | | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | Define and document the roles (`Admin`, `Editor`, `Viewer`) in the backend role configuration system | 20      | | Implement backend authorization logic to enforce role-based access on protected endpoints            | 25      | | Add frontend guards to show or hide UI elements based on roles, relying only on backend validation   | 20      | | Display appropriate error messages or redirect unauthorized users based on backend responses         | 15      | | Write unit and integration tests to verify role enforcement in backend controllers/services          | 30      | | Validate role restrictions and behavior with the Product Owner                                       | 10      | | Perform pull request and code review                                                                 | 10      |  **Total estimate:** 130 minutes (\~2.2 hours)  ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 – Unauthorized user redirected to "Access Denied"  * **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criterion: *“Unauthorized user tries to access a restricted feature.”* * **System Behavior:** The system detects insufficient permissions and prevents access at both frontend and backend.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c02c7575-9e76-4c47-ae83-d544353f8938)  ---  ### Test 2 – Admin sees all available features and controls  * **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criterion: *“Authorized user accesses permitted features.”* * **System Behavior:** The UI renders admin-specific controls based on the user’s role, and the backend allows full access.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/15c3fe1b-21c3-4d5b-be1e-4d2f71cc29a5)  ---  ### Test 3 – Role-specific UI behavior for Editor  * **Validation:** Confirms that role-based visibility is respected on the frontend. * **System Behavior:** UI elements are conditionally rendered based on role, and unauthorized actions are blocked on the backend.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/94412c08-8004-416c-80ee-2b47ab11a4a9)  ---  ### Test 4 – Viewer role sees read-only content  * **Validation:** Confirms strict read-only access, satisfying the *principle of least privilege*. * **System Behavior:** The interface disables or hides actions like "Edit" or "Delete", and backend enforces access restrictions.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8b164901-a9fd-4a50-bb00-3bff8a92a3b3)

title: SQL-LS-002-010 Paginate learning spaces web list
author: RolandoVillavicencio013
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-002  **Name**: Learning Spaces Web Interface  **Description:** As part of the development of the learning spaces module, the system must provide a user-friendly web interface with essential functionalities, including listing, viewing, updating, creating, and deleting records. It must also support data registration and import through forms. In parallel, initial permission control and security mechanisms will be implemented to support scalability.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Custom Page Size for Paginated Learning Spaces List  As an administrator user,   I want to select how many learning spaces are shown per page (10 by default), So that I can control the amount of information visible and navigate the list in the way that best suits me. ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: User selects a custom page size   Given the user is viewing the list of learning spaces   When the user selects a desired page size (5, 10, 15 or 20, as max value)   Then the system updates the list to show that number of learning spaces (if available) per page and adjusts the pagination controls accordingly ```  ```gherkin Scenario: User navigates forward to the next page   Given the user is viewing any page of the learning spaces list   When the user clicks on the "Next" pagination control   Then the system displays the next page of learning spaces using the selected page size ```  ```gherkin Scenario: User navigates backward to the previous page   Given the user is viewing any page of the learning spaces list except the first   When the user clicks on the "Previous" pagination control   Then the system displays the previous page of learning spaces using the selected page size ```  ---  ### Non-functional Requirements | Category            | Description                                                                                                           | |---------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | **Performance**     | Pagination updates must complete and render the list view within 700 ms under normal system conditions.              | | **Usability**       | Page size selection must be accessible and provide feedback (e.g., highlighting current selection, updating controls).| | **Maintainability** | Pagination logic must be encapsulated in reusable components or hooks for future use in similar views.               | | **Reliability**     | The list must correctly reflect the selected page size and maintain consistency across navigation actions.            |  ---  ### Technical Tasks  | Task                                                                                                 | Minutes | |------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Review functional expectations and page size limits with the PO                                     | 20      | | Refactor frontend pagination component to support dynamic page size                                 | 50      | | Implement dropdown or selection control for page size                                               | 28      | | Bind selected page size to state and update fetch logic accordingly                                 | 45      | | Adjust backend API call to accept page size and return proper subset                                | 40      | | Handle navigation logic (Next/Previous) with new dynamic limits                                     | 35      | | Perform manual UX validation across different screen sizes and interactions                         | 30      | | Write unit and integration tests for all edge cases (e.g., last page, page size switch mid-flow)    | 50      | | Submit for review, incorporate feedback, and validate with PO                                       | 20      |  Total time: 328 minutes  ---  ### Performed Tests:  **Test 1 - User selects a custom page size**  * The system updates the list to show that number of learning spaces (if available)  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/450d73ee-7b55-4cfe-97ab-ecfb88add870)  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/97913f68-87c2-439b-a87c-0e8eb3236822)  **Test 2 -  User navigates forward to the next page**  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a53506e1-c0c5-4861-a841-c25942ec14f0)  **Test 3 - User navigates backward to the previous page**  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7bdc8e82-31b0-419d-88f5-886538717654)

title: SQL-LS-001-012 Paginate learning spaces list
author: RolandoVillavicencio013
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-001  **Name**: Learning spaces management  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow users to explore and manipulate data related to learning spaces. This includes the ability to list available spaces, view details of a specific space, update existing records, create new entries, and retrieve relevant information.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Pagination with user-selectable page size for learning spaces  As an administrator user,   I want to specify the page number and page size when requesting the learning spaces list,   So that I can retrieve only the subset of learning spaces I want to see. ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: Request a custom page size   Given the administrator requests the learning spaces list.   When the administrator specifies a valid page size parameter (5, 10, 15 or 20 as max-limit).   Then the response contains that number of learning spaces (if available). ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Request a page with no available elements   Given the administrator requests a page number (1 if not provided) impossible to retrieve due to the current available elements for the selected page size   When the system processes the request   Then the response contains an empty list of learning spaces. ```  ---  ### Non-functional Requirements   | Category            | Description                                                                                                          | |---------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | **Performance**     | Backend must respond to paginated list requests within 700 ms under normal load.                                    | | **Usability**       | API must accept and validate `page` and `pageSize` query parameters with clear error messages for invalid input.     | | **Maintainability** | Pagination logic should follow standard patterns to ensure consistency and reusability across endpoints.             | | **Reliability**     | API responses must consistently reflect accurate pagination boundaries, even on edge cases (e.g., empty pages).       |  ---  ### Technical Tasks  | Task                                                                                          | Minutes | |-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Confirm expected query parameter names, ranges, and defaults with PO                          | 20      | | Add pagination logic to learning space repository or database access layer                    | 42      | | Implement controller/service method to parse and apply page and pageSize values               | 35      | | Enforce input validation (positive integers, page size limits, defaults)                      | 28      | | Handle edge case where requested page exceeds result bounds and return empty list             | 30      | | Update API documentation (e.g., Swagger) with examples of usage and parameter description     | 22      | | Write unit tests for pagination logic (with and without results)                              | 38      | | Perform integration testing across full pagination range                                      | 36      | | Submit PR and verify compliance with expected API behavior in demo with PO                    | 20      |  Total time: 271 minutes  ---  ### Performed Tests  **Test 1 - Request a custom page size**  * **Result:** **200 OK** with a JSON response containing the paginated list of learning spaces. * **Validation:** The success scenario shows a paginated list with all the elements request according to the page size or less if there are no enough available elements. Also, metadata such as the current page size and index, the total count of elements across all pages and the total count of pages is provided.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b12cc344-6235-4563-badf-61cbe99361c2)  **Test 2 - Invalid page size** * **Result:** **400 Bad Request** with the message: "Page size must be greater than or equal to 1 and less than or equal to 20." * **Validation:** The max page size that can be request is 20 as it was validated with the PO. In that, case, a user friendly message indicate the invalid request to the user.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8e749747-9b81-4068-840c-40ceba1538c4)  **Test 3 - Request a page with no available elements** * **Result:** **200 OK** with a JSON response containing the paginated list of learning spaces. * **Validation:** In this case, there are no available elements for the page request, so an empty list is shown to the user.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/07d9799d-9fac-461a-af28-eac083f51ca9)  **Test 4 - Default page size and page index**  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5a8b7826-7777-48a9-be99-046749170b4b)

title: PQL-WV-004-006 Standarized UI design
author: AleJimenezRJ
body: **Name**: Standarized UI design  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must have a standarized user experience and user interface so to it's easier for the user to be familiarized with it.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Standarized design As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want to be the system to have all screens that have the same function to look the same So that I can get familiarized to it quicker ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator wants to add a bulding and a university   Given the administrator is on the home page of the web page   When the administrator tries to add a building and a university   Then the screens and buttons should look as similar as possible as the other functionalities of add in the system ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator wants to edit a bulding and a university   Given the administrator is on the home page of the web page   When the administrator tries to edit a building and a university   Then the screens and buttons should look as similar as possible as the other functionalities of edit in the system ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator wants to list a bulding and a university   Given the administrator is on the home page of the web page   When the administrator tries to list a building and a university   Then the screens and buttons should look as similar as possible as the other functionalities of list in the system ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator wants to search a bulding   Given the administrator is on the home page of the web page   When the administrator tries to search a specific building   Then the screens and buttons should look as similar as possible as the other functionalities of search in the system ```   ## Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs)  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 2 | | **Scalability**  | The infrastructure should follow the CA guideliness | | **Security**     | Should use the authentication service | | **Usability**    | Ease of use in swagger | | **Maintainability** | Ease of changing upper layer implementations | | **Portability**  | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 2 | | **Compliance**   | Adherence to regulatory standards | | **Reliability**  | Should build for all development teams | | **Localization** | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 2 |  ## Task division  | Technical Task | Minimum Time (Minutes) | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Refining/Validate basic business needs with PO | 20| | Clone git repository | 5| | Create branches | 3| | Coordination with teams | 30| | Implement standardization| 500 | | Tests standards | 60|   | Total Type | Total | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Total Minutes | 618 | | Total Hours | 10.3 |

title: PQL-WV-004-007 Standarized errors
author: AleJimenezRJ
body: **Name**: Standarized errors  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must have a standarized error management in the user interface so to it's easier for the user to troubleshoot the error.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Standarized errors As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want to be the system to have an uniform error management So that troubleshoot issues quicker and have a better experience overall ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator fails to add a bulding and a university   Given the administrator tries to add a building or university   When the administrator fails due to adding something that already exists or doesn't provide the full information   Then the screen shows an error as similar as possible as the others  in the system ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator fails to edit a bulding   Given the administrator tries to edit a building   When the administrator fails due to incorrect data   Then the screen shows an error as similar as possible as the others for edit services in the system ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator wants to list a bulding and a university   Given the administrator is on the home page of the web page   When the administrator tries to list a building and a university   And there are no buildings or universities in the system   Then the screen shows an error as similar as possible as the other functionalities of list in the system for empty scenarios ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator wants to search a bulding   Given the administrator is on the home page of the web page   When the administrator tries to search a specific building   And that building doesn't exist   Then the screens should show a message of error as similar as possible as the other functionalities of search in the system ```   ## Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs)  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Scalability**  | The infrastructure should follow the CA guideliness | | **Availability** | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Security**     | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Usability**    | Ease of use in swagger | | **Maintainability** | Ease of changing upper layer implementations | | **Portability**  | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Compliance**   | Adherence to regulatory standards | | **Reliability**  | Should build for all development teams | | **Localization** | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 |  ## title: PQL-AU-012-001 Authorization to manage buildings in the user interface
author: AleJimenezRJ
assignees: Giulyos, disotocastro, camsko, WilliamJMF, Felipequesada21, AleJimenezRJ
body: **Name**: Authorization to manage buildings in the User Interface  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must secure and only allow administrators to manage the buildings, this to avoid improper use of the system in the frontend  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Authorization to manage buildings As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want that only other administrator and me, are allowed to manage the buildings in the system So that only authorized persons can use the services to add, delete, and edit buildings. ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: A verified administrator tries to manage the buildings   Given the administrator tries to manage the buildings   When the administrator tries to add, edit, or delete a building   Then the action should require some sort of security authetication related to the user   And if passed the verification, the administrator should be able to add, edit, or delete a building. ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: An un-authorized user tries to manage the buildings   Given the user tries to add, edit, or delete a building without having the permissions   When the user enters to the windows   Then the options to do this actions should be hidden  ```   ## Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs)  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 2 | | **Scalability**  | The infrastructure should follow the CA guideliness | | **Availability** | Should always be required | | **Security**     | Only authorized users can manage the buildings | | **Usability**    | Ease of use for administrators | | **Maintainability** | Ease of changing upper layer implementations | | **Portability**  | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 2 | | **Compliance**   | Adherence to regulatory standards | | **Reliability**  | Should always work | | **Localization** | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 2 |  ## Task division  | Technical Task | Minimum Time (Minutes) | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Refining/Validate basic business needs with PO | 20| | Clone git repository | 5| | Create branches | 3|   | Total Type | Total | |:---------------|:----------------------:| | Total Minutes | 1181| | Total Hours | 19.69|

title: SPT-UI-006-008 Standardize UI components in user management
author: gaelalpizar
body: ## **EPIC ID:** SPT-UI-006  **Name:** User Interface  **Description:** To facilitate user management, ThemePark\@UCR must provide user a pleasant navigation through an UI that has all its components standardized.  ---  ## **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Standardize UI components in user management  As a system user,   I want all interface elements related to user management (like buttons, tables, and pop-ups) to follow a consistent style and behavior,   So that the experience is cohesive, professional, and easy to understand. ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Standard visual style for all action buttons Given I am on any user management interface When action buttons (e.g., Modify, Delete, Save, Cancel) are displayed Then they should follow the same color, icon, size, and position pattern as defined in the UI guidelines ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Consistent table formatting across user management views Given I am viewing a table of users When the page loads Then all tables should use the same column alignment, headers, spacing, and visual style ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Uniform behavior of modals and pop-ups Given I trigger a pop-up for modifying or confirming an action When the modal is displayed Then it should follow a consistent layout, size, and interaction pattern (e.g., overlay behavior, button placement) ``` ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category                 | Description                                                                                        | | ------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Usability**            | The interface must follow UCR modern design guidelines and be intuitive for both new and expert users. | | **Consistency**          | All UI elements (buttons, tables, modals) must follow a unified design language across the system. | | **Responsiveness**       | The layout and elements must adapt correctly to mobile, tablet, and desktop screen sizes.          | | **Internationalization** | All visible UI text must support localization in Spanish and title: PQL-WV-004-008 Language compliance
author: disotocastro
body: **Name**: Language compliance  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must have a standarized language in the user interface so to it's easier for the user to read the information in the system.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Language compliance As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want the system to display all standardized language messages in Spanish So that I can read and understand the text on screen in my native language. ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: The administrator wants to edit a building and a university Given the administrator is on the home page When they attempt to edit a building and a university Then all screens, labels, and buttons should appear in Spanish and follow the style and consistency of other "edit" functionalities ```  ```gherkin Scenario: The administrator wants to list buildings and universities   Given the administrator is on the home page   When they attempt to list buildings and universities   Then all pop ups should appear in Spanish and follow the style and consistency of other functionalities ```  ```gherkin Scenario: The administrator wants to search for a building   Given the administrator is on the home page   When they attempt to search for a specific building   Then all screens, labels, and buttons should show the correct entity name in Spanish and follow the style and consistency of other functionalities ``` ## title: PQL-UT-006-001 Unit Test for backend
author: Felipequesada21
assignees: Giulyos, disotocastro, camsko, WilliamJMF, Felipequesada21, AleJimenezRJ
body: ### PBI: PQL-UT-006-001 Unit Test for backend.  ### **EPIC ID: PQL-UT-006**  **Name**: Unit Testing **Description**: As part of the core development principles of the system, the codebase must include unit tests to validate the behavior of the backend project. These tests ensure the correctness and stability of core business logic and help detect errors early in the systems cycle.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Unit test the backend  As a developer   I want to create unit tests for the backend So that I can ensure the correctness of our systemlogic    ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Successful execution of backend unit tests Given the backend contains business logic and API components  When the developer runs the unit test suite   Then the system should execute the tests successfully   And all tests should pass with valid assertions   ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Test failure on invalid backendlogic Given a backend component behaves incorrectly   When the developer runs the unit test suite   Then the system should detect the error   And display which test(s) failed with clear messages   ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Code coverage for backend Given the unit tests are implemented correctly   When coverage tools are run   Then at least 80% of the backend codebase should be covered by tests    ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements - Unit Test for backend**  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | The test suite should execute in under 5 seconds under normal conditions. | | **Reliability**  | Tests should return consistent results across environments and executions. |  **Maintainability** | Tests must be written in a clean, readable format and separated by scenario. | | **Portability**  | The unit tests should work across different deployment environments. |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks – Unit Test for backend**  | Task                                                       | Minutes | |------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Review backend components and responsibilities             | 15      | | Define test cases for each method and controller logic     | 20      | | Write unit tests for key services and API handlers         | 40      | | Add assertion logic for all expected outcomes              | 20      | | Run tests and ensure all pass                              | 10      | | Ensure minimum 80% code coverage is achieved               | 10      | | Commit changes, open PR, and request code review           | 15      |  **Total estimate (minutes):** 130   **Total estimate (hours):** 2.17

title: CPD-LC-002-006 Logical deletion of a learning component
author: Earaya12
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-LC-002  **Name**: Web interface for managing learning components **Description**:  This epic focuses on enabling administrators to manage components (e.g., projectors, whiteboards) through a web interface. Administrators users should be able to create, view, update and delete components in a user-friendly interface.   ---  ## User Story   ```gherkin Feature: Logical deletion of a learning component  As an administrator,   I want to logically delete a learning component,   So that it no longer appears in the system but is still retained in the database for future reference. ``` ---  ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: Logically delete a component Given I am an authenticated administrator When I confirm deletion of a learning component Then the system should mark the component as deleted without physically removing it from the database  Scenario: Hide logically deleted components from all views Given a component has been logically deleted When any user views the list of components Then the deleted component should not be shown  Scenario: Prevent duplicate deletion attempts Given a component has already been logically deleted When an admin attempts to delete it again Then the system should return an appropriate message or error ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                                                                 | | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Security**        | Only users with delete permissions can perform logical deletion.            | | **Consistency**     | Logically deleted components should be consistently excluded across all queries and views. | | **Auditability**    | Deletion timestamps and user identity should be recorded for auditing.      | | **Maintainability** | Logical deletion logic should be reusable across component types.           |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                                  | Minutes | |-----------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Add `IsDeleted` flag to learning component | 15      | | Update repository logic to filter out logically deleted components    | 25      | | Implement delete endpoint to set the `IsDeleted` flag                 | 20      | | Add UI confirmation modal for delete action                           | 20      | | Hide deleted components from all views                                | 20      | | Return informative messages when deleting already deleted components  | 15      | | Write unit tests for logical deletion logic                           | 30      | | Write integration tests for deletion flow                             | 30      | | Perform code review and open pull request                             | 10      |  ---  ### **Total Time**  | **Total Minutes** | **Total Hours**     | |-------------------|----------------------| | 185 minutes       | 3 hours 5 minutes    |  ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 – Logical Deletion * **Before deleting Validation:** title: PQL-LB-002-003 Pagination in list buildings
author: AleJimenezRJ
body: **Name**: Pagination in List Buildings  **Description**: To improve usability and performance when dealing with large sets of data, the ThemePark@UCR system must implement pagination in the building listing interface.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Paginated building listings As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want the list of buildings to be paginated, So that I can navigate through large data sets without performance issues or overwhelming the UI. ```  ### Acceptance criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: Default view paginates building list Given the administrator is on the building list page, When the list of buildings is displayed, Then only a fixed number of entries (e.g., 10 per page) should be shown by default. ```   ```gherkin Scenario: Navigation through pages Given the administrator is viewing the building list, When the administrator clicks the "Next" or "Previous" pagination button, Then the corresponding page of building entries should be displayed. ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Direct navigation to a page number Given the administrator is on the building list page, When the administrator enters a page number or clicks on a specific page indicator, Then that page of the building list should be shown. ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Preserving state during pagination Given the administrator is filtering or searching buildings, When the administrator navigates through pages, Then the applied filters or search terms should persist across paginated views. ``` ## Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs)  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Scalability**  | The infrastructure should follow the CA guideliness | | **Availability** | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Security**     | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Usability**    | Ease of use in swagger | | **Maintainability** | Ease of changing upper layer implementations | | **Portability**  | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 | | **Compliance**   | Adherence to regulatory standards | | **Reliability**  | Should build for all development teams | | **Localization** | Not taken into consideration for Sprint 0 |  ## title: SQL-LS-002-007 - Colors in the Creation of Learning Spaces Via Web Interface
author: andervar
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-002  **Name**: Learning Spaces Web Interface  **Description:** As part of the development of the learning spaces module, the system must provide a user-friendly web interface with essential functionalities, including listing, viewing, updating, creating, and deleting records. It must also support data registration and import through forms. In parallel, initial permission control and security mechanisms will be implemented to support scalability.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Set Learning Space color via web interface  As an administrator user, I want to assign and store the color of the wall, ceiling, and floor for a learning space via the web interface, So that the system maintains accurate and detailed records of the space’s appearance and characteristics. ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: Successfully set colors for a learning space   Given the admin is creating or editing a learning space via the web interface   When they provide valid color values for wall, ceiling, and floor (e.g., hex code or color name)   Then the system saves these color values for the learning space ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Invalid color value   Given the admin enters an invalid color value for wall, ceiling, or floor   When they try to save the learning space   Then the system shows an error message for the color field and does not save changes ``` ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements – Web Color Selection in Creation Flow**  | Category            | Description                                                                                             | | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Performance**     | The color picker interface must load in under 300 ms and respond instantly to user input.               | | **Usability**       | The form must include accessible, labeled color pickers with live visual previews and input validation. | | **Maintainability** | Color input components must be reusable across creation, update, and preview modules.                   | | **Reliability**     | The form must send valid color data consistently to the backend in 99.9% of form submissions.           | ---   | Task title: SPT-UI-006-009 User audit logs window
author: cyaos
body: ### EPIC ID: SPT-UI-006  **Name:** User Interface  **Description:**  ThemePark@UCR must provide comprehensive tools for user management. This includes an interface to view, create, modify, and delete users, with role and permission control from a single window.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: View audit log for user account changes  As an admin user,   I want to see a log of all user creation, updates, and deletions,   So that I can track changes to user accounts for monitoring and auditing purposes. ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: Display user account audit logs Given the administrator is on the user audit log page, When the data loads, Then the administrator should see a table showing. ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Follow standard table UI Given the administrator is viewing the audit log table, When the page finishes loading the audit data, Then the table should display user audit entries, And each column should follow the system-wide formatting guidelines. ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Support pagination Given there are more than 20 user audit entries in the system, When the administrator access the audit log page, Then the table should display the first 20 entries, And pagination controls (e.g., next, previous, page numbers) should be visible below the table. ```  ---  ### Non-Functional Requirements Category | Description -- | -- Usability | Interface must clearly show user change events, with meaningful labels and tooltips if needed. Consistency | Follows standard UI components (tables, buttons, icons) as per EPIC SPT-UI-006. Internationalization | Text supports both Spanish and English locales. Performance | Must load and paginate audit entries efficiently (even with 1000+ rows).  ---  ### Technical Tasks Task | Time (min) -- | -- Create DTO and endpoint to retrieve user audit logs | 15 Implement Blazor table view for audit log entries | 20 Apply standard UI style to table (headers, alignment, spacing) | 10 Add filters by action type and user | 10 Add pagination controls and connect to backend | 10 Make table responsive and test across screen sizes | 10 Enable i18n text using resource files | 10 Add loading, empty state, and error messages | 5 Add snapshot/UI test for the audit table | 5 Code review and pull request | 5  **Total estimate:** 100 minutes (~1.7 hours)   ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 - Display user account audit logs  - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “the administrator should see a table showing”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/21838696-af48-4e48-8afc-cda9ec7b7659)  ---  ### Test 2 - Follow standard table UI  - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria:  “ the table should display user audit entries”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/04a206c1-95c1-40e9-8ffd-7b8bc03e5664)   ---  ### Test 3 - Support pagination  - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria:  “the table should display the first 20 entries”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fb6f1d4f-705c-4ee5-8492-d2e5f38a7f5a)

title: SPT-UM-005-005 List User Audit Logs
author: cyaos
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-UM-005**  * **Name:** User Management   * **Description:** ThemePark@UCR requires a comprehensive user management system to allow administrators to create, update, and delete user accounts. This is essential for maintaining system integrity, ensuring that only authorized individuals have access, and keeping user data current and accurate.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: List all user audit logs  As an administrator   I want to retrieve a list of all user audit logs   So that I can monitor changes to user accounts for accountability and traceability ``` ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Retrieve all user audit logs Given that the administrator queries the audit log endpoint   When the request is processed   Then the system should return a list of audit records including action type (create/update/delete), timestamp, and user information  ```  ```gherkin Scenario: No audit logs in the system Given that no audit records exist   When the endpoint is called   Then the system should return a 200 OK status with a message saying "There are no registered user audits" and an empty list ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  Category | Description -- | -- Performance | Retrieving the audit logs should take no longer than 1 second under normal system load. Security | Only authenticated and authorized users (e.g., administrators) can access the audit log data. Reliability | The endpoint must return accurate and complete audit data in 99.9% of valid requests. Maintainability | The audit retrieval logic should be modular and support future extensions such as filtering or pagination. Portability | The endpoint must function consistently across all supported environments.  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  Task | Minutes -- | -- Validate audit log structure and fields with the PO | 10 Ensure test database has audit entries for testing | 10 Implement application layer logic to retrieve all audit logs | 30 Implement method in the audit repository (infrastructure layer) | 25 Handle empty result sets with appropriate response | 15 Write unit and integration tests for success and empty scenarios | 35 Create pull request and request code review | 10 Validate with PO that the functionality meets expectations | 15  **Total estimate:** 140 minutes (~2.3 hours)   ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 - Retrieve all user audit logs  - **Result:** 200 OK with successful modify. - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “the system should return a list of audit records including action type (create/update/delete), timestamp, and user ”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dbb5d689-63ab-44d8-a3e6-02e9b580f2e3)  ---  ### Test 2 - No audit logs in the system  - **Result:** 200 OK with successful search. - **Validation:** Satisfies the second acceptance criterion: “the system should return a 200 OK status with a message saying "There are no registered user audits" and an empty list”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d31bb880-5891-4bf1-a201-14498986de9f)

title: SQL-LS-002-011 Contextualization of  LearningSpaces via web Interface
author: EJW1001001
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-002  **Name**: Learning Spaces Web Interface  **Description:** As part of the development of the learning spaces module, the system must provide a user-friendly web interface with essential functionalities, including listing, viewing, updating, creating, and deleting records. It must also support data registration and import through forms. In parallel, initial permission control and security mechanisms will be implemented to support scalability.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Visual contextualization of learning space location in the frontend interface  As a system user navigating the web application   I want to always see clear labels indicating the building and floor of the current learning space   So that I can stay aware of the physical context of the space I’m viewing or editing. ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Display contextual labels with building and floor information in the UI   Given I am viewing a page related to a specific learning space in the Blazor web interface   When the page is rendered   Then I should see labels indicating the building name and the floor number associated with the learning space at the top of the page ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Building and floor information fails to load or is missing   Given I access a learning space interface   When the system cannot retrieve the building or floor data correctly   Then the system should halt rendering of the page and return an explicit error before continuing, indicating that location context could not be retrieved ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements – Contextual Labels**  | Category            | Description                                                                                                                               | | ------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Performance**     | Location context (building and floor) should load and render with the page in under 1 second under normal conditions.                     | | **Usability**       | The UI must display location labels (building and floor) in a consistent and visible location across all learning space views.            | | **Maintainability** | Location display logic should be centralized and reused across pages to minimize duplication and ensure consistency.                      | | **Reliability**     | The contextual labels must render correctly in 99.9% of interactions; in case of failure, clear fallback or blocking behavior must occur. |  ---  ### Technical Tasks  | Task                                                                                              | Minutes | |---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Review design expectations and placement of contextual labels with PO                             | 20      | | Update layout templates to reserve location for building and floor labels                         | 32      | | Implement component to fetch and display building/floor context for a learning space              | 42      | | Handle loading state and fallback behavior when context is missing or invalid                     | 30      | | Add shared service or utility for location data retrieval and ensure reusability                  | 35      | | Integrate location component into all relevant learning space views                               | 28      | | Perform visual QA on different screen sizes and states (empty/missing location)                   | 27      | | Submit implementation for review and verify expectations with PO                                  | 20      |  | Task                                                                                              | Minutes | |---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Review design expectations and placement of contextual labels with PO                             | 20      | | Update layout templates to reserve location for building and floor labels                         | 32      | | Implement component to fetch and display building/floor context for a learning space              | 42      | | Handle loading state and fallback behavior when context is missing or invalid                     | 30      | | Add shared service or utility for location data retrieval and ensure reusability                  | 35      | | Integrate location component into all relevant learning space views                               | 28      | | Write UI and integration tests for successful display and failure scenarios                       | 40      | | Perform visual QA on different screen sizes and states (empty/missing location)                   | 27      | | Submit implementation for review and verify expectations with PO                                  | 20      |  Total time: 234 minutes  ---  ### Performed Tests:  **Test 1 - Display contextual labels with building and floor information in the UI**  * View details of a specific learning space  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8172b701-9fc2-43df-809d-04bac110d0bc)  * Update a specific learning space
