title: SQL-LS-002-012 Refactor Learning Space web interface
author: andervar
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-002  **Name**: Learning Spaces Web Interface  **Description:** As part of the development of the learning spaces module, the system must provide a user-friendly web interface with essential functionalities, including listing, viewing, updating, creating, and deleting records. It must also support data registration and import through forms. In parallel, initial permission control and security mechanisms will be implemented to support scalability.  ---  ```gherkin Feature: Refactor Learning Spaces Web Interface  As an administrator, I want an improved web interface that allows me to manage learning spaces efficiently, So that the system accurately reflects the institution’s physical infrastructure and is easy to maintain. ```  ---  ## Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: Correct information is displayed   Given I access the learning spaces page for a specific floor   When the page loads   Then it displays only the learning spaces relevant to that floor, showing correct and updated information ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Relevant and functional buttons   Given I am on the learning spaces page   When I view the available buttons   Then only buttons relevant to the actions on this page are visible, and all visible buttons are fully functional   And there are no buttons shown that have no functionality or are unrelated to the current context ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Page is specific to the selected floor   Given I select or am redirected to a floor’s learning spaces page   When I view the content   Then the page title, content, and actions clearly indicate and operate only on the selected floor ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Proper navigation and redirection   Given I am on the learning spaces page for a specific floor   When I use navigation options   Then I am redirected correctly either back to the building, to another floor, or to related pages, and navigation is intuitive and consistent with the application’s design ```  ---  ### Non-functional Requirements  | Category            | Description                                                                                                                       | | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Performance**     | The page should load and render all relevant learning spaces for the selected floor within 1.2 seconds under normal load.         | | **Usability**       | Buttons and navigation controls must be intuitive, responsive, and only display options relevant to the current page context.     | | **Maintainability** | The interface code should be modular, with clearly separated components for floor-specific data and navigation elements.           | | **Reliability**     | Navigation and page data should be accurate and stable, with 99.9% correctness in displayed information and redirection behavior. |   ---  ### Technical Tasks  | Task                                                                                          | Minutes | |-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Analyze current interface and identify areas to modularize and improve                        | 45      | | Refactor data fetching logic to filter learning spaces by floor                              | 50      | | Redesign UI components to dynamically update title, content, and buttons per selected floor  | 40      | | Implement conditional rendering of buttons based on context                                 | 35      | | Update routing and navigation logic to support smooth transitions between floors/buildings  | 45      | | Write unit and integration tests for new interface components                               | 50      | | Perform manual UI/UX testing and gather feedback for adjustments                            | 35      | | Conduct final review and incorporate last fixes                                            | 30      |  Total time: 370   ---  ### Perfomed Test:  **Test 1 - Correct information is displayed**  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e21c09e7-decb-434d-8c3d-41e47371d9ac)

title: SPT-UI-006-003-009 Paginated user listings
author: andresMurillo23
body: ## **EPIC ID:** SPT-UI-006  **Name:** User Interface  **Description:** To facilitate user management, ThemePark\@UCR must provide user a pleasant navigation through an UI that has all its components standardized.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Paginated user listings As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want the list of users to be paginated, So that I can manage large datasets efficiently without overwhelming the interface. ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: Default view paginates user list Given the administrator is on the user management page, When the user list is displayed, Then only a fixed number of entries (10 per page) should be shown by default. ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Navigation through user pages Given the administrator is viewing the user list, When they click "Next" or "Previous", Then the corresponding page of users should be shown. ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Changing number of users per page Given the administrator is viewing the user list, When they change the rows-per-page value, Then the table should reload with the new pagination setting and start at page 1. ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Rows per page follows system constraints Given the backend limits pagination to [5,10,15,20], When the administrator selects a page size, Then only those valid options are available and applied correctly. ```  ---  ### Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs)  | Category           | Description                                                  | |--------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------| | Performance        | Fast rendering of each user page, avoiding UI delays         | | Scalability        | Supports growing user base without performance loss          | | Usability          | Pagination controls are intuitive and match UI standards     | | Maintainability    | Easy to extend to other list-based modules                   | | Reliability        | Should handle empty lists and errors gracefully              | | Compliance         | Respects frontend/backend contract constraints               |  ---  ### Task Breakdown  | Technical Task                                          | Estimated Time (Minutes) | |---------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------| | Refine acceptance criteria with PO                      | 15                        | | Add `RowsPerPageOptions = [5, 10, 15, 20]` in UI         | 5                         | | Add `RowsPerPage = 10` as default                       | 3                         | | Handle `OnRowsPerPageChanged` logic                     | 15                        | | Update MudTable configuration in Blazor view            | 10                        | | Clear page state when rows-per-page changes             | 10                        | | Ensure backend enforces max allowed page size           | 10                        | | Test: Default page loads with 10 users                  | 8                         | | Test: Navigation (next/prev/page number click)          | 8                         | | Test: Changing pagination options                       | 10                        | | Peer review & merge PR                                  | 15                        | | Update documentation and tag release                    | 10                        |  Total estimate: 175 minutes (~2.9 hours)   ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 - Default view paginates user list  - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “only a fixed number of entries (10 per page) should be shown by default.”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/aad54d85-bb95-4775-8dda-3a1a36d14977)  ---  ### Test 2 - Navigation through user pages  - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “the corresponding page of users should be shown.”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4c2f5e0b-41bb-47b8-8590-92e8e2121581)   ---  ### Test 3 - Changing number of users per page  - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “the table should reload with the new pagination setting and start at page 1”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/321c6b97-9bac-4f44-a877-4358d8a6b153)   ---  ### Test 4 - Rows per page follows system constraints  - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “only those valid options are available and applied correctly”.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a1d0175d-4a56-47c9-9f25-61459021044e)

title: SQL-LS-001-009 - Colors in the Creation of Learning Spaces
author: andervar
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-001  **Name**: Learning spaces management  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow users to explore and manipulate data related to learning spaces. This includes the ability to list available spaces, view details of a specific space, update existing records, create new entries, and retrieve relevant information.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Set Learning Space Colors  As an administrator user,   I want to assign and store the color of the wall, ceiling, and floor for a learning space,   So that the system can maintain accurate and detailed records of the space’s appearance and characteristics. ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: Successfully set colors for a learning space   Given the admin is creating or editing a learning space   When they provide valid color values for wall, ceiling, and floor   Then the system saves and displays these color values for the learning space ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Invalid color value   Given the admin enters an invalid color value for wall, ceiling, or floor   When they try to save the learning space   Then the system shows an error message for the color field and does not save changes ```   ### **Non-functional Requirements – Add Color Attributes**  | Category            | Description                                                                                                | | ------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Performance**     | The system should process and store color attributes without adding significant latency to space creation. | | **Usability**       | Color fields must be clearly documented in Swagger with valid input formats and constraints.               | | **Maintainability** | The color attribute logic should be modular and allow future extension (e.g., color palettes, themes).     | | **Reliability**     | Color data must be stored and retrieved with 99.9% consistency across API calls.                           |  ---  | Task                                                                                          | Minutes | |-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Discuss expected color format and backend flow with the PO                                    | 10      | | Update database models or migrations to include color fields if not present                   | 15      | | Modify creation/update logic to accept and persist color values                               | 25      | | Add validation logic to detect empty or invalid color inputs                                  | 18      | | Update Swagger docs with field descriptions and accepted formats                              | 12      | | Implement color property handling in the repository/service layer                             | 28      | | Write unit and integration tests for color assignment and validation                          | 32      | | Code review, refactor if needed, and confirm with PO                                          | 14      |  Total time: 154 minutes  ---  ## Performed Manual Tests  ### Test 1 – Creating a learning space  * **Result:** **200 OK** with a JSON response containing the attributes of a learning space. * **Validation:** The success scenario shows all attributes of a learning space in a floor(learning space ID, type, max capacity, height, width, length, colorFloor, colorWalls and colorCeiling) and success message "The learning space was created succesfully.".  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e2037946-852b-456f-9d06-9398c5998787)  ### Test 2 – Try to create a nonexistent floor  * **Result:** **404 Error** with the messages: "Floor with Id '99999' not found.". * **Validation:** The system successfully handled the error, displaying the expected behavior and the respective error message.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/469769de-34f1-4e8a-8d28-3638661fd72a)  ### Test 3 – Try to create with invalid data  * **Result:** **400 Error**. * **Validation:** The system successfully handled the error, displaying the expected behavior and the respective error message.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/61b6af0c-392e-402e-9153-d6fb45518cf9)

title: SQL-LS-001-013 Paginate floors list
author: RolandoVillavicencio013
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-001  **Name**: Learning spaces management  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow users to explore and manipulate data related to learning spaces. This includes the ability to list available spaces, view details of a specific space, update existing records, create new entries, and retrieve relevant information.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Pagination with user-selectable page size for floors  As an administrator user,   I want to specify the page number and page size when requesting the floors list, So that I can retrieve only the subset of floors I want to see. ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: Request a custom page size   Given the administrator requests the floors list.   When the administrator specifies a valid page size parameter (5, 10, 15 or 20 as max-limit).   Then the response contains that number of floors (if available). ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Request a page with no available elements   Given the administrator requests a page number (0 if not provided) impossible to retrieve due to the current available elements for the selected page size   When the system processes the request   Then the response contains an empty list of floors. ```  ---  ### Non-functional Requirements  | Category            | Description                                                                                                               | |---------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | **Performance**     | The backend must respond to paginated floor list requests within 700 ms under normal conditions.                         | | **Usability**       | The API must accept and validate `page` and `pageSize` parameters with meaningful errors when values are out of bounds.  | | **Maintainability** | The pagination logic for floors should reuse shared pagination utilities or services where possible.                     | | **Reliability**     | API must return consistent and correct subsets of floor data, including empty lists when appropriate.                    |  ---  ### Technical Tasks  | Task                                                                                          | Minutes | |-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Align pagination parameter names and constraints with the PO                                  | 18      | | Add paginated query logic to the floors repository or data service                            | 46      | | Implement backend controller or endpoint logic for parsing `page` and `pageSize`              | 30      | | Add guards for invalid input values and enforce default behavior if parameters are missing    | 25      | | Ensure accurate offset/limit calculation and return empty lists when out-of-range             | 32      | | Document the new parameters and response structure in the API docs (e.g., Swagger)            | 24      | | Write and execute unit tests for multiple scenarios including invalid and edge values         | 40      | | Perform full integration test to validate pagination behavior with real dataset               | 35      | | Submit for code review, address feedback, and validate flow with PO                           | 22      |  Total time: 272 minutes  ---  ### Performed tests  **Test 1 - Request a custom page size**  * **Result:** **200 OK** with a JSON response containing the paginated list of floors. * **Validation:** The success scenario shows a paginated list with all the elements request according to the page size or less if there are no enough available elements. Also, metadata such as the current page size and index, the total count of elements across all pages and the total count of pages is provided.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8bdb4a02-8d6d-48a3-a1cf-93ec5699301f)  **Test 2 - Invalid page size**  * **Result:** **400 Bad Request** with the message: "Page size must be greater than or equal to 1 and less than or equal to 20." * **Validation:** The max page size that can be request is 20 as it was validated with the PO. In that, case, a user friendly message indicate the invalid request to the user.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1215cdc3-7417-4118-91e4-95377a4a95f6)  **Test 3 - Request a page with no available elements**  * **Result:** **200 OK** with a JSON response containing the paginated list of floors. * **Validation:** In this case, there are no available elements for the page request, so an empty list is shown to the user.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/01b45098-63d5-41e7-8141-30e943340c25)  **Test 4 - Default page size and page index**  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/443c8179-eaad-4ffb-a707-77a423e17c5f)

title: SQL-LS-002-013 Paginate floors web list
author: RolandoVillavicencio013
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-002  **Name**: Learning Spaces Web Interface  **Description:** As part of the development of the learning spaces module, the system must provide a user-friendly web interface with essential functionalities, including listing, viewing, updating, creating, and deleting records. It must also support data registration and import through forms. In parallel, initial permission control and security mechanisms will be implemented to support scalability.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Custom Page Size for Paginated Floors List  As an administrator user,   I want to select how many floors are shown per page (10 by default), So that I can control the amount of information visible and navigate the list in the way that best suits me. ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: User selects a custom page size   Given the user is viewing the list of floors   When the user selects a desired page size (5, 10, 15 or 20, as max value)   Then the system updates the list to show that number of floors (if available) per page and adjusts the pagination controls accordingly ```  ```gherkin Scenario: User navigates forward to the next page   Given the user is viewing any page of the floors list   When the user clicks on the "Next" pagination control   Then the system displays the next page of floors using the selected page size ```  ```gherkin Scenario: User navigates backward to the previous page   Given the user is viewing any page of the floors list except the first   When the user clicks on the "Previous" pagination control   Then the system displays the previous page of floors using the selected page size ```  ---  ### Non-functional Requirements | Category            | Description                                                                                                           | |---------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | **Performance**     | Pagination updates must complete and render the list view within 700 ms under normal system conditions.              | | **Usability**       | Page size selection must be accessible and provide feedback (e.g., highlighting current selection, updating controls).| | **Maintainability** | Pagination logic must be encapsulated in reusable components or hooks for future use in similar views.               | | **Reliability**     | The list must correctly reflect the selected page size and maintain consistency across navigation actions.            |  ---  ### Technical Tasks  | Task                                                                                                 | Minutes | |------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Review functional expectations and page size limits with the PO                                     | 20      | | Refactor frontend pagination component to support dynamic page size                                 | 50      | | Implement dropdown or selection control for page size                                               | 28      | | Bind selected page size to state and update fetch logic accordingly                                 | 45      | | Adjust backend API call to accept page size and return proper subset                                | 40      | | Handle navigation logic (Next/Previous) with new dynamic limits                                     | 35      | | Perform manual UX validation across different screen sizes and interactions                         | 30      | | Write unit and integration tests for all edge cases | 50      | | Submit for review, incorporate feedback, and validate with PO                                       | 20      |  Total time: 328 minutes  ---  ### Performed Tests:  **Test 1 - User selects a custom page size**   * The system updates the list to show that number of floors (if available) navigates forward to the next page**  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c17f76e3-304d-4538-866f-b558bddbdaa4)  **Test 3 - User navigates backward to the previous page** title: PQL-IT-007-001 Integration tests to backend
author: AleJimenezRJ
assignees: Giulyos, disotocastro, camsko, WilliamJMF, Felipequesada21, AleJimenezRJ
body: ### **EPIC ID: PQL-IT-007**  **Name**:  Integration tests **Description**:  As part of the core development principles of the system, the codebase must include integration tests to validate the behavior of the backend project. These tests ensure the correct interaction between various modules and services in the system. These tests ensure that components such as the API, database, authentication services, and domain logic work together as expected under "realistic" conditions.  ---  ### User Story: Verify Integration Between API and Database  ```gherkin Feature: Integration Tests In the Backend   As a developer,   I want to ensure that the components interact as expected in the system   So that I can trust that user operations such as create, update, and retrieve are functioning correctly ``` ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Successful execution of backend unit tests Given the backend contains business logic and API components  When the developer runs the unit test suite   Then the system should execute the tests successfully   And all tests should pass with valid assertions   ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Test failure on invalid backendlogic Given a backend component behaves incorrectly   When the developer runs the unit test suite   Then the system should detect the error   And display which test(s) failed with clear messages   ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Code coverage for backend Given the unit tests are implemented correctly   When coverage tools are run   Then at least 80% of the backend codebase should be covered by tests    ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements - Integration Test for backend**  | Category         | Description | |------------------|-------------| | **Performance**  | The test suite should execute in under 20 seconds under normal conditions. | | **Reliability**  | Tests should return consistent results across environments and executions. |  **Maintainability** | Tests must be written in a clean, readable format and separated by scenario. | | **Portability**  | The tests should work across different deployment environments. |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks – Integration Test for backend**  | Task                                                       | Minutes | |------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Review backend components and responsibilities             | 15      | | Define test cases for each method and controller logic     | 20      | | Write unit tests for key services and API handlers         | 40      | | Add assertion logic for all expected outcomes              | 20      | | Run tests and ensure all pass                              | 10      | | Ensure minimum 80% code coverage is achieved               | 10      | | Commit changes, open PR, and request code review           | 15      |  **Total estimate (minutes):** 130   **Total estimate (hours):** 2.17

title: CPD-UI-003-003 Add visual context to indicate user's location
author: angevarart
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-UI-003 - **Name**:  Improve learning component interface usability - **Description**: Enhance the user administrator experience when managing learning components by improving the visual layout and interaction patterns. This includes making information easier to find, reducing user effort, and ensuring that the design is intuitive for administrators.   --- User Story ```gherkin Feature: Visual context to indicate location   As a user, I want to see a clear indication of where I am within the system, So that I can better understand the context of my current actions and navigate more efficiently. ``` --- ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: Display contextual hierarchy in page  Given the user navigates to a section related to a learning component When the page loads Then the page header should display the current section name And it should include the building, floor, and learning space as contextual subtitles ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Display breadcrumb navigation without IDs Given the user is in a nested page or detail view When the page is displayed Then a breadcrumb trail should show the hierarchical path to the current page ``` ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                                                                 | | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Usability**        | Contextual information (e.g., building, floor, space) should be clearly visible and readable.     | | **Consistency**     | Page headers and breadcrumbs must follow a consistent layout and naming convention. | | **Performance**    | Contextual information should load without noticeable delay.     |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                                  | Minutes | |-----------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Add contextual subtitles in page headers | 10      | | Update breadcrumb logic to exclude entity IDs   | 10      | | Get names related to the ID of building, learning space and floor            | 25      | | Adjust layout styles for subtitle readability                          | 5      | | Perform code review and open pull request                             | 10      |  ---  ### **Total Time**  | **Total Minutes** | **Total Hours**     | |-------------------|----------------------| | 60 minutes         | 1 hora    |  ---  ## Performed Tests  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bc0d8303-b325-4ce7-8bf9-7cc1a9225bbe)

title: PQL-TR-008-001 Log using a Database Trigger for Buildings
author: AleJimenezRJ
body: **Name**: Log using Database Trigger for Buildings  **Description**: As part of the core features of development of the system, it is desired to have a log that uses a database trigger with all operations done to the buildings, this to improve the development  process.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Database Trigger for Buildings  As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want a log with all the operations done to buildings So that I can have better control of the system ```  ### Acceptance criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: No building logs registered Given that no logs are registered, and the administrator tries to review all the operations done to buildings When the request is processed  Then the system should have a window with the log And the system should return a list of all actions (create/update/delete), done to a building, this including a  timestamp ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Retrieve all building logs Given that the administrator tries to review all the operations done to buildings When the request is processed  Then the system should have a window with the log And the system should return a 200 OK status with a message saying "There are no registered building logs" and an empty list ```  ## title: PQL-WV-004-009 Pagination in University Window
author: AleJimenezRJ
body: **Name**: Pagination in University Window  **Description**: To improve usability and performance when dealing with large sets of data, the ThemePark@UCR system must implement pagination for all entities in the university management window  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Paginated building in the university management window As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want the list of areas, campus, and universities to be paginated, So that I can navigate through large data sets without performance issues or overwhelming the UI. ```  ### Acceptance criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: Default view paginates areas, campus, and universities Given the administrator is on the list page for any entity, When the list is displayed, Then only a fixed number of entries (e.g., 10 per page) should be shown by default. ``` ---  ```gherkin Scenario: Navigation through pages Given the administrator is viewing the list, When the administrator clicks the "Next" or "Previous" pagination button, Then the corresponding page should be displayed. ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Direct navigation to a page number Given the administrator is on the list page, When the administrator enters a page number or clicks on a specific page indicator, Then that page  should be shown. ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Preserving state during pagination Given the administrator is filtering or searching, When the administrator navigates through pages, Then the applied filters or search terms should persist across paginated views. ``` ## title: PQL-VR-009-001 Unity Visualization
author: AleJimenezRJ
body: ### EPIC ID: PQL-VR-009  **Name**: Unity Visualization  **Description**: One of the most important ideas behind ThemePark@UCR was the idea of having a virtual world where user could interact with the university. For this, the system must allow user to view the buildings in a 3D world.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: View a building using  VR  As a ThemePark@UCR user, I want to be able to use a  VR headset to view the buildings in the campus, So that I have a better experience using the application ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator clicks a button to add a building   Given the administrator has displayed a building they have permission to add   When the administrator clicks the "Add" button   Then the screen changes to the add building screen   And fields appear to add the information of the buillding   And there are "Save" and "Cancel" buttons on the screen.  ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator adds the building information and saves changes   Given the administrator has added all the building information   When clicks the "Save" button   Then the building is saved   And the screen returns to the list of buildings with the newly added building. ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator cancels an add operation   Given the administrator is in add mode   When the administrator clicks the "Cancel" button   Then the changes are discarded, the building is not added   And then the system returns to the list of buildings screen ```  ## title: PQL-AE-001-008 Academic Unit
author: AleJimenezRJ
body: ### EPIC ID: PQL-AE-001  **Name**: Academic Unit  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow administrators to register new academic units in the system to keep track of the university's organization.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Add and register a new academix unit in the system.  As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want to be able to register new academic units in the system, so that I can maintain an up-to-date record of the university. ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: Successful adding of the new academic unit and registering of its information    Given the administrator added a new academic units   When the administrator submits the academic units's information   Then the system should store the new academic unitsas a new entry in the Academic Units table   And each entry should at least include basic information ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: Prevent addition of new academic unita with incorrect property format   Given the administrator tries to add a new academic unit with incorrect property format   When the administrator submits the information   Then the system should reject the registration and display an error message   And the academic unit should not be added to the system. ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: Prevent duplicate academic units registration   Given an Academic unit with the same name that already exists in the system   When the administrator attempts to register a new academic unit with the same name   Then the system should reject the registration and display an error message   And the it should not be added to the system. ```  ## title: PQL-AI-011-001 AI for list buildings
author: AleJimenezRJ

title: PQL-FL-005-004 Filter buildings by matching words
author: AleJimenezRJ
body: **Name**: Filter buildings by matching words  **Description**: As part of the features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must enhance the user administrator experience when managing buildings by adding a filter to the paginated buildings. This includes making information easier to find, reducing user effort, and ensuring that the design is intuitive for administrators.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Filter campus  As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want to be able to use a friendly GUI to filter campus by universities, so that I can more easily maintain an updated record of the campus available for each university ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: The administrator accesses the Building filter functionality Given the administrator is on the Building management window When the administrator views the building list Then a search box is displayed And and the search box display all the building related info  ```  ```gherkin Scenario: The administrator clears the filter Given the administrator has applied a filter And only something is currently displayed When the administrator deletes the filter  Then all building information are displayed again And the filter is reset to its default state ```  ```gherkin Scenario: The administrator cancels an add operation Given the administrator is in list mode When the administrator clicks the "Cancel" button Then the data is discarded And the screen returns to the homepage ```  ```gherkin Scenario: The administrator filters by a something and has no occurrences Given the administrator is filtering by something And there is not associated information  When the administrator selects the filter  Then an empty list is displayed ```   ## title: PQL-AU-012-002 Authorization for building and university related entities in the backend
author: AleJimenezRJ
body: **Name**: Authorization to manage buildings in the backend  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must secure and only allow administrators to manage the buildings, this to avoid improper use of the system in the frontend  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Authorization to manage buildings or universities As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want that only other administrator and me, are allowed to manage the buildings and universities in the system So that only authorized persons can use the critical services ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: A verified administrator tries to manage the buildings   Given the administrator tries to manage the buildings   When the administrator tries to add, edit, or delete a building   Then the action should require some sort of security authetication related to the user   And if passed the verification, the administrator should be able to add, edit, or delete a building. ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: An un-authorized user tries to manage the buildings   Given the user tries to add, edit, or delete a building without having the permissions   When the user enters to the windows   Then the options to do this actions should be hidden  ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: A verified administrator tries to manage the university   Given the administrator tries to manage the university   When the administrator tries to add, or delete a university   Then the action should require some sort of security authetication related to the user   And if passed the verification, the administrator should be able to add, edit, or delete a university. ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: An un-authorized user tries to manage the university   Given the user tries to add, edit, or delete a university without having the permissions   When the user enters to the windows   Then the options to do this actions should be hidden  ```  ## title: PQL-WV-004-008 Consistent error management in add and update services
author: AleJimenezRJ
body: **Name**: Consistent error management in add and update buildings  **Description**: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must have a standarized error management in the user interface so to it's easier for the user to troubleshoot the error.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Standarized errors As a ThemePark@UCR Administrator, I want to be the system to have an uniform error management So that troubleshoot issues quicker and have a better experience overall ```  --- ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator fails to add a bulding and a university   Given the administrator tries to add a building or university   When the administrator fails due to adding something that already exists or doesn't provide the full information   Then the screen shows an error as similar as possible as the others  in the system ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator fails to edit a bulding   Given the administrator tries to edit a building   When the administrator fails due to incorrect data   Then the screen shows an error as similar as possible as the others for edit services in the system ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator wants to list a bulding and a university   Given the administrator is on the home page of the web page   When the administrator tries to list a building and a university   And there are no buildings or universities in the system   Then the screen shows an error as similar as possible as the other functionalities of list in the system for empty scenarios ```  ```gherkin   Scenario: The administrator wants to search a bulding   Given the administrator is on the home page of the web page   When the administrator tries to search a specific building   And that building doesn't exist   Then the screens should show a message of error as similar as possible as the other functionalities of search in the system ```   ## title: SQL-LS-003-002 Roles for Learning Spaces & Floors Backend
author: EJW1001001
body: ### **EPIC ID: SQL-LS-003**  **Name:** Role Management **Description:** To support granular access control within ThemePark\@UCR, the system must allow the enforcement of permissions for operations related to Learning Spaces and Floors. This ensures that only users with the correct roles can perform actions like creating, updating, deleting, or reading these entities via the backend interface (e.g., Swagger).  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Enforce permissions on Learning Space and Floor operations  As an administrator   I want user actions in the backend to be restricted based on their role   So that users without appropriate permissions cannot perform unauthorized modifications ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: User with viewer role accesses Learning Spaces Given the user has the "viewer" role When they attempt to list or read Learning Spaces Then the system allows the operation And they are not able to update, delete, or create ```  ```gherkin Scenario: User with editor role updates a Floor Given the user has the "editor" role When they attempt to update a Floor entity Then the system allows the update And prevents creation or deletion if not permitted by their role ```  ```gherkin Scenario: User with full access deletes a Learning Space Given the user has the "admin" role When they attempt to delete a Learning Space Then the system processes the deletion successfully ```  ```gherkin Scenario: User without role attempts any action Given the user is authenticated but has no role assigned When they try to perform any Learning Space or Floor operation Then the system denies access with an unauthorized response ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                                                              | | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | **Security**        | Endpoint access must be secured by role-based authorization policies.    | | **Reliability**     | The system must consistently enforce permissions across all controllers. | | **Auditability**    | All access attempts (granted and denied) should be logged with metadata. | | **Usability**       | Swagger or UI must clearly indicate unauthorized operations.             | | **Maintainability** | Permission logic must be centralized and easy to extend for new roles.   |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                             | Minutes | | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | Define permission enums and roles (`Viewer`, `Editor`, `Admin`)  |       | | Configure authorization policies in backend (e.g., ASP.NET Core) |       | | Apply `[Authorize(Policy = "...")]` to Learning Space endpoints  |       | | Apply same logic to Floor controller endpoints                   |      | | Implement and test fallback for users without assigned roles     |       | | Log all access control decisions (success/failure)               |      | | Write unit/integration tests for all permission combinations     |       | | Update Swagger annotations to reflect access limitations         |      | | Code review and PR                                               |       |  **Total estimate:**

title: SQL-LS-004-001 Spaces Standardization
author: EJW1001001
body: ##  EPIC ID: SQL-LS-004 – Learning Spaces Page Standardization  **Name:** Standardization of UI for Floor and Learning Spaces Pages  **Description:** To ensure a consistent and professional user experience across the ThemePark\@UCR platform, we aim to standardize the UI components, layout, and interaction patterns used on the **Floor** and **Learning Spaces** pages. This includes reaching a team consensus on the position and styling of elements such as labels, inputs, text boxes, selects, tables, and pagination components. The result should be a clear, uniform, and easily maintainable UI that can serve as a template for similar CRUD pages in the future.  --- ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Standardize the structure of Learning Spaces pages  As a frontend developer   I want consistent rules for how components are laid out on these pages   So that users have a uniform experience and code is easier to maintain ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Consistent structure for both pages Given that I open the Floor and Learning Spaces pages   When I inspect their structure and components   Then I should see the same layout rules applied to labels, inputs, and buttons ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Unified pagination experience Given that both pages include paginated data   When users interact with pagination controls   Then the controls should look and behave identically across pages ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Consistent form field alignment Given that both pages contain forms for data input or editing   When a user interacts with the form   Then all labels and fields should be consistently aligned, sized, and styled ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Use of shared frontend components Given the components (input, select, etc.) used on both pages   When developers build or update those pages   Then they must use the shared component library or follow the same style guide ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                                                                 | | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Usability**       | Pages must provide a consistent and intuitive interface.                    | | **Accessibility**   | Labels, selects, and text fields must comply with accessibility standards.  | | **Maintainability** | Changes to styles or layout logic should only require edits in shared code. | | **Scalability**     | The standard must be applicable to future entity-based pages.               |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                                   | Minutes | | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | Analyze current implementations of Floor and Learning Spaces pages     |       | | Define and document a unified layout structure (labels, spacing, etc.) |       | | Refactor both pages to apply the agreed layout and component spacing   |      | | Ensure consistent pagination layout and behavior                       |      | | Refactor component use (inputs, selects, etc.) to use shared versions  |       | | Create internal dev documentation for the standard                     |       | | Review changes with UX/UI and get approval                             |       | | QA testing to ensure layout consistency and no regressions             |       | | Code review and merge process                                          |     |  **Total estimate:**

title: SQL-LS-003-001 Roles in Learning Spaces & Floors Frontend
author: EJW1001001
body: ### **EPIC ID: SQL-LS-003**  **Name:** Role Management **Description:** To support granular access control within ThemePark\@UCR, the system must allow the enforcement of permissions for operations related to Learning Spaces and Floors. This ensures that only users with the correct roles can perform actions like creating, updating, deleting, or reading these entities via the backend interface (e.g., Swagger).  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Restrict UI interactions based on user roles  As a user   I want to see only the interface elements I'm allowed to interact with   So that I have a clear understanding of what actions I’m authorized to perform ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Viewer role sees only read-only interface Given a user has the "viewer" role When they navigate to the Learning Spaces or Floor page Then they should only see options to list and read And all edit, delete, or create buttons should be hidden or disabled ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Editor role can update but not delete Given a user has the "editor" role When they open a detail view Then they should be able to see and use the update form And not see buttons for creating or deleting ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Admin role sees full interface Given a user has the "admin" role When they open any entity view Then they should have full access to all actions including create, edit, and delete ``` ```gherkin Scenario: User with no role sees restricted interface Given a user is logged in but has no role When they navigate to these pages Then they should be blocked from any action And redirected or shown an unauthorized access message ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category        | Description                                                                 | | --------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Security**    | All UI restrictions should reflect backend permission rules.                | | **Usability**   | The interface must clearly indicate which actions are available or blocked. | | **Consistency** | UI logic must be the same across both Learning Spaces and Floors.           | | **Scalability** | New roles or permissions should be easy to add without UI rewrites.         |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                           | Minutes | | -------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | Integrate user role info into frontend state/context           |       |  | Define role-based permission matrix (Viewer, Editor, Admin)    |       |  | Conditionally render buttons/forms based on role               |       |  | Apply restrictions to Learning Spaces page                     |      |  | Apply restrictions to Floor page                               |       |  | Display tooltip or message for disabled actions                |       |  | Handle unauthorized page access (optional redirect or warning) |      |  | Write unit tests for role-based UI conditions                  |       |  | Code review and PR                                             |       |   **Total estimate:**   ---

title: SQL-LS-005-001 List Learning Spaces Audit Log
author: KPalaciosG
body: ### **EPIC ID: SQL-LS-003**  **Name:** Learning Space Activity Logging **Description:**  To ensure transparency and traceability within ThemePark@UCR, the system must provide functionality to log and list all activities performed on Learning Spaces. This includes operations such as creation, updates, and deletions. The information should be stored in an audit log and be accessible for administrative review, facilitating monitoring and checking information related to the learning spaces.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: List all learning spaces audit logs  As an administrator   I want to retrieve a list of all learning spaces audit logs   So that I can monitor changes related to the learning spaces. ``` ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Retrieve all user audit logs Given that the administrator queries the audit log When the request is processed   Then the system should return a list of audit records, including action type (create/update/delete) and timestamp.  ```  ```gherkin Scenario: No audit logs in the system Given that no audit records exist   When the endpoint is called   Then the system should return a 200 OK status with a message saying "There are no registered user audits" and an empty list ```  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  Task | Minutes -- | -- Validate audit log structure and fields with the PO |  Ensure test database has audit entries for testing |  Implement application layer logic to retrieve all audit logs |  Implement method in the audit repository (infrastructure layer) |  Handle empty result sets with appropriate response |  Write unit and integration tests for success and empty scenarios |  Create pull request and request code review |  Validate with PO that the functionality meets expectations |   **Total estimate:**

title: SQL-LS-001-014 Filter Learning Spaces by Partial Name
author: EJW1001001
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-001  *Name*: Learning spaces management  *Description*: As part of the core features of ThemePark@UCR, the system must allow users to explore and manipulate data related to learning spaces. This includes the ability to list available spaces, view details of a specific space, update existing records, create new entries, and retrieve relevant information.  ---  ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Search learning spaces by name  As a user   I want to search for learning spaces by typing a full or partial name   So that I can quickly find a relevant list of learning spaces without needing the exact name ``` ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Search by partial name returns matching learning spaces Given that the system has learning spaces stored When specific learning space ```  ```gherkin Scenario: No matches found Given that no learning space name contains the input string "xyz" When a user submits the search Then the backend should return an empty list ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                                                                 | | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Performance**     | The search should return results within 300ms for typical input sizes.      | | **Scalability** **Maintainability** | Search logic should be centralized and reusable for other domain entities.  |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                             | method to encapsulate search logic   |         | | Implement search filtering using `LIKE` or equivalent (EF Core)  |         | | Ensure case-insensitive matching logic                           |         | | Validate that input strings are properly sanitized               |         | | Write unit tests for search scenarios (partial, exact, no match) |         | | Add integration test with real database records                  |         | | Code review and PR                                               |         |  **Total estimate:**

title: SQL-LS-002-014 Filter Learning Spaces by Partial Name Via Web
author: EJW1001001
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-LS-002  **Name**: Learning Spaces Web Interface  **Description:** As part of the development of the learning spaces module, the system must provide a user-friendly web interface with essential functionalities, including listing, viewing, updating, creating, and deleting records. results must be fully keyboard-navigable.                |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                            | Minutes | | title: CPD-DB-006-001 Implement trigger
author: angevarart
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-DB-006 - **Name**: Logging and Traceability of Learning Components - **Description**: This EPIC includes the development of a backend trigger that stores audit information in a dedicated log table, as well as the implementation of a frontend module to view this log. The log viewer will present the information in a clear, non-paginated list format, sorted chronologically to help administrators and system users easily audit the history of component modifications. --- ## User Story ```gherkin Feature: Automatically log learning component changes As a system administrator, I want all changes to learning components to be automatically logged in an audit table, So that I can trace historical modifications for accountability and compliance. ``` ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: Trigger logs insert operations Given a new learning component is added When the database trigger fires Then an entry is recorded in the audit log with action type "INSERT" Scenario: Trigger logs update operations Given an existing learning component is modified When the database trigger fires Then an entry is recorded in the audit log with action type "UPDATE" and modified fields Scenario: Trigger logs delete operations Given a learning component is logically deleted When the database trigger fires Then an entry is recorded in the audit log with action type "DELETE" ``` --- ### **Non-functional Requirements** | Category | Description | | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Reliability** | The trigger must reliably fire on all `INSERT`, `UPDATE`, and `DELETE` operations. | | **Traceability** | The log must clearly reflect who made changes, what changed, and when. | | **Performance** | The trigger logic must not introduce noticeable latency on database writes. | | **Auditability** | The frontend list view must present audit records in a clear, chronological format. | | **Maintainability** | The trigger code and log schema should be documented and easy to update. | --- ### **Technical Tasks** | Task | Minutes | |----------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Design audit log schema and trigger fields | 25 | | Implement trigger on LearningComponents table | 40 | | Test trigger for `INSERT`, `UPDATE`, and `DELETE` operations | 30 | | Create stored procedure for frontend to query audit log | 20 | | Build frontend log viewer component (non-paginated list) | 30 | | Sort audit records chronologically in the frontend | 10 | | Display component metadata (user, date, action, summary) | 20 | | Write unit/integration tests for trigger and procedure | 25 | | Review logic with backend team | 10 | --- ### **Total Time** | **Total Minutes** | **Total Hours** | |-------------------|----------------------| | 210 minutes | 3 hours 30 minutes | --- ## **Performed Tests** ### Test 1 – Show logs in the database table * **Result:** shows entries with actions 'CREATED', 'UPDATED', and 'DELETED'

title: SQL-IF-001-001 Building pages color view
author: andervar
body: ### EPIC ID: SQL-IF-001   **Name:** Color view management for spatial entities **Description:** To enhance user experience and visual identification within ThemePark@UCR, the system should support managing color views for buildings, learning spaces, and learning components. This includes the ability to view, assign, update, and display colors in read, create, and edit pages for each entity.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Assign, edit and display color for buildings  As an administrator user, I want to assign, edit and view a color attribute for each building, So that users can visually distinguish different buildings within the application. ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: Assign color when creating a building   Given the administrator is on the create building page,   When the administrator selects a color and submits the form,   Then the new building is created with the selected color and the color is visible on subsequent views. ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Update color on edit   Given the administrator is on the edit building page,   When the administrator changes the color and submits,   Then the building’s color is updated and reflected on all relevant pages. ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Display colors in read consistent across supported browsers and devices.                     |  ---  ### Technical Tasks  | Task                                                       | Minutes | support color CRUD                  | 26      | | Write unit tests for color logic                            | 32      | | Perform integration testing for color assignment and display| 30      | | title: CPD-LC-002-007 Refactor of polymorphism
author: luis-fch
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-LC-002   - **Name**: Learning Component Core Refactors   - **Description**: This epic aims to improve the design, maintainability, and scalability of the learning component module through architectural refactors, including clean abstraction of mappers and component transformation logic.  ---  ## User Story   ```gherkin Feature: Refactor mapping logic for learning components using polymorphism  As a developer,   I want to refactor the transformation logic from entities to DTOs and vice versa   So that the system can be more maintainable, extensible, and loosely coupled. ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: Centralized and extensible mapping logic for LearningComponent hierarchy Given a set of LearningComponent-derived entities (e.g., Projector, Whiteboard) When the system transforms them to DTOs or reconstructs them from DTOs Then it should use a centralized GlobalMapper that dynamically resolves the correct mapper ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Support for different DTO variants (e.g., with or without ID) Given the need to handle both DTOs with IDs and DTOs without IDs When using the GlobalMapper Then the correct mapper for each DTO type should be invoked automatically ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                                                                 | |---------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | **Maintainability** | New components or DTOs can be added by simply registering a new mapper.     | | **Extensibility**   | The system supports both DTOs with IDs and without IDs using separate mappers. | | **Robustness**      | Invalid or unregistered types throw clear exceptions with useful messages.  | | **Consistency**     | Mapping logic behaves uniformly across all handler and service layers.      |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                                                      | Minutes | |-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Design generic base mapper class for LearningComponent <T, TDto>                          | 60     | | Implement `ProjectorDtoMapper`, `WhiteboardDtoMapper`, `ProjectorNoIdDtoMapper`, `WhiteboardNoIdDtoMapper`  based on the new base class        | 30     | | Implement `GlobalMapper` to dynamically resolve correct mapper per type                  | 70      | | Add support for DTOs without IDs and their corresponding mappers                         | 10      | | Refactor handlers (e.g., `GetLearningComponentsByIdHandler`) to use the GlobalMapper     | 40      | | Manual testing and debugging of runtime mapper resolution                                | 40      | | Discussion and technical validation with assistants regarding polymorphism and patterns  | 80      | | Final review and cleanup                                                                 | 10      |  ---  ### **Total Time**  | **Total Minutes** | **Total Hours**     | |-------------------|----------------------| |  340 minutes       | 5 hours 40 minutes   |  ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 – Mapping from Projector entity to DTO   **Validation**: A `Projector` instance is converted correctly to `ProjectorDto`.  ```csharp var projector = new Projector(...); var dto = globalMapper.ToDto<ProjectorDto>(projector); Assert.Equal(projector.ComponentId, dto.Id); ```  ---  ### Test 2 – Mapping from DTO to Projector entity   **Validation**: A `ProjectorDto` is reconstructed successfully into a domain entity.  ```csharp var dto = new ProjectorDto(...); var projector = globalMapper.ToEntity<Projector>(dto); Assert.Equal(dto.Id, projector.ComponentId); ```  ---  ### Test 3 – Invalid type throws exception   **Validation**: An unregistered type results in a clear exception.  ```csharp var unknownComponent = new UnknownComponent(); Assert.Throws<NotSupportedException>(() => globalMapper.ToDto<LearningComponentDto>(unknownComponent)); ```  ---  ### Test 4 – Handler integration works correctly   **Validation**: The handler `GetLearningComponentsByIdHandler` uses the new mapper system and returns the expected list of DTOs without switch or type checking.  ---

title: SPT-AI-001-001 Retrieve Recent Modifications for User via Identity Number using an AI agent
author: andresMurillo23
body: ### **EPIC ID:** SPT-AI-001 **Name:** AI-Driven Insights for User Management **Description:** To enhance operational oversight and data integrity within the User Management module, ThemePark\@UCR must integrate AI-powered insights based on the system’s existing database. These insights will allow administrators to ask specific questions and receive intelligent responses about the current state of users, roles, activity patterns, and potential data inconsistencies—enabling faster and more accurate decision-making. --- ### **User Story** ```gherkin Feature: View recent changes for a specific user by identity number As an administrator I want to retrieve the recent changes made to a user’s account by providing their identity number and a time range So that I can audit activity and ensure accountability for modifications ``` --- ### **Acceptance Criteria** ```gherkin Scenario: Valid user with recent changes Given the administrator provides a valid identity number and week range When recent changes exist for that user in the specified time frame Then the system should return a list of audit entries with relevant modification data ``` ```gherkin Scenario: No recent changes Given the system is queried for a valid user When no changes exist within the given number of weeks Then the system should respond clearly that no modifications were found ``` ``` gherkin Scenario: Invalid input Given an invalid identity number or non-positive week value When the tool is invoked Then the system should return appropriate validation error messages ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Result format Given audit entries are returned Then each item should include timestamp, modified fields, and action summary ``` --- ### **Non-Functional Requirements** Category | Description -- | -- Usability | Errors and results should be presented in a readable, structured format. Performance | Should return results within 2 seconds for up to 1,000,000 audit entries. Security | Only authorized users may access this audit tool. Accuracy | Data must come from the real-time user audit log, excluding invalid entries. UX/UI | Should be accessible from the admin insights or audit tools panel. --- ### **Technical Tasks** Task | Est. Time -- | -- Add validation for identity number format and week range | 10 mins Implement query to filter audit logs by user ID and date range | 20 mins Map filtered results to DTOs for display | 15 mins Return formatted response or error messages | 10 mins Handle case of no matching audit records | 10 mins Add unit tests for validation and service logic | 20 mins Add integration test for tool endpoint | 20 mins Code review and pull request | 10 mins **Total Estimate:** 115 minutes (\~1.9 hours) ## Performed Tests ### Test 1 - Returns recent modifications for a valid user - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario from the acceptance criteria: “the system should return a list of audit entries with relevant modification data”. --- ### Test 2 - Shows friendly message when no changes found - **Validation:** Satisfies the success scenario: “the system should respond clearly that no modifications were found”. --- ### Test 3 - Invalid identity number triggers helpful error - **Validation:** Satisfies the scenario: “appropriate validation error messages should be returned” for invalid formats like missing dashes or letters. --- ### Test 4 - Invalid week range prompts clear feedback - **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criteria: “non-positive week value returns an error explaining the issue in simple terms”.

body: ### **EPIC ID: FE-LS-003**  **Name:** Learning Space Audit Log UI   **Description:** To ensure transparency and traceability within ThemePark@UCR, the system must provide a frontend interface to visualize all activities performed on Learning Spaces. This includes creation, updates, and deletions. The interface should present the audit log in a user-friendly way, accessible for administrative review, and allow filtering and navigation to monitor changes efficiently.  ---  ### **User Story**  Feature: Display all learning spaces audit logs  As an administrator   I want to view a list of all learning spaces audit logs   So that I can easily monitor changes related to the learning spaces.  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  Scenario: Display all learning spaces audit logs   Given that the administrator navigates to the audit logs panel   When the audit log data is successfully retrieved   Then the UI should display a list of audit records, including action type (create/update/delete), timestamp, and responsible user.  Scenario: No audit logs available   Given that there are no audit records   When the administrator accesses the audit logs panel   Then the UI should display a message saying "There are no registered audit logs" and an empty state illustration or placeholder.  ---  ### **Non-Functional Requirements**  | Category        | Description                                                                  | | --------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Usability**   | The audit log list must be easy to read, support pagination, and display clear labels. | | **Performance** | The UI should render results within 2 seconds . | | **Security**    | Only authorized admin users should be able to access this interface.          | | **Accuracy**    | The UI must display real-time audit data fetched from the backend API.         | | **UX/UI**       | The audit log view should be accessible from the same panel as the search or insights tab, with consistent theme and navigation. |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task | Minutes | | ---- | ------- | | Design the UI layout for the audit logs panel |  60| | Implement the audit log table with action type, timestamp, and user columns | 40 | | Add empty state illustration and message handling when no logs are present |  30| | Implement pagination and loading states |  35| | Connect the UI to the audit logs API endpoint | 25 | | Ensure proper error handling and display user-friendly error messages | 25 | | Restrict access to authorized admin users | 35 | | Write unit and integration tests for the UI components |  50| | Create pull request and request code review |  35| | Validate with PO that the UI meets usability and functional expectations |  35|  **Total estimate:** 370 minutes / 6.16 hours

title: CPD-UI-003-004 Add text filter to learning components table
author: luis-fch
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-UI-003 - **Name**: Improve learning component interface usability - **Description**: Enhance the user administrator experience when managing learning components by improving the visual layout and interaction patterns. This includes making information easier to find, reducing user effort, and ensuring that the design is intuitive for administrators. --- ### User Story ```gherkin Feature: Add text filter to paginated table. As an administrator of ThemePark@UCR I want to filter the table of learning components So that I can search components in the table. ``` --- ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: Filter components by identifier Given a valid learning component identifier When the administrator wants to filter components in the table entering an identifier in the search field Then table is updated correctly and shows only the components that match with the given identifier ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Filter components by orientation Given a valid learning component orientation When the administrator wants to filter components in the table entering the orientation in the search field Then table is updated correctly and shows only the components that match with the given orientation ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Filter components by invalid identifier Given an invalid learning component identifier When the administrator wants to filter components in the table entering the identifier in the search field Then table is updated correctly and shows a message saying that there is no components to show ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Filter components by invalid orientation Given an invalid learning component orientation When the administrator wants to filter components in the table entering the orientation in the search field Then table is updated correctly and shows a message saying that there is no components to show ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Filter components by invalid search text Given an invalid search text to filter on When the administrator wants to filter components in the table entering the invalid search text Then table is updated correctly and shows a message saying that there is no components to show ``` ### Technical Tasks Task | Minutes -- | -- Validation with the to support the filtering feature | 60 Create Kiota repository in the frontend to integrate the backend service | 30 Update the table page to use the search bar component | 15 Integrate the search functionality in the table page | 15 Code review and pull request | 15 Documentation updates | 15 **Total estimate (minutes):** 360 **Total estimate (hours):** 6 --- ### Performed Tests #### Filtering tests https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3112cca9-f8a0-4a36-a768-a1a82edaab74 #### Invalid search test #### Backend tests All unit and integration tests were updated to comply with the new filtering functionality.

title: SQL-AI-006-001 AI Agent
author: KPalaciosG
body: ## **EPIC ID: SQL-AI-006** **Name:** LLM Integration for ThemePark@UCR  **Description:** The goal is to integrate a Large Language Model (LLM) into the ThemePark@UCR system to enable natural language interaction with domain data. This includes the ability to query and manipulate entities such as Learning Spaces, Floors, and Buildings through an AI agent. The integration will require project configuration and a connection to an external API provider like OpenAI, allowing the system to understand user prompts and respond with accurate, relevant information.  ---  ### User Story  ```gherkin Feature: Enable Copilot to interact with application services via MCP Server  As a developer   I want an MCP server that exposes application-layer tools   So that I can use GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code to automate and interact with backend services ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria  ```gherkin Scenario: Successfully create and configure MCP Server Given the developer intends to expose application services as tools When the MCP Server is set up and configured Then the server should be able to register and expose tools corresponding to application services And provide secure endpoints for Copilot consumption ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Copilot uses a tool to consume an application-layer service Given the MCP Server is running and tools are configured When Copilot in Visual Studio Code sends a request to a tool endpoint Then the request should be routed to the appropriate application service and return the expected result ```  ```gherkin Scenario: Secure access and error handling Given the MCP Server is configured for Copilot access When an invalid or unauthorized request is made Then the server should reject the request and return a meaningful error message without exposing sensitive information ```  ---  ## Non-Functional Requirements  | Category            | Description                                                                 | | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Performance**     | The MCP server should respond within 1–2 seconds for standard operations    | | **Scalability**     | The solution must support multiple concurrent tool invocations              | | **Availability**    | High availability expected; must recover gracefully from failures           | | **Security**        | Secure endpoints, authentication, and least-privilege access enforced       | | **Usability**       | Clear documentation for configuration and tool usage                        | | **Maintainability** | Modular codebase with clear separation of concerns                          | | **Portability**     | Should be easily deployable to different environments                       | | **Compliance**      | Must adhere to internal compliance and data handling standards              | | **Reliability**     | All critical paths covered by automated tests and integration tests         | | **Localization**    | Not considered for the initial implementation                               |  ---  ## Task Division  | Technical Task align with Product Owner                   |                     20 | | Clone or initialize MCP server repository                            |                      5 | | Create feature branch for MCP integration                           |                      3 |  ---  ### Back-End Development with Clean Code & Clean Architecture  | Technical Task                                                                           | Minimum Time (Minutes) | | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------: | | Design modular MCP server architecture                                                   |                     40 | | Implement secure authentication and authorization for tool endpoints                     |                     30 | | Configure environment variables and settings for API/tool registry                       |                     20 | | Implement tool registry to dynamically expose application-layer services as tools        |                     45 | | Integrate application-layer services into tool interfaces                                |                     30 | | Validate tool input/output and ensure safe request/response handling                    |                     25 | | Test tool execution via Copilot in Visual Studio Code                                    |                     30 | |
