title: SPT-UI-006-010 Instant Search in User Management Table
author: andresMurillo23
body: ### **EPIC ID:** SPT-UI-006 **Name:** User Interface **Description:** ThemePark\@UCR must provide comprehensive tools for user management. This includes an interface to view, create, modify, and delete users, with role and permission control from a single window. --- ### **User Story** ```gherkin Feature: Instant search in the user management table As an administrator I want to instantly filter users by username, full name, or ID number So that I can quickly locate specific users without scrolling or paginating unnecessarily ``` --- ### **Acceptance Criteria** ```gherkin Scenario: Filter users by username Given the user management page is loaded When the administrator types a partial or full username into the search bar (e.g., “ana.mor”) Then the table should update to show only users whose usernames match the input ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Filter users by full name Given the administrator types part of a full name into the search bar (e.g., “Fabian”) Then the table should display users whose full names contain the input ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Filter users by ID number Given the administrator types part of an identification number (e.g., “1002”) Then the table should display users whose ID includes the string ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Clear the input field Given the administrator entered a search query When they clear the search input Then the full list of users should be displayed again ``` ```gherkin Scenario: No results found Given the administrator enters a string that does not match any users (e.g., “zzzz”) Then a message should be displayed indicating “No results found” or an empty state ``` --- ### **Non-Functional Requirements** | Category | Description | | ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | **Usability** | The search must be responsive and react as the user types. | | **Performance** | Debounce input (e.g., 300ms) to avoid firing the search logic too often. | | **UX/UI** | Use MUI components such as `TextField`, `Table`, and `Typography`. | | **Accessibility** | The search field and resulting table must be fully keyboard-navigable. | | **Consistency** | Behavior and styling must match other admin modules within the system. | --- ### **Technical Tasks** | Task | Est. Time | | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------- | | Add `TextField` component for search input | 15 mins | | Bind input to state using `useState` | 10 mins | | Implement `useDebounce` logic with `useEffect` | 20 mins | | Filter user data based on `username`, `full name`, or `ID number` | 20 mins | | Update table dynamically with filtered results | 15 mins | | Display message when no results match the query | 10 mins | | Reset the table to show all users when input is cleared | 10 mins | | (Optional) Add loading indicator using Skeleton or CircularProgress | 10 mins | | Write unit tests for filtering logic | 20 mins | | Code review and pull request process | 10 mins | **Total Estimate:** 140 minutes (\~2.3 hours) --- ## Performed Tests ### Test 1 – Filter users by username * **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criteria: *“When the administrator types a partial or full username (e.g., 'ana.mor') and presses Enter or clicks the search button, the table updates to show only matching users.”* * **System Behavior:** Upon confirmation (Enter or search button), the system filters users whose username includes the input string. Results are updated accordingly. --- ### Test 2 – Filter users by full name * **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criterion: *“When the administrator types a name fragment (e.g., 'Fabian') and confirms the search, users with matching full names are displayed.”* * **System Behavior:** Upon confirmation (Enter or search button), the system filters users whose name includes the input string. Results are updated accordingly. --- ### Test 3 – Filter users by ID number * **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criterion: *“When the administrator types part of an ID (e.g., '1002') and confirms the search, the table updates with matching users.”* * **System Behavior:** The search is applied to the `identityNumber` field. Users whose IDs contain the input string are shown in the results. --- ### Test 4 – Clear the input field * **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criterion: *“When the administrator clears the search query and confirms, the full user list is restored.”* * **System Behavior:** Upon submitting an empty search (either by clearing and pressing Enter or clicking the search button), the system reloads the complete list of users. --- ### Test 5 – No results found * **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criterion: *“When the administrator searches for a string that matches no users (e.g., 'zzzz'), a message is shown indicating no results.”* * **System Behavior:** The table becomes empty and displays a user-friendly message such as “No hay elementos para mostrar” when no matches are found across any attribute.

title: SPT-AU-002-007 Register a new user in the system
author: andresMurillo23
assignees: ebai03, tatsparamo, cyaos, isabellarodriguezs, andresMurillo23, gaelalpizar
body: ### **EPIC ID: SPT-AU-002** * **Name:** Authentication * **Description:** As part of the security infrastructure of ThemePark\@UCR, the system must implement user authentication using Microsoft Auth B2C. This ensures that only authorized individuals can access the system, enabling secure personalization, protection of data, and controlled access to features. --- ### **User Story** ```gherkin Feature: Register a new user in the system As a person interested in using the ThemePark system I want to register using Microsoft Azure AD B2C with pre-validation through an API Connector So that my information is validated securely and I can gain access to the system with a valid account ``` --- ### **Acceptance Criteria** ```gherkin Scenario: Successful registration Given I provide valid registration data (name, email, password, etc.) And the data passes all business rules and format validations via API Connector When I attempt to register Then the system should create my account in Azure AD B2C And synchronize my profile with the backend database And redirect me to a welcome page or the login page ``` ```gherkin Scenario: Failed registration due to invalid data Given I provide registration data that violates business rules or format validations When I attempt to register Then the API Connector should reject the registration And the system should display clear and meaningful error messages And no account should be created in Azure AD B2C or in the backend ``` --- ### **Non-functional Requirements – Registration** | Category | Description | | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Performance** | The registration process must complete in under 3 seconds under normal system load. | | **Security** | Passwords must meet complexity requirements defined in B2C policies and never be stored in plaintext. All API communication must use HTTPS. | | **Portability** | The registration feature must work seamlessly across major web browsers and mobile devices. | | **Maintainability** | API Connector URL, tenant settings (client ID, scopes, redirect URIs) must be externalized and configurable. | --- ### **Technical Tasks** | Task | Minutes | | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------- | | Configure Azure AD B2C signup user flow with API Connector for pre-validation | 30 | | Develop backend API for API Connector to validate input using Value Objects (Email, Phone, IdentityNumber, etc.) | 30 | | Configure API Connector to invoke backend API during user signup flow | 15 | | Implement frontend flow to handle registration, including client-side validation and handling API Connector errors | 30 | | Test end-to-end flow: successful registration and backend synchronization | 20 | | Test end-to-end flow: failed registration due to validation errors | 20 | | Document the registration process, API Connector integration, and error handling strategy | 10 | | Perform code review and submit pull request | 10 | **Total estimate:** 165 minutes (\~2.7 hours) --- ## Performed Tests ### Test 1 – Successful registration * **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criteria” * * **System Behavior:** The data passes all business rules and format validations via API Connector. ### Test 2 – Failed registration due to invalid data * **Validation:** Satisfies the acceptance criteria” * * **System Behavior:** The API Connector should reject the registration.

title: CPD-DB-006-002 Create a page of learning component logs
author: angevarart
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-DB-006 - **Name**: Logging and Traceability of Learning Components - **Description**: This EPIC includes the development of a backend trigger that stores audit information in a dedicated log table, as well as the implementation of a frontend module to view this log. The log viewer will present the information in a clear, non-paginated list format, sorted chronologically to help administrators and system users easily audit the history of component modifications. --- ## User Story ```gherkin Feature: View audit logs for learning component changes As an administrator, I want to see a clear, chronological list of changes made to learning components, So that I can monitor and audit activity for accountability. ``` ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: Display all audit log records Given audit log entries exist in the database When I open the audit log viewer Then I see all logs listed in descending order by date Scenario: Show key metadata for each log Given a list of audit records When each record is displayed Then it includes timestamp, action type, learningComponentId, and characteristics ``` --- ### **Non-functional Requirements** | Category | Description | | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Consistency** | The audit log viewer must follow shared UI layout and style guide. | | **Readability** | Information should be clearly displayed with consistent formatting. | | **Performance** | Rendering a large number of audit logs should not slow down the page. | | **Accessibility** | Log entries should be readable via screen readers and keyboard navigation. | | **Maintainability** | The viewer component should be reusable and decoupled from data logic. | --- ### **Technical Tasks** | Task | Minutes | |----------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Create frontend component to list audit logs | 30 | | Fetch data using stored procedure from backend | 20 | | Map stored procedure results to display model | 15 | | Implement non-paginated scrollable list view | 20 | | Style list using existing layout and component library | 20 | | Include fields: date, user, action type, description | 15 | | Sort entries descending by timestamp | 10 | | Write snapshot/component tests | 25 | | Review implementation with product/design team | 10 | | Finalize code review and submit PR | 15 | --- ### **Total Time** | **Total Minutes** | **Total Hours** | |-------------------|----------------------| | 180 minutes | 3 hours | --- ## **Performed Tests** ### Test 1 – Show log that is not empty * **Result:** shows the history of logs ### Test 2 – Show log that is empty * **Result:** shows a message

title: PQL-AI-011-004 IA For University

title: CPD-AI-005-002 AI query that gets a learning component by its ID
author: Earaya12
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-AI-005 - **Name**: AI Integration for Learning Components - **Description**: This epic focuses on enhancing the Theme Park backend by integrating an AI agent capable of interpreting user queries and retrieving learning component data. The goal is to create natural language interaction capabilities powered by existing handler and service layers. --- ## User Story ```gherkin Feature: AI-powered retrieval of learning component by ID As a user of the AI agent, I want to ask for a specific learning component by its ID So that I can retrieve its full configuration and details using natural language. ``` --- ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: AI agent returns a learning component by ID Given a valid learning component ID (e.g., "123") When the user requests "Show me the learning component 123" Then the AI should call the GetSingleLearningComponentByIdHandler and return a structured response ``` ```gherkin Scenario: AI handles invalid component ID gracefully Given an invalid or non-existent ID When the user requests it Then the AI should return a friendly error or fallback message, not a system error ``` --- ### **Non-functional Requirements** | Category | Description | |---------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | **Robustness** | Invalid or null inputs return meaningful messages from the AI agent. | | **Integration** | The AI calls directly into the existing handler without duplicating logic. | | **Reusability** | The AI reuses application layer services (`LearningComponentServices`). | | **Maintainability** | Future learning component types remain supported without changing the AI. | --- ### **Technical Tasks** | Task | Minutes | |----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Integrate the server for the AI | 20 | | Created the Tool method related to the endpoint | 20 | | Final testing and code review | 20 | --- ### **Total Time** | **Total Minutes** | **Total Hours** | |-------------------|----------------------| | 60 minutes | 1 hours 0 minutes | --- ## Performed Tests ### Test 1 – The AI agent give information of a valid id. ### Test 2 - The AI agent returns a friendly error or fallback message, not a system error, when an invalid id is asked. ---

title: CPD-AI-005-001 AI query that creates a projector
author: Earaya12
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-AI-005 - **Name**: AI Integration for Learning Components - **Description**: This epic focuses on enhancing the Theme Park backend by integrating an AI agent capable of interpreting user queries and retrieving learning component data. The goal is to create natural language interaction capabilities powered by existing handler and service layers. --- ## User Story ```gherkin Feature: AI-powered projector creation As a user, I want to use natural language to ask the AI to add a new projector So that I can quickly configure and register a projector in a learning space without filling out complex forms. ``` --- ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: Successfully adding a projector via AI input Given the user provides a natural language prompt like "Add a projector in learning space 3 facing north with dimensions 2x1.5x0.5 and projecting Math video" When the AI processes the request Then it should convert it to a valid DTO and call the appropriate handler And the response should confirm that the projector was created Scenario: Invalid orientation in AI prompt Given the user requests a projector facing "upwards" When the AI validates the request Then it should respond with a validation error indicating invalid orientation Scenario: Missing required dimensions Given the user requests a projector but doesn't specify width, height, or depth When the AI attempts to construct the DTO Then it should return a validation message asking for the missing dimension data Scenario: AI fails to resolve learning space context Given the user says "Add a projector to the room" When the AI cannot infer which learning space "room" refers to Then it should prompt the user to clarify the learning space ID ``` --- ### **Non-functional Requirements** | Category | Description | |---------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | **Robustness** | Invalid or null inputs return meaningful messages from the AI agent. | | **Integration** | The AI calls directly into the existing handler without duplicating logic. | | **Reusability** | The AI reuses application layer services (`LearningComponentServices`). | | **Maintainability** | Future learning component types remain supported without changing the AI. | --- ### **Technical Tasks** | Task | Minutes | |----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------| | Integrate the server for the AI | 20 | | Created the Tool method related to the endpoint | 20 | | Final testing and code review | 20 | --- ### **Total Time** | **Total Minutes** | **Total Hours** | |-------------------|----------------------| | 60 minutes | 1 hours 0 minutes | --- ## Performed Tests ### Test 1 – The user gives valid characteristics to add a projector. ### Test 2 – The user gives an invalid orientation. ### Test 3 – The user doesn't specify dimensions for the projector. ### Test 4 - The user add learning space context that the ai agent can't resolve. ---

title: CPD-T-006-001 Create unit and integration tests in frontend and backend
author: elenig10
assignees: SebasArceCRUCR, Fett-Ron, angevarart, luis-fch, Earaya12, elenig10
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-T-006-001 - **Name**: Protect core functionality through testing to ensure long-term stability  - **Description**: This epic aims to create automated tests that continuously verify the system’s behavior. This investment in testing helps catch bugs before they reach users and reduces the risk of regressions and downtime in future releases. By adding tests to the project, it assures the users a secure and more confident system that ensures long-term stability.  ---  ## User Story   ```gherkin Feature: System reliability through automated testing  As a product owner,   I want the system to have automated tests that protect key functionality   So that I can trust each release won't break existing features and can be delivered faster and more confidently. ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: Validating component behavior through backend unit tests Given core backend services and entities   When unit tests are executed   Then they should confirm that each method behaves correctly with both valid and invalid inputs  Scenario: Ensuring integration with the database through infrastructure-level tests Given a working database connection and EF Core repositories   When integration tests run   Then they should validate that data is saved, retrieved, and deleted correctly  Scenario: Preventing frontend regressions through unit tests Given component logic and forms in the frontend   When unit tests are triggered   Then they should verify correct rendering, validation, and state transitions ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category           | Description                                                                 | |--------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Reliability         | Core functionality is protected by repeatable, automated test coverage     | | Maintainability     | New features can be added with confidence due to existing test base        | | Regression Safety   | Future changes are validated against current business logic automatically  | | Performance         | Test suites should execute in a reasonable time (<30s per test run)        | | Documentation       | Test code serves as living documentation for expected behaviors            |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                                                 | Time (min) | |--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------| | Write backend unit tests for services and entities   | 90         | | Write backend integration tests for repository and infrastructure layer              | 100        | | Write frontend unit tests for Blazor components (e.g., forms, dropdown logic)        | 80         | | Configure test fixtures, mocks, and in-memory databases                              | 40         | | Review and debug failed test cases                                                   | 30         | | Add test coverage metrics and CI integration                                         | 30         | | Validate test results                                   | 30         |   ---  ### **Total Time**  | Total Minutes | Total Hours     | |---------------|-----------------| | 400 minutes   | 6 hours 40 mins |  ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1 –  **Result**:  --

title: [SPT-CPD-SQL-PQL]-DP-001-001 Deploy application
author: andresMurillo23
assignees: ebai03, andresMurillo23, luis-fch, Earaya12, RolandoVillavicencio013, AleJimenezRJ
body: ### **EPIC ID:** SPT-DP-001  **Name:** Deployment and Hosting **Description:** * To ensure that ThemePark\@UCR is accessible in production or staging environments, the system must support deployment on Microsoft Internet Information Services (IIS). This includes the configuration of the hosting environment, building the publish artifacts, and ensuring proper server settings for secure and reliable hosting.  ---   ### **User Story**  ```gherkin Feature: Deploy application  As a developer or system administrator   I want to deploy the ASP.NET Core web application   So that it is accessible on IIS securely and reliably ```  ---  ### **Acceptance Criteria**  ```gherkin Scenario: Application is deployed and runs on IIS Given the application is ready for deployment   When I publish and move it to the IIS site folder   Then the website should be accessible from the configured domain or IP  Scenario: Required files are present Given the deployment is manual   When the app is copied to the server   Then all necessary configuration files must be included  Scenario: IIS is set up correctly Given the app is deployed   When IIS is configured with proper settings   Then the site must run and use HTTPS ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                                                              | | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | **Availability**    | The application must auto-recover on restart or crash.                   | | **Security**        | Must enforce HTTPS and proper hosting settings.                          | | **Performance**     | The hosted version should respond under 1 second in a local environment. | | **Maintainability** | Must use standard publish profiles and scripts for repeatability.        |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task                                                                                 | Minutes | | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------- | | Create and configure publish profile in Visual Studio (Release mode, target folder). | 10      | | Create IIS site and set ports (HTTP 80, HTTPS 443 or custom).                        | 15      | | Set Application Pool: No Managed Code, AlwaysRunning, start automatically.           | 10 | | Add and configure `web.config` (hosting bundle, HTTPS redirect, request limits).     | 10      | | Check ASP.NET Core Hosting Bundle is installed on the server.                        | 5       | | Open firewall ports for HTTP and HTTPS traffic.                                      | 10      | | Test deployment: access via browser (localhost and external URL), check IIS logs.    | 15      | | Document all steps: publish profile, IIS config, cert, tunnel, firewall.             | 15      | | Validate app auto-restart after app pool recycle or IIS restart.                     | 5       | | Review and merge PR if deployment scripts are used.                                  | 5       |  ---  **Total estimate:** **125 minutes (\~2h 5m)**

title: CPD-AI-005-006 AI query that returns all components created
author: Fett-Ron
body: ### EPIC ID: CPD-AI-005 - **Name**: AI Integration for Learning Components - **Description**: This epic focuses on enhancing the Theme Park backend by integrating an AI agent capable of interpreting user queries and retrieving learning component data. The goal is to create natural language interaction capabilities powered by existing handler and service layers. ---  ## User Story   ```gherkin Feature: AI query that returns all components created  As a user of the AI agent,   I want the agent to get a list of all learning components So that I can get a list of learning components using natural language. ```  ---  ### Acceptance Criteria ```gherkin Scenario: AI agent gets the list of components Given a user When the user requests the AI agent to get the list of all the learning components Then the AI should call the `GetLearningComponentAsync` service and succesfully return a list of projectors ```  ```gherkin Scenario: AI agent handles a invalid request Given an user When the user ask the AI agent to get a list of all learning components and it fails Then the AI agent should return a friendly error message ```  ---  ### **Non-functional Requirements**  | Category            | Description                   | |---------------------|-------------------------------| | **Robustness**      | Invalid or null inputs return meaningful messages from the AI agent.        | | **Integration**     | The AI calls directly into the existing handler without duplicating logic.  | | **Reusability**     | The AI reuses application layer services (`GetLearningComponentAsync`).     | | **Maintainability** | Future learning component types remain supported without changing the AI.   |  ---  ### **Technical Tasks**  | Task      | Minutes | |------------------|---------| | Integrate the server for the AI       | 5     | | Created the Tool method related to the endpoint          | 20      | | Final testing and code review               | 10      |  ---  **Total minutes**: 35 ---  ## Performed Tests  ### Test 1: When the user asks for all the learning components.  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ba6e7709-c185-40af-9113-5152ceff862a)

title: Enable User Groups Functionality
author: pablodevrzmz
body: ### User Story  As a system administrator I want to create User Groups that contain a predefined set of Roles, and have users automatically inherit those roles—while allowing each inherited role to be activated or deactivated per user So that I can efficiently manage consistent access controls across multiple users while still supporting individual exceptions.  ### Acceptance Criteria  - I can create, edit, and deactivate a User Group. - I can assign or remove Roles from a User Group. - When I add a User to a User Group, the user automatically inherits the group’s roles. - The inherited roles must support a per-user Active/Inactive toggle. - A user’s effective permissions must reflect only the active roles, whether assigned directly or inherited from a User Group. - Removing a User from a User Group removes all role inheritance derived from that group.
